[]string `json:\"warnings,omitempty\"`\n191: \tInfos     []string `json:\"infos,omitempty\"`\n192: }\n193: \n194: type apiFuncResult struct {\n195: \tdata      any\n196: \terr       *apiError\n197: \twarnings  annotations.Annotations\n198: \tfinalizer func()\n199: }\n200: \n201: type apiFunc func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult\n202: \n203: // TSDBAdminStats defines the tsdb interfaces used by the v1 API for admin operations as well as statistics.\n204: type TSDBAdminStats interface {\n205: \tCleanTombstones() error\n206: \tDelete(ctx context.Context, mint, maxt int64, ms ...*labels.Matcher) error\n207: \tSnapshot(dir string, withHead bool) error\n208: \tStats(statsByLabelName string, limit int) (*tsdb.Stats, error)\n209: \tWALReplayStatus() (tsdb.WALReplayStatus, error)\n210: \tBlockMetas() ([]tsdb.BlockMeta, error)\n211: }\n212: \n213: type QueryOpts interface {\n214: \tEnablePerStepStats() bool\n215: \tLookbackDelta() time.Duration\n216: }\n217: \n218: // API can register a set of endpoints in a router and handle\n219: // them using the provided storage and query engine.\n220: type API struct {\n221: \tQueryable         storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable\n222: \tQueryEngine       promql.QueryEngine\n223: \tExemplarQueryable storage.ExemplarQueryable\n224: \n225: \tscrapePoolsRetriever  func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever\n226: \ttargetRetriever       func(context.Context) TargetRetriever\n227: \talertmanagerRetriever func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever\n228: \trulesRetriever        func(context.Context) RulesRetriever\n229: \tnow                   func() time.Time\n230: \tconfig                func() config.Config\n231: \tflagsMap              map[string]string\n232: \tready                 func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc\n233: \tglobalURLOptions      GlobalURLOptions\n234: \n235: \tdb                  TSDBAdminStats\n236: \tdbDir               string\n237: \tenableAdmin         bool\n238: \tlogger              *slog.Logger\n239: \tCORSOrigin          *regexp.Regexp\n240: \tbuildInfo           *PrometheusVersion\n241: \truntimeInfo         func() (RuntimeInfo, error)\n242: \tgatherer            prometheus.Gatherer\n243: \tisAgent             bool\n244: \tstatsRenderer       StatsRenderer\n245: \tnotificationsGetter func() []notifications.Notification\n246: \tnotificationsSub    func() (<-chan notifications.Notification, func(), bool)\n247: \t// Allows customizing the default mapping\n248: \toverrideErrorCode OverrideErrorCode\n249: \n250: \tremoteWriteHandler http.Handler\n251: \tremoteReadHandler  http.Handler\n252: \totlpWriteHandler   http.Handler\n253: \n254: \tcodecs []Codec\n255: }\n256: \n257: // NewAPI returns an initialized API type.\n258: func NewAPI(\n259: \tqe promql.QueryEngine,\n260: \tq storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable,\n261: \tap storage.Appendable,\n262: \teq storage.ExemplarQueryable,\n263: \tspsr func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever,\n264: \ttr func(context.Context) TargetRetriever,\n265: \tar func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever,\n266: \tconfigFunc func() config.Config,\n267: \tflagsMap map[string]string,\n268: \tglobalURLOptions GlobalURLOptions,\n269: \treadyFunc func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc,\n270: \tdb TSDBAdminStats,\n271: \tdbDir string,\n272: \tenableAdmin bool,\n273: \tlogger *slog.Logger,\n274: \trr func(context.Context) RulesRetriever,\n275: \tremoteReadSampleLimit int,\n276: \tremoteReadConcurrencyLimit int,\n277: \tremoteReadMaxBytesInFrame int,\n278: \tisAgent bool,\n279: \tcorsOrigin *regexp.Regexp,\n280: \truntimeInfo func() (RuntimeInfo, error),\n281: \tbuildInfo *PrometheusVersion,\n282: \tnotificationsGetter func() []notifications.Notification,\n283: \tnotificationsSub func() (<-chan notifications.Notification, func(), bool),\n284: \tgatherer prometheus.Gatherer,\n285: \tregisterer prometheus.Registerer,\n286: \tstatsRenderer StatsRenderer,\n287: \trwEnabled bool,\n288: \tacceptRemoteWriteProtoMsgs []config.RemoteWriteProtoMsg,\n289: \totlpEnabled, otlpDeltaToCumulative, otlpNativeDeltaIngestion bool,\n290: \tctZeroIngestionEnabled bool,\n291: \tlookbackDelta time.Duration,\n292: \tenableTypeAndUnitLabels bool,\n293: \toverrideErrorCode OverrideErrorCode,\n294: ) *API {\n295: \ta := &API{\n296: \t\tQueryEngine:       qe,\n297: \t\tQueryable:         q,\n298: \t\tExemplarQueryable: eq,\n299: \n300: \t\tscrapePoolsRetriever:  spsr,\n301: \t\ttargetRetriever:       tr,\n302: \t\talertmanagerRetriever: ar,\n303: \n304: \t\tnow:                 time.Now,\n305: \t\tconfig:              configFunc,\n306: \t\tflagsMap:            flagsMap,\n307: \t\tready:               readyFunc,\n308: \t\tglobalURLOptions:    globalURLOptions,\n309: \t\tdb:                  db,\n310: \t\tdbDir:               dbDir,\n311: \t\tenableAdmin:         enableAdmin,\n312: \t\trulesRetriever:      rr,\n313: \t\tlogger:              logger,\n314: \t\tCORSOrigin:          corsOrigin,\n315: \t\truntimeInfo:         runtimeInfo,\n316: \t\tbuildInfo:           buildInfo,\n317: \t\tgatherer:            gatherer,\n318: \t\tisAgent:             isAgent,\n319: \t\tstatsRenderer:       DefaultStatsRenderer,\n320: \t\tnotificationsGetter: notificationsGetter,\n321: \t\tnotificationsSub:    notificationsSub,\n322: \t\toverrideErrorCode:   overrideErrorCode,\n323: \n324: \t\tremoteReadHandler: remote.NewReadHandler(logger, registerer, q, configFunc, remoteReadSampleLimit, remoteReadConcurrencyLimit, remoteReadMaxBytesInFrame),\n325: \t}\n326: \n327: \ta.InstallCodec(JSONCodec{})\n328: \n329: \tif statsRenderer != nil {\n330: \t\ta.statsRenderer = statsRenderer\n331: \t}\n332: \n333: \tif ap == nil && (rwEnabled || otlpEnabled) {\n334: \t\tpanic(\"remote write or otlp write enabled, but no appender passed in.\")\n335: \t}\n336: \n337: \tif rwEnabled {\n338: \t\ta.remoteWriteHandler = remote.NewWriteHandler(logger, registerer, ap, acceptRemoteWriteProtoMsgs, ctZeroIngestionEnabled)\n339: \t}\n340: \tif otlpEnabled {\n341: \t\ta.otlpWriteHandler = remote.NewOTLPWriteHandler(logger, registerer, ap, configFunc, remote.OTLPOptions{\n342: \t\t\tConvertDelta:            otlpDeltaToCumulative,\n343: \t\t\tNativeDelta:             otlpNativeDeltaIngestion,\n344: \t\t\tLookbackDelta:           lookbackDelta,\n345: \t\t\tEnableTypeAndUnitLabels: enableTypeAndUnitLabels,\n346: \t\t})\n347: \t}\n348: \n349: \treturn a\n350: }\n351: \n352: // InstallCodec adds codec to this API's available codecs.\n353: // Codecs installed first take precedence over codecs installed later when evaluating wildcards in Accept headers.\n354: // The first installed codec is used as a fallback when the Accept header cannot be satisfied or if there is no Accept header.\n355: func (api *API) InstallCodec(codec Codec) {\n356: \tapi.codecs = append(api.codecs, codec)\n357: }\n358: \n359: // ClearCodecs removes all available codecs from this API, including the default codec installed by NewAPI.\n360: func (api *API) ClearCodecs() {\n361: \tapi.codecs = nil\n362: }\n363: \n364: func setUnavailStatusOnTSDBNotReady(r apiFuncResult) apiFuncResult {\n365: \tif r.err != nil && errors.Is(r.err.err, tsdb.ErrNotReady) {\n366: \t\tr.err.typ = errorUnavailable\n367: \t}\n368: \treturn r\n369: }\n370: \n371: // Register the API's endpoints in the given router.\n372: func (api *API) Register(r *route.Router) {\n373: \twrap := func(f apiFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\n374: \t\thf := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n375: \t\t\thttputil.SetCORS(w, api.CORSOrigin, r)\n376: \t\t\tresult := setUnavailStatusOnTSDBNotReady(f(r))\n377: \t\t\tif result.finalizer != nil {\n378: \t\t\t\tdefer result.finalizer()\n379: \t\t\t}\n380: \t\t\tif result.err != nil {\n381: \t\t\t\tapi.respondError(w, result.err, result.data)\n382: \t\t\t\treturn\n383: \t\t\t}\n384: \n385: \t\t\tif result.data != nil {\n386: \t\t\t\tapi.respond(w, r, result.data, result.warnings, r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n387: \t\t\t\treturn\n388: \t\t\t}\n389: \t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n390: \t\t})\n391: \t\treturn api.ready(httputil.CompressionHandler{\n392: \t\t\tHandler: hf,\n393: \t\t}.ServeHTTP)\n394: \t}\n395: \n396: \twrapAgent := func(f apiFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\n397: \t\treturn wrap(func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n398: \t\t\tif api.isAgent {\n399: \t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, errors.New(\"unavailable with Prometheus Agent\")}, nil, nil}\n400: \t\t\t}\n401: \t\t\treturn f(r)\n402: \t\t})\n403: \t}\n404: \n405: \tr.Options(\"/*path\", wrap(api.options))\n406: \n407: \tr.Get(\"/query\", wrapAgent(api.query))\n408: \tr.Post(\"/query\", wrapAgent(api.query))\n409: \tr.Get(\"/query_range\", wrapAgent(api.queryRange))\n410: \tr.Post(\"/query_range\", wrapAgent(api.queryRange))\n411: \tr.Get(\"/query_exemplars\", wrapAgent(api.queryExemplars))\n412: \tr.Post(\"/query_exemplars\", wrapAgent(api.queryExemplars))\n413: \n414: \tr.Get(\"/format_query\", wrapAgent(api.formatQuery))\n415: \tr.Post(\"/format_query\", wrapAgent(api.formatQuery))\n416: \n417: \tr.Get(\"/parse_query\", wrapAgent(api.parseQuery))\n418: \tr.Post(\"/parse_query\", wrapAgent(api.parseQuery))\n419: \n420: \tr.Get(\"/labels\", wrapAgent(api.labelNames))\n421: \tr.Post(\"/labels\", wrapAgent(api.labelNames))\n422: \tr.Get(\"/label/:name/values\", wrapAgent(api.labelValues))\n423: \n424: \tr.Get(\"/series\", wrapAgent(api.series))\n425: \tr.Post(\"/series\", wrapAgent(api.series))\n426: \tr.Del(\"/series\", wrapAgent(api.dropSeries))\n427: \n428: \tr.Get(\"/scrape_pools\", wrap(api.scrapePools))\n429: \tr.Get(\"/targets\", wrap(api.targets))\n430: \tr.Get(\"/targets/metadata\", wrap(api.targetMetadata))\n431: \tr.Get(\"/alertmanagers\", wrapAgent(api.alertmanagers))\n432: \n433: \tr.Get(\"/metadata\", wrap(api.metricMetadata))\n434: \n435: \tr.Get(\"/status/config\", wrap(api.serveConfig))\n436: \tr.Get(\"/status/runtimeinfo\", wrap(api.serveRuntimeInfo))\n437: \tr.Get(\"/status/buildinfo\", wrap(api.serveBuildInfo))\n438: \tr.Get(\"/status/flags\", wrap(api.serveFlags))\n439: \tr.Get(\"/status/tsdb\", wrapAgent(api.serveTSDBStatus))\n440: \tr.Get(\"/status/tsdb/blocks\", wrapAgent(api.serveTSDBBlocks))\n441: \tr.Get(\"/status/walreplay\", api.serveWALReplayStatus)\n442: \tr.Get(\"/notifications\", api.notifications)\n443: \tr.Get(\"/notifications/live\", api.notificationsSSE)\n444: \tr.Post(\"/read\", api.ready(api.remoteRead))\n445: \tr.Post(\"/write\", api.ready(api.remoteWrite))\n446: \tr.Post(\"/otlp/v1/metrics\", api.ready(api.otlpWrite))\n447: \n448: \tr.Get(\"/alerts\", wrapAgent(api.alerts))\n449: \tr.Get(\"/rules\", wrapAgent(api.rules))\n450: \n451: \t// Admin APIs\n452: \tr.Post(\"/admin/tsdb/delete_series\", wrapAgent(api.deleteSeries))\n453: \tr.Post(\"/admin/tsdb/clean_tombstones\", wrapAgent(api.cleanTombstones))\n454: \tr.Post(\"/admin/tsdb/snapshot\", wrapAgent(api.snapshot))\n455: \n456: \tr.Put(\"/admin/tsdb/delete_series\", wrapAgent(api.deleteSeries))\n457: \tr.Put(\"/admin/tsdb/clean_tombstones\", wrapAgent(api.cleanTombstones))\n458: \tr.Put(\"/admin/tsdb/snapshot\", wrapAgent(api.snapshot))\n459: }\n460: \n461: type QueryData struct {\n462: \tResultType parser.ValueType `json:\"resultType\"`\n463: \tResult     parser.Value     `json:\"result\"`\n464: \tStats      stats.QueryStats `json:\"stats,omitempty\"`\n465: }\n466: \n467: func invalidParamError(err error, parameter string) apiFuncResult {\n468: \treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{\n469: \t\terrorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid parameter %q: %w\", parameter, err),\n470: \t}, nil, nil}\n471: }\n472: \n473: func (*API) options(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n474: \treturn apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\n475: }\n476: \n477: func (api *API) query(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n478: \tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n479: \tif err != nil {\n480: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n481: \t}\n482: \tts, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"time\", api.now())\n483: \tif err != nil {\n484: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"time\")\n485: \t}\n486: \tctx := r.Context()\n487: \tif to := r.FormValue(\"timeout\"); to != \"\" {\n488: \t\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\n489: \t\ttimeout, err := parseDuration(to)\n490: \t\tif err != nil {\n491: \t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"timeout\")\n492: \t\t}\n493: \n494: \t\tctx, cancel = context.WithDeadline(ctx, api.now().Add(timeout))\n495: \t\tdefer cancel()\n496: \t}\n497: \n498: \topts, err := extractQueryOpts(r)\n499: \tif err != nil {\n500: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n501: \t}\n502: \tqry, err := api.QueryEngine.NewInstantQuery(ctx, api.Queryable, opts, r.FormValue(\"query\"), ts)\n503: \tif err != nil {\n504: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n505: \t}\n506: \n507: \t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n508: \t// be called by the caller) or call qry.Close ourselves (which is\n509: \t// required in the case of a panic).\n510: \tdefer func() {\n511: \t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n512: \t\t\tqry.Close()\n513: \t\t}\n514: \t}()\n515: \n516: \tctx = httputil.ContextFromRequest(ctx, r)\n517: \n518: \tres := qry.Exec(ctx)\n519: \tif res.Err != nil {\n520: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(res.Err), res.Warnings, qry.Close}\n521: \t}\n522: \n523: \twarnings := res.Warnings\n524: \tif limit > 0 {\n525: \t\tvar isTruncated bool\n526: \n527: \t\tres, isTruncated = truncateResults(res, limit)\n528: \t\tif isTruncated {\n529: \t\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n530: \t\t}\n531: \t}\n532: \t// Optional stats field in response if parameter \"stats\" is not empty.\n533: \tsr := api.statsRenderer\n534: \tif sr == nil {\n535: \t\tsr = DefaultStatsRenderer\n536: \t}\n537: \tqs := sr(ctx, qry.Stats(), r.FormValue(\"stats\"))\n538: \n539: \treturn apiFuncResult{&QueryData{\n540: \t\tResultType: res.Value.Type(),\n541: \t\tResult:     res.Value,\n542: \t\tStats:      qs,\n543: \t}, nil, warnings, qry.Close}\n544: }\n545: \n546: func (*API) formatQuery(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n547: \texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n548: \tif err != nil {\n549: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n550: \t}\n551: \n552: \treturn apiFuncResult{expr.Pretty(0), nil, nil, nil}\n553: }\n554: \n555: func (*API) parseQuery(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n556: \texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n557: \tif err != nil {\n558: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n559: \t}\n560: \n561: \treturn apiFuncResult{data: translateAST(expr), err: nil, warnings: nil, finalizer: nil}\n562: }\n563: \n564: func extractQueryOpts(r *http.Request) (promql.QueryOpts, error) {\n565: \tvar duration time.Duration\n566: \n567: \tif strDuration := r.FormValue(\"lookback_delta\"); strDuration != \"\" {\n568: \t\tparsedDuration, err := parseDuration(strDuration)\n569: \t\tif err != nil {\n570: \t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing lookback delta duration: %w\", err)\n571: \t\t}\n572: \t\tduration = parsedDuration\n573: \t}\n574: \n575: \treturn promql.NewPrometheusQueryOpts(r.FormValue(\"stats\") == \"all\", duration), nil\n576: }\n577: \n578: func (api *API) queryRange(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n579: \tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n580: \tif err != nil {\n581: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n582: \t}\n583: \tstart, err := parseTime(r.FormValue(\"start\"))\n584: \tif err != nil {\n585: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n586: \t}\n587: \tend, err := parseTime(r.FormValue(\"end\"))\n588: \tif err != nil {\n589: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n590: \t}\n591: \tif end.Before(start) {\n592: \t\treturn invalidParamError(errors.New(\"end timestamp must not be before start time\"), \"end\")\n593: \t}\n594: \n595: \tstep, err := parseDuration(r.FormValue(\"step\"))\n596: \tif err != nil {\n597: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"step\")\n598: \t}\n599: \n600: \tif step <= 0 {\n601: \t\treturn invalidParamError(errors.New(\"zero or negative query resolution step widths are not accepted. Try a positive integer\"), \"step\")\n602: \t}\n603: \n604: \t// For safety, limit the number of returned points per timeseries.\n605: \t// This is sufficient for 60s resolution for a week or 1h resolution for a year.\n606: \tif end.Sub(start)/step > 11000 {\n607: \t\terr := errors.New(\"exceeded maximum resolution of 11,000 points per timeseries. Try decreasing the query resolution (?step=XX)\")\n608: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n609: \t}\n610: \n611: \tctx := r.Context()\n612: \tif to := r.FormValue(\"timeout\"); to != \"\" {\n613: \t\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\n614: \t\ttimeout, err := parseDuration(to)\n615: \t\tif err != nil {\n616: \t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"timeout\")\n617: \t\t}\n618: \n619: \t\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)\n620: \t\tdefer cancel()\n621: \t}\n622: \n623: \topts, err := extractQueryOpts(r)\n624: \tif err != nil {\n625: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n626: \t}\n627: \tqry, err := api.QueryEngine.NewRangeQuery(ctx, api.Queryable, opts, r.FormValue(\"query\"), start, end, step)\n628: \tif err != nil {\n629: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n630: \t}\n631: \t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n632: \t// be called by the caller) or call qry.Close ourselves (which is\n633: \t// required in the case of a panic).\n634: \tdefer func() {\n635: \t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n636: \t\t\tqry.Close()\n637: \t\t}\n638: \t}()\n639: \n640: \tctx = httputil.ContextFromRequest(ctx, r)\n641: \n642: \tres := qry.Exec(ctx)\n643: \tif res.Err != nil {\n644: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(res.Err), res.Warnings, qry.Close}\n645: \t}\n646: \n647: \twarnings := res.Warnings\n648: \tif limit > 0 {\n649: \t\tvar isTruncated bool\n650: \n651: \t\tres, isTruncated = truncateResults(res, limit)\n652: \t\tif isTruncated {\n653: \t\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n654: \t\t}\n655: \t}\n656: \n657: \t// Optional stats field in response if parameter \"stats\" is not empty.\n658: \tsr := api.statsRenderer\n659: \tif sr == nil {\n660: \t\tsr = DefaultStatsRenderer\n661: \t}\n662: \tqs := sr(ctx, qry.Stats(), r.FormValue(\"stats\"))\n663: \n664: \treturn apiFuncResult{&QueryData{\n665: \t\tResultType: res.Value.Type(),\n666: \t\tResult:     res.Value,\n667: \t\tStats:      qs,\n668: \t}, nil, warnings, qry.Close}\n669: }\n670: \n671: func (api *API) queryExemplars(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n672: \tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n673: \tif err != nil {\n674: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n675: \t}\n676: \tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n677: \tif err != nil {\n678: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n679: \t}\n680: \tif end.Before(start) {\n681: \t\terr := errors.New(\"end timestamp must not be before start timestamp\")\n682: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n683: \t}\n684: \n685: \texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n686: \tif err != nil {\n687: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n688: \t}\n689: \n690: \tselectors := parser.ExtractSelectors(expr)\n691: \tif len(selectors) < 1 {\n692: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\n693: \t}\n694: \n695: \tctx := r.Context()\n696: \teq, err := api.ExemplarQueryable.ExemplarQuerier(ctx)\n697: \tif err != nil {\n698: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n699: \t}\n700: \n701: \tres, err := eq.Select(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end), selectors...)\n702: \tif err != nil {\n703: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n704: \t}\n705: \n706: \treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n707: }\n708: \n709: func returnAPIError(err error) *apiError {\n710: \tif err == nil {\n711: \t\treturn nil\n712: \t}\n713: \n714: \tvar eqc promql.ErrQueryCanceled\n715: \tvar eqt promql.ErrQueryTimeout\n716: \tvar es promql.ErrStorage\n717: \tswitch {\n718: \tcase errors.As(err, &eqc):\n719: \t\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\n720: \tcase errors.As(err, &eqt):\n721: \t\treturn &apiError{errorTimeout, err}\n722: \tcase errors.As(err, &es):\n723: \t\treturn &apiError{errorInternal, err}\n724: \t}\n725: \n726: \tif errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {\n727: \t\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\n728: \t}\n729: \n730: \treturn &apiError{errorExec, err}\n731: }\n732: \n733: func (api *API) labelNames(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n734: \tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n735: \tif err != nil {\n736: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n737: \t}\n738: \n739: \tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n740: \tif err != nil {\n741: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n742: \t}\n743: \tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n744: \tif err != nil {\n745: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n746: \t}\n747: \n748: \tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\"match[]\"])\n749: \tif err != nil {\n750: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n751: \t}\n752: \n753: \thints := &storage.LabelHints{\n754: \t\tLimit: toHintLimit(limit),\n755: \t}\n756: \n757: \tq, err := api.Queryable.Querier(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end))\n758: \tif err != nil {\n759: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n760: \t}\n761: \tdefer q.Close()\n762: \n763: \tvar (\n764: \t\tnames    []string\n765: \t\twarnings annotations.Annotations\n766: \t)\n767: \tif len(matcherSets) > 1 {\n768: \t\tlabelNamesSet := make(map[string]struct{})\n769: \n770: \t\tfor _, matchers := range matcherSets {\n771: \t\t\tvals, callWarnings, err := q.LabelNames(r.Context(), hints, matchers...)\n772: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n773: \t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), warnings, nil}\n774: \t\t\t}\n775: \n776: \t\t\twarnings.Merge(callWarnings)\n777: \t\t\tfor _, val := range vals {\n778: \t\t\t\tlabelNamesSet[val] = struct{}{}\n779: \t\t\t}\n780: \t\t}\n781: \n782: \t\t// Convert the map to an array.\n783: \t\tnames = make([]string, 0, len(labelNamesSet))\n784: \t\tfor key := range labelNamesSet {\n785: \t\t\tnames = append(names, key)\n786: \t\t}\n787: \t\tslices.Sort(names)\n788: \t} else {\n789: \t\tvar matchers []*labels.Matcher\n790: \t\tif len(matcherSets) == 1 {\n791: \t\t\tmatchers = matcherSets[0]\n792: \t\t}\n793: \t\tnames, warnings, err = q.LabelNames(r.Context(), hints, matchers...)\n794: \t\tif err != nil {\n795: \t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, err}, warnings, nil}\n796: \t\t}\n797: \t}\n798: \n799: \tif names == nil {\n800: \t\tnames = []string{}\n801: \t}\n802: \n803: \tif limit > 0 && len(names) > limit {\n804: \t\tnames = names[:limit]\n805: \t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n806: \t}\n807: \treturn apiFuncResult{names, nil, warnings, nil}\n808: }\n809: \n810: func (api *API) labelValues(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n811: \tctx := r.Context()\n812: \tname := route.Param(ctx, \"name\")\n813: \n814: \tif strings.HasPrefix(name, \"U__\") {\n815: \t\tname = model.UnescapeName(name, model.ValueEncodingEscaping)\n816: \t}\n817: \n818: \tif !model.UTF8Validation.IsValidLabelName(name) {\n819: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid label name: %q\", name)}, nil, nil}\n820: \t}\n821: \n822: \tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n823: \tif err != nil {\n824: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n825: \t}\n826: \n827: \tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n828: \tif err != nil {\n829: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n830: \t}\n831: \tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n832: \tif err != nil {\n833: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n834: \t}\n835: \n836: \tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\"match[]\"])\n837: \tif err != nil {\n838: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n839: \t}\n840: \n841: \thints := &storage.LabelHints{\n842: \t\tLimit: toHintLimit(limit),\n843: \t}\n844: \n845: \tq, err := api.Queryable.Querier(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end))\n846: \tif err != nil {\n847: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, err}, nil, nil}\n848: \t}\n849: \t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n850: \t// be called by the caller) or call q.Close ourselves (which is required\n851: \t// in the case of a panic).\n852: \tdefer func() {\n853: \t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n854: \t\t\tq.Close()\n855: \t\t}\n856: \t}()\n857: \tcloser := func() {\n858: \t\tq.Close()\n859: \t}\n860: \n861: \tvar (\n862: \t\tvals     []string\n863: \t\twarnings annotations.Annotations\n864: \t)\n865: \tif len(matcherSets) > 1 {\n866: \t\tvar callWarnings annotations.Annotations\n867: \t\tlabelValuesSet := make(map[string]struct{})\n868: \t\tfor _, matchers := range matcherSets {\n869: \t\t\tvals, callWarnings, err = q.LabelValues(ctx, name, hints, matchers...)\n870: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n871: \t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, err}, warnings, closer}\n872: \t\t\t}\n873: \t\t\twarnings.Merge(callWarnings)\n874: \t\t\tfor _, val := range vals {\n875: \t\t\t\tlabelValuesSet[val] = struct{}{}\n876: \t\t\t}\n877: \t\t}\n878: \n879: \t\tvals = make([]string, 0, len(labelValuesSet))\n880: \t\tfor val := range labelValuesSet {\n881: \t\t\tvals = append(vals, val)\n882: \t\t}\n883: \t} else {\n884: \t\tvar matchers []*labels.Matcher\n885: \t\tif len(matcherSets) == 1 {\n886: \t\t\tmatchers = matcherSets[0]\n887: \t\t}\n888: \t\tvals, warnings, err = q.LabelValues(ctx, name, hints, matchers...)\n889: \t\tif err != nil {\n890: \t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, err}, warnings, closer}\n891: \t\t}\n892: \n893: \t\tif vals == nil {\n894: \t\t\tvals = []string{}\n895: \t\t}\n896: \t}\n897: \n898: \tslices.Sort(vals)\n899: \n900: \tif limit > 0 && len(vals) > limit {\n901: \t\tvals = vals[:limit]\n902: \t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n903: \t}\n904: \n905: \treturn apiFuncResult{vals, nil, warnings, closer}\n906: }\n907: \n908: var (\n909: \t// MinTime is the default timestamp used for the start of optional time ranges.\n910: \t// Exposed to let downstream projects reference it.\n911: \t//\n912: \t// Historical note: This should just be time.Unix(math.MinInt64/1000, 0).UTC(),\n913: \t// but it was set to a higher value in the past due to a misunderstanding.\n914: \t// The value is still low enough for practical purposes, so we don't want\n915: \t// to change it now, avoiding confusion for importers of this variable.\n916: \tMinTime = time.Unix(math.MinInt64/1000+62135596801, 0).UTC()\n917: \n918: \t// MaxTime is the default timestamp used for the end of optional time ranges.\n919: \t// Exposed to let downstream projects to reference it.\n920: \t//\n921: \t// Historical note: This should just be time.Unix(math.MaxInt64/1000, 0).UTC(),\n922: \t// but it was set to a lower value in the past due to a misunderstanding.\n923: \t// The value is still high enough for practical purposes, so we don't want\n924: \t// to change it now, avoiding confusion for importers of this variable.\n925: \tMaxTime = time.Unix(math.MaxInt64/1000-62135596801, 999999999).UTC()\n926: \n927: \tminTimeFormatted = MinTime.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n928: \tmaxTimeFormatted = MaxTime.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n929: )\n930: \n931: func (api *API) series(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n932: \tctx := r.Context()\n933: \n934: \tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n935: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n936: \t}\n937: \tif len(r.Form[\"match[]\"]) == 0 {\n938: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"no match[] parameter provided\")}, nil, nil}\n939: \t}\n940: \n941: \tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n942: \tif err != nil {\n943: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n944: \t}\n945: \n946: \tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n947: \tif err != nil {\n948: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n949: \t}\n950: \tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n951: \tif err != nil {\n952: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n953: \t}\n954: \n955: \tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\"match[]\"])\n956: \tif err != nil {\n957: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"match[]\")\n958: \t}\n959: \n960: \tq, err := api.Queryable.Querier(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end))\n961: \tif err != nil {\n962: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n963: \t}\n964: \t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n965: \t// be called by the caller) or call q.Close ourselves (which is required\n966: \t// in the case of a panic).\n967: \tdefer func() {\n968: \t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n969: \t\t\tq.Close()\n970: \t\t}\n971: \t}()\n972: \tcloser := func() {\n973: \t\tq.Close()\n974: \t}\n975: \n976: \thints := &storage.SelectHints{\n977: \t\tStart: timestamp.FromTime(start),\n978: \t\tEnd:   timestamp.FromTime(end),\n979: \t\tFunc:  \"series\", // There is no series function, this token is used for lookups that don't need samples.\n980: \t\tLimit: toHintLimit(limit),\n981: \t}\n982: \tvar set storage.SeriesSet\n983: \n984: \tif len(matcherSets) > 1 {\n985: \t\tvar sets []storage.SeriesSet\n986: \t\tfor _, mset := range matcherSets {\n987: \t\t\t// We need to sort this select results to merge (deduplicate) the series sets later.\n988: \t\t\ts := q.Select(ctx, true, hints, mset...)\n989: \t\t\tsets = append(sets, s)\n990: \t\t}\n991: \t\tset = storage.NewMergeSeriesSet(sets, 0, storage.ChainedSeriesMerge)\n992: \t} else {\n993: \t\t// At this point at least one match exists.\n994: \t\tset = q.Select(ctx, false, hints, matcherSets[0]...)\n995: \t}\n996: \n997: \tmetrics := []labels.Labels{}\n998: \n999: \twarnings := set.Warnings()\n1000: \n1001: \ti := 1\n1002: \tfor set.Next() {\n1003: \t\tif i%checkContextEveryNIterations == 0 {\n1004: \t\t\tif err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {\n1005: \t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), warnings, closer}\n1006: \t\t\t}\n1007: \t\t}\n1008: \t\ti++\n1009: \n1010: \t\tmetrics = append(metrics, set.At().Labels())\n1011: \n1012: \t\tif limit > 0 && len(metrics) > limit {\n1013: \t\t\tmetrics = metrics[:limit]\n1014: \t\t\twarnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n1015: \t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{metrics, nil, warnings, closer}\n1016: \t\t}\n1017: \t}\n1018: \tif set.Err() != nil {\n1019: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(set.Err()), warnings, closer}\n1020: \t}\n1021: \n1022: \treturn apiFuncResult{metrics, nil, warnings, closer}\n1023: }\n1024: \n1025: func (*API) dropSeries(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1026: \treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, errors.New(\"not implemented\")}, nil, nil}\n1027: }\n1028: \n1029: // Target has the information for one target.\n1030: type Target struct {\n1031: \t// Labels before any processing.\n1032: \tDiscoveredLabels labels.Labels `json:\"discoveredLabels\"`\n1033: \t// Any labels that are added to this target and its metrics.\n1034: \tLabels labels.Labels `json:\"labels\"`\n1035: \n1036: \tScrapePool string `json:\"scrapePool\"`\n1037: \tScrapeURL  string `json:\"scrapeUrl\"`\n1038: \tGlobalURL  string `json:\"globalUrl\"`\n1039: \n1040: \tLastError          string              `json:\"lastError\"`\n1041: \tLastScrape         time.Time           `json:\"lastScrape\"`\n1042: \tLastScrapeDuration float64             `json:\"lastScrapeDuration\"`\n1043: \tHealth             scrape.TargetHealth `json:\"health\"`\n1044: \n1045: \tScrapeInterval string `json:\"scrapeInterval\"`\n1046: \tScrapeTimeout  string `json:\"scrapeTimeout\"`\n1047: }\n1048: \n1049: type ScrapePoolsDiscovery struct {\n1050: \tScrapePools []string `json:\"scrapePools\"`\n1051: }\n1052: \n1053: // DroppedTarget has the information for one target that was dropped during relabelling.\n1054: type DroppedTarget struct {\n1055: \t// Labels before any processing.\n1056: \tDiscoveredLabels labels.Labels `json:\"discoveredLabels\"`\n1057: \tScrapePool       string        `json:\"scrapePool\"`\n1058: }\n1059: \n1060: // TargetDiscovery has all the active targets.\n1061: type TargetDiscovery struct {\n1062: \tActiveTargets       []*Target        `json:\"activeTargets\"`\n1063: \tDroppedTargets      []*DroppedTarget `json:\"droppedTargets\"`\n1064: \tDroppedTargetCounts map[string]int   `json:\"droppedTargetCounts\"`\n1065: }\n1066: \n1067: // GlobalURLOptions contains fields used for deriving the global URL for local targets.\n1068: type GlobalURLOptions struct {\n1069: \tListenAddress string\n1070: \tHost          string\n1071: \tScheme        string\n1072: }\n1073: \n1074: // sanitizeSplitHostPort acts like net.SplitHostPort.\n1075: // Additionally, if there is no port in the host passed as input, we return the\n1076: // original host, making sure that IPv6 addresses are not surrounded by square\n1077: // brackets.\n1078: func sanitizeSplitHostPort(input string) (string, string, error) {\n1079: \thost, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(input)\n1080: \tif err != nil && strings.HasSuffix(err.Error(), \"missing port in address\") {\n1081: \t\tvar errWithPort error\n1082: \t\thost, _, errWithPort = net.SplitHostPort(input + \":80\")\n1083: \t\tif errWithPort == nil {\n1084: \t\t\terr = nil\n1085: \t\t}\n1086: \t}\n1087: \treturn host, port, err\n1088: }\n1089: \n1090: func getGlobalURL(u *url.URL, opts GlobalURLOptions) (*url.URL, error) {\n1091: \thost, port, err := sanitizeSplitHostPort(u.Host)\n1092: \tif err != nil {\n1093: \t\treturn u, err\n1094: \t}\n1095: \n1096: \tfor _, lhr := range LocalhostRepresentations {\n1097: \t\tif host == lhr {\n1098: \t\t\t_, ownPort, err := net.SplitHostPort(opts.ListenAddress)\n1099: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n1100: \t\t\t\treturn u, err\n1101: \t\t\t}\n1102: \n1103: \t\t\tif port == ownPort {\n1104: \t\t\t\t// Only in the case where the target is on localhost and its port is\n1105: \t\t\t\t// the same as the one we're listening on, we know for sure that\n1106: \t\t\t\t// we're monitoring our own process and that we need to change the\n1107: \t\t\t\t// scheme, hostname, and port to the externally reachable ones as\n1108: \t\t\t\t// well. We shouldn't need to touch the path at all, since if a\n1109: \t\t\t\t// path prefix is defined, the path under which we scrape ourselves\n1110: \t\t\t\t// should already contain the prefix.\n1111: \t\t\t\tu.Scheme = opts.Scheme\n1112: \t\t\t\tu.Host = opts.Host\n1113: \t\t\t} else {\n1114: \t\t\t\t// Otherwise, we only know that localhost is not reachable\n1115: \t\t\t\t// externally, so we replace only the hostname by the one in the\n1116: \t\t\t\t// external URL. It could be the wrong hostname for the service on\n1117: \t\t\t\t// this port, but it's still the best possible guess.\n1118: \t\t\t\thost, _, err := sanitizeSplitHostPort(opts.Host)\n1119: \t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1120: \t\t\t\t\treturn u, err\n1121: \t\t\t\t}\n1122: \t\t\t\tu.Host = host\n1123: \t\t\t\tif port != \"\" {\n1124: \t\t\t\t\tu.Host = net.JoinHostPort(u.Host, port)\n1125: \t\t\t\t}\n1126: \t\t\t}\n1127: \t\t\tbreak\n1128: \t\t}\n1129: \t}\n1130: \n1131: \treturn u, nil\n1132: }\n1133: \n1134: func (api *API) scrapePools(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1135: \tnames := api.scrapePoolsRetriever(r.Context()).ScrapePools()\n1136: \tsort.Strings(names)\n1137: \tres := &ScrapePoolsDiscovery{ScrapePools: names}\n1138: \treturn apiFuncResult{data: res, err: nil, warnings: nil, finalizer: nil}\n1139: }\n1140: \n1141: func (api *API) targets(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1142: \tgetSortedPools := func(targets map[string][]*scrape.Target) ([]string, int) {\n1143: \t\tvar n int\n1144: \t\tpools := make([]string, 0, len(targets))\n1145: \t\tfor p, t := range targets {\n1146: \t\t\tpools = append(pools, p)\n1147: \t\t\tn += len(t)\n1148: \t\t}\n1149: \t\tslices.Sort(pools)\n1150: \t\treturn pools, n\n1151: \t}\n1152: \n1153: \tscrapePool := r.URL.Query().Get(\"scrapePool\")\n1154: \tstate := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"state\"))\n1155: \tshowActive := state == \"\" || state == \"any\" || state == \"active\"\n1156: \tshowDropped := state == \"\" || state == \"any\" || state == \"dropped\"\n1157: \tres := &TargetDiscovery{}\n1158: \tbuilder := labels.NewBuilder(labels.EmptyLabels())\n1159: \n1160: \tif showActive {\n1161: \t\ttargetsActive := api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsActive()\n1162: \t\tactivePools, numTargets := getSortedPools(targetsActive)\n1163: \t\tres.ActiveTargets = make([]*Target, 0, numTargets)\n1164: \n1165: \t\tfor _, pool := range activePools {\n1166: \t\t\tif scrapePool != \"\" && pool != scrapePool {\n1167: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1168: \t\t\t}\n1169: \t\t\tfor _, target := range targetsActive[pool] {\n1170: \t\t\t\tlastErrStr := \"\"\n1171: \t\t\t\tlastErr := target.LastError()\n1172: \t\t\t\tif lastErr != nil {\n1173: \t\t\t\t\tlastErrStr = lastErr.Error()\n1174: \t\t\t\t}\n1175: \n1176: \t\t\t\tglobalURL, err := getGlobalURL(target.URL(), api.globalURLOptions)\n1177: \n1178: \t\t\t\tres.ActiveTargets = append(res.ActiveTargets, &Target{\n1179: \t\t\t\t\tDiscoveredLabels: target.DiscoveredLabels(builder),\n1180: \t\t\t\t\tLabels:           target.Labels(builder),\n1181: \t\t\t\t\tScrapePool:       pool,\n1182: \t\t\t\t\tScrapeURL:        target.URL().String(),\n1183: \t\t\t\t\tGlobalURL:        globalURL.String(),\n1184: \t\t\t\t\tLastError: func() string {\n1185: \t\t\t\t\t\tswitch {\n1186: \t\t\t\t\t\tcase err == nil && lastErrStr == \"\":\n1187: \t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1188: \t\t\t\t\t\tcase err != nil:\n1189: \t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s: %w\", lastErrStr, err).Error()\n1190: \t\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n1191: \t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn lastErrStr\n1192: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1193: \t\t\t\t\t}(),\n1194: \t\t\t\t\tLastScrape:         target.LastScrape(),\n1195: \t\t\t\t\tLastScrapeDuration: target.LastScrapeDuration().Seconds(),\n1196: \t\t\t\t\tHealth:             target.Health(),\n1197: \t\t\t\t\tScrapeInterval:     target.GetValue(model.ScrapeIntervalLabel),\n1198: \t\t\t\t\tScrapeTimeout:      target.GetValue(model.ScrapeTimeoutLabel),\n1199: \t\t\t\t})\n1200: \t\t\t}\n1201: \t\t}\n1202: \t} else {\n1203: \t\tres.ActiveTargets = []*Target{}\n1204: \t}\n1205: \tif showDropped {\n1206: \t\tres.DroppedTargetCounts = api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsDroppedCounts()\n1207: \n1208: \t\ttargetsDropped := api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsDropped()\n1209: \t\tdroppedPools, numTargets := getSortedPools(targetsDropped)\n1210: \t\tres.DroppedTargets = make([]*DroppedTarget, 0, numTargets)\n1211: \t\tfor _, pool := range droppedPools {\n1212: \t\t\tif scrapePool != \"\" && pool != scrapePool {\n1213: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1214: \t\t\t}\n1215: \t\t\tfor _, target := range targetsDropped[pool] {\n1216: \t\t\t\tres.DroppedTargets = append(res.DroppedTargets, &DroppedTarget{\n1217: \t\t\t\t\tDiscoveredLabels: target.DiscoveredLabels(builder),\n1218: \t\t\t\t\tScrapePool:       pool,\n1219: \t\t\t\t})\n1220: \t\t\t}\n1221: \t\t}\n1222: \t} else {\n1223: \t\tres.DroppedTargets = []*DroppedTarget{}\n1224: \t}\n1225: \treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n1226: }\n1227: \n1228: func matchLabels(lset labels.Labels, matchers []*labels.Matcher) bool {\n1229: \tfor _, m := range matchers {\n1230: \t\tif !m.Matches(lset.Get(m.Name)) {\n1231: \t\t\treturn false\n1232: \t\t}\n1233: \t}\n1234: \treturn true\n1235: }\n1236: \n1237: func (api *API) targetMetadata(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1238: \tlimit := -1\n1239: \tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit\"); s != \"\" {\n1240: \t\tvar err error\n1241: \t\tif limit, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil {\n1242: \t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit must be a number\")}, nil, nil}\n1243: \t\t}\n1244: \t}\n1245: \n1246: \tmatchTarget := r.FormValue(\"match_target\")\n1247: \tvar matchers []*labels.Matcher\n1248: \tvar err error\n1249: \tif matchTarget != \"\" {\n1250: \t\tmatchers, err = parser.ParseMetricSelector(matchTarget)\n1251: \t\tif err != nil {\n1252: \t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"match_target\")\n1253: \t\t}\n1254: \t}\n1255: \n1256: \tbuilder := labels.NewBuilder(labels.EmptyLabels())\n1257: \tmetric := r.FormValue(\"metric\")\n1258: \tres := []metricMetadata{}\n1259: \tfor _, tt := range api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsActive() {\n1260: \t\tfor _, t := range tt {\n1261: \t\t\tif limit >= 0 && len(res) >= limit {\n1262: \t\t\t\tbreak\n1263: \t\t\t}\n1264: \t\t\ttargetLabels := t.Labels(builder)\n1265: \t\t\t// Filter targets that don't satisfy the label matchers.\n1266: \t\t\tif matchTarget != \"\" && !matchLabels(targetLabels, matchers) {\n1267: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1268: \t\t\t}\n1269: \t\t\t// If no metric is specified, get the full list for the target.\n1270: \t\t\tif metric == \"\" {\n1271: \t\t\t\tfor _, md := range t.ListMetadata() {\n1272: \t\t\t\t\tres = append(res, metricMetadata{\n1273: \t\t\t\t\t\tTarget:       targetLabels,\n1274: \t\t\t\t\t\tMetricFamily: md.MetricFamily,\n1275: \t\t\t\t\t\tType:         md.Type,\n1276: \t\t\t\t\t\tHelp:         md.Help,\n1277: \t\t\t\t\t\tUnit:         md.Unit,\n1278: \t\t\t\t\t})\n1279: \t\t\t\t}\n1280: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1281: \t\t\t}\n1282: \t\t\t// Get metadata for the specified metric.\n1283: \t\t\tif md, ok := t.GetMetadata(metric); ok {\n1284: \t\t\t\tres = append(res, metricMetadata{\n1285: \t\t\t\t\tTarget: targetLabels,\n1286: \t\t\t\t\tType:   md.Type,\n1287: \t\t\t\t\tHelp:   md.Help,\n1288: \t\t\t\t\tUnit:   md.Unit,\n1289: \t\t\t\t})\n1290: \t\t\t}\n1291: \t\t}\n1292: \t}\n1293: \n1294: \treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n1295: }\n1296: \n1297: type metricMetadata struct {\n1298: \tTarget       labels.Labels    `json:\"target\"`\n1299: \tMetricFamily string           `json:\"metric,omitempty\"`\n1300: \tType         model.MetricType `json:\"type\"`\n1301: \tHelp         string           `json:\"help\"`\n1302: \tUnit         string           `json:\"unit\"`\n1303: }\n1304: \n1305: // AlertmanagerDiscovery has all the active Alertmanagers.\n1306: type AlertmanagerDiscovery struct {\n1307: \tActiveAlertmanagers  []*AlertmanagerTarget `json:\"activeAlertmanagers\"`\n1308: \tDroppedAlertmanagers []*AlertmanagerTarget `json:\"droppedAlertmanagers\"`\n1309: }\n1310: \n1311: // AlertmanagerTarget has info on one AM.\n1312: type AlertmanagerTarget struct {\n1313: \tURL string `json:\"url\"`\n1314: }\n1315: \n1316: func (api *API) alertmanagers(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1317: \turls := api.alertmanagerRetriever(r.Context()).Alertmanagers()\n1318: \tdroppedURLS := api.alertmanagerRetriever(r.Context()).DroppedAlertmanagers()\n1319: \tams := &AlertmanagerDiscovery{ActiveAlertmanagers: make([]*AlertmanagerTarget, len(urls)), DroppedAlertmanagers: make([]*AlertmanagerTarget, len(droppedURLS))}\n1320: \tfor i, url := range urls {\n1321: \t\tams.ActiveAlertmanagers[i] = &AlertmanagerTarget{URL: url.String()}\n1322: \t}\n1323: \tfor i, url := range droppedURLS {\n1324: \t\tams.DroppedAlertmanagers[i] = &AlertmanagerTarget{URL: url.String()}\n1325: \t}\n1326: \treturn apiFuncResult{ams, nil, nil, nil}\n1327: }\n1328: \n1329: // AlertDiscovery has info for all active alerts.\n1330: type AlertDiscovery struct {\n1331: \tAlerts []*Alert `json:\"alerts\"`\n1332: }\n1333: \n1334: // Alert has info for an alert.\n1335: type Alert struct {\n1336: \tLabels          labels.Labels `json:\"labels\"`\n1337: \tAnnotations     labels.Labels `json:\"annotations\"`\n1338: \tState           string        `json:\"state\"`\n1339: \tActiveAt        *time.Time    `json:\"activeAt,omitempty\"`\n1340: \tKeepFiringSince *time.Time    `json:\"keepFiringSince,omitempty\"`\n1341: \tValue           string        `json:\"value\"`\n1342: }\n1343: \n1344: func (api *API) alerts(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1345: \talertingRules := api.rulesRetriever(r.Context()).AlertingRules()\n1346: \talerts := []*Alert{}\n1347: \n1348: \tfor _, alertingRule := range alertingRules {\n1349: \t\talerts = append(\n1350: \t\t\talerts,\n1351: \t\t\trulesAlertsToAPIAlerts(alertingRule.ActiveAlerts())...,\n1352: \t\t)\n1353: \t}\n1354: \n1355: \tres := &AlertDiscovery{Alerts: alerts}\n1356: \n1357: \treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n1358: }\n1359: \n1360: func rulesAlertsToAPIAlerts(rulesAlerts []*rules.Alert) []*Alert {\n1361: \tapiAlerts := make([]*Alert, len(rulesAlerts))\n1362: \tfor i, ruleAlert := range rulesAlerts {\n1363: \t\tapiAlerts[i] = &Alert{\n1364: \t\t\tLabels:      ruleAlert.Labels,\n1365: \t\t\tAnnotations: ruleAlert.Annotations,\n1366: \t\t\tState:       ruleAlert.State.String(),\n1367: \t\t\tActiveAt:    &ruleAlert.ActiveAt,\n1368: \t\t\tValue:       strconv.FormatFloat(ruleAlert.Value, 'e', -1, 64),\n1369: \t\t}\n1370: \t\tif !ruleAlert.KeepFiringSince.IsZero() {\n1371: \t\t\tapiAlerts[i].KeepFiringSince = &ruleAlert.KeepFiringSince\n1372: \t\t}\n1373: \t}\n1374: \n1375: \treturn apiAlerts\n1376: }\n1377: \n1378: func (api *API) metricMetadata(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1379: \tmetrics := map[string]map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n1380: \n1381: \tlimit := -1\n1382: \tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit\"); s != \"\" {\n1383: \t\tvar err error\n1384: \t\tif limit, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil {\n1385: \t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit must be a number\")}, nil, nil}\n1386: \t\t}\n1387: \t}\n1388: \tlimitPerMetric := -1\n1389: \tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit_per_metric\"); s != \"\" {\n1390: \t\tvar err error\n1391: \t\tif limitPerMetric, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil {\n1392: \t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit_per_metric must be a number\")}, nil, nil}\n1393: \t\t}\n1394: \t}\n1395: \n1396: \tmetric := r.FormValue(\"metric\")\n1397: \tfor _, tt := range api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsActive() {\n1398: \t\tfor _, t := range tt {\n1399: \t\t\tif metric == \"\" {\n1400: \t\t\t\tfor _, mm := range t.ListMetadata() {\n1401: \t\t\t\t\tm := metadata.Metadata{Type: mm.Type, Help: mm.Help, Unit: mm.Unit}\n1402: \t\t\t\t\tms, ok := metrics[mm.MetricFamily]\n1403: \n1404: \t\t\t\t\tif limitPerMetric > 0 && len(ms) >= limitPerMetric {\n1405: \t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1406: \t\t\t\t\t}\n1407: \n1408: \t\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n1409: \t\t\t\t\t\tms = map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n1410: \t\t\t\t\t\tmetrics[mm.MetricFamily] = ms\n1411: \t\t\t\t\t}\n1412: \t\t\t\t\tms[m] = struct{}{}\n1413: \t\t\t\t}\n1414: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1415: \t\t\t}\n1416: \n1417: \t\t\tif md, ok := t.GetMetadata(metric); ok {\n1418: \t\t\t\tm := metadata.Metadata{Type: md.Type, Help: md.Help, Unit: md.Unit}\n1419: \t\t\t\tms, ok := metrics[md.MetricFamily]\n1420: \n1421: \t\t\t\tif limitPerMetric > 0 && len(ms) >= limitPerMetric {\n1422: \t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1423: \t\t\t\t}\n1424: \n1425: \t\t\t\tif !ok {\n1426: \t\t\t\t\tms = map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n1427: \t\t\t\t\tmetrics[md.MetricFamily] = ms\n1428: \t\t\t\t}\n1429: \t\t\t\tms[m] = struct{}{}\n1430: \t\t\t}\n1431: \t\t}\n1432: \t}\n1433: \n1434: \t// Put the elements from the pseudo-set into a slice for marshaling.\n1435: \tres := map[string][]metadata.Metadata{}\n1436: \tfor name, set := range metrics {\n1437: \t\tif limit >= 0 && len(res) >= limit {\n1438: \t\t\tbreak\n1439: \t\t}\n1440: \n1441: \t\ts := []metadata.Metadata{}\n1442: \t\tfor metadata := range set {\n1443: \t\t\ts = append(s, metadata)\n1444: \t\t}\n1445: \t\tres[name] = s\n1446: \t}\n1447: \n1448: \treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n1449: }\n1450: \n1451: // RuleDiscovery has info for all rules.\n1452: type RuleDiscovery struct {\n1453: \tRuleGroups     []*RuleGroup `json:\"groups\"`\n1454: \tGroupNextToken string       `json:\"groupNextToken,omitempty\"`\n1455: }\n1456: \n1457: // RuleGroup has info for rules which are part of a group.\n1458: type RuleGroup struct {\n1459: \tName string `json:\"name\"`\n1460: \tFile string `json:\"file\"`\n1461: \t// In order to preserve rule ordering, while exposing type (alerting or recording)\n1462: \t// specific properties, both alerting and recording rules are exposed in the\n1463: \t// same array.\n1464: \tRules          []Rule    `json:\"rules\"`\n1465: \tInterval       float64   `json:\"interval\"`\n1466: \tLimit          int       `json:\"limit\"`\n1467: \tEvaluationTime float64   `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n1468: \tLastEvaluation time.Time `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n1469: }\n1470: \n1471: type Rule any\n1472: \n1473: type AlertingRule struct {\n1474: \t// State can be \"pending\", \"firing\", \"inactive\".\n1475: \tState          string           `json:\"state\"`\n1476: \tName           string           `json:\"name\"`\n1477: \tQuery          string           `json:\"query\"`\n1478: \tDuration       float64          `json:\"duration\"`\n1479: \tKeepFiringFor  float64          `json:\"keepFiringFor\"`\n1480: \tLabels         labels.Labels    `json:\"labels\"`\n1481: \tAnnotations    labels.Labels    `json:\"annotations\"`\n1482: \tAlerts         []*Alert         `json:\"alerts\"`\n1483: \tHealth         rules.RuleHealth `json:\"health\"`\n1484: \tLastError      string           `json:\"lastError,omitempty\"`\n1485: \tEvaluationTime float64          `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n1486: \tLastEvaluation time.Time        `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n1487: \t// Type of an alertingRule is always \"alerting\".\n1488: \tType string `json:\"type\"`\n1489: }\n1490: \n1491: type RecordingRule struct {\n1492: \tName           string           `json:\"name\"`\n1493: \tQuery          string           `json:\"query\"`\n1494: \tLabels         labels.Labels    `json:\"labels,omitempty\"`\n1495: \tHealth         rules.RuleHealth `json:\"health\"`\n1496: \tLastError      string           `json:\"lastError,omitempty\"`\n1497: \tEvaluationTime float64          `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n1498: \tLastEvaluation time.Time        `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n1499: \t// Type of a recordingRule is always \"recording\".\n1500: \tType string `json:\"type\"`\n1501: }\n1502: \n1503: func (api *API) rules(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1504: \tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n1505: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n1506: \t}\n1507: \n1508: \tqueryFormToSet := func(values []string) map[string]struct{} {\n1509: \t\tset := make(map[string]struct{}, len(values))\n1510: \t\tfor _, v := range values {\n1511: \t\t\tset[v] = struct{}{}\n1512: \t\t}\n1513: \t\treturn set\n1514: \t}\n1515: \n1516: \trnSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"rule_name[]\"])\n1517: \trgSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"rule_group[]\"])\n1518: \tfSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"file[]\"])\n1519: \n1520: \tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\"match[]\"])\n1521: \tif err != nil {\n1522: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n1523: \t}\n1524: \n1525: \truleGroups := api.rulesRetriever(r.Context()).RuleGroups()\n1526: \tres := &RuleDiscovery{RuleGroups: make([]*RuleGroup, 0, len(ruleGroups))}\n1527: \ttyp := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"type\"))\n1528: \n1529: \tif typ != \"\" && typ != \"alert\" && typ != \"record\" {\n1530: \t\treturn invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"not supported value %q\", typ), \"type\")\n1531: \t}\n1532: \n1533: \treturnAlerts := typ == \"\" || typ == \"alert\"\n1534: \treturnRecording := typ == \"\" || typ == \"record\"\n1535: \n1536: \texcludeAlerts, err := parseExcludeAlerts(r)\n1537: \tif err != nil {\n1538: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"exclude_alerts\")\n1539: \t}\n1540: \n1541: \tmaxGroups, nextToken, parseErr := parseListRulesPaginationRequest(r)\n1542: \tif parseErr != nil {\n1543: \t\treturn *parseErr\n1544: \t}\n1545: \n1546: \trgs := make([]*RuleGroup, 0, len(ruleGroups))\n1547: \n1548: \tfoundToken := false\n1549: \n1550: \tfor _, grp := range ruleGroups {\n1551: \t\tif maxGroups > 0 && nextToken != \"\" && !foundToken {\n1552: \t\t\tif nextToken != getRuleGroupNextToken(grp.File(), grp.Name()) {\n1553: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1554: \t\t\t}\n1555: \t\t\tfoundToken = true\n1556: \t\t}\n1557: \n1558: \t\tif len(rgSet) > 0 {\n1559: \t\t\tif _, ok := rgSet[grp.Name()]; !ok {\n1560: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1561: \t\t\t}\n1562: \t\t}\n1563: \n1564: \t\tif len(fSet) > 0 {\n1565: \t\t\tif _, ok := fSet[grp.File()]; !ok {\n1566: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1567: \t\t\t}\n1568: \t\t}\n1569: \n1570: \t\tapiRuleGroup := &RuleGroup{\n1571: \t\t\tName:           grp.Name(),\n1572: \t\t\tFile:           grp.File(),\n1573: \t\t\tInterval:       grp.Interval().Seconds(),\n1574: \t\t\tLimit:          grp.Limit(),\n1575: \t\t\tRules:          []Rule{},\n1576: \t\t\tEvaluationTime: grp.GetEvaluationTime().Seconds(),\n1577: \t\t\tLastEvaluation: grp.GetLastEvaluation(),\n1578: \t\t}\n1579: \n1580: \t\tfor _, rr := range grp.Rules(matcherSets...) {\n1581: \t\t\tvar enrichedRule Rule\n1582: \n1583: \t\t\tif len(rnSet) > 0 {\n1584: \t\t\t\tif _, ok := rnSet[rr.Name()]; !ok {\n1585: \t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1586: \t\t\t\t}\n1587: \t\t\t}\n1588: \n1589: \t\t\tlastError := \"\"\n1590: \t\t\tif rr.LastError() != nil {\n1591: \t\t\t\tlastError = rr.LastError().Error()\n1592: \t\t\t}\n1593: \t\t\tswitch rule := rr.(type) {\n1594: \t\t\tcase *rules.AlertingRule:\n1595: \t\t\t\tif !returnAlerts {\n1596: \t\t\t\t\tbreak\n1597: \t\t\t\t}\n1598: \t\t\t\tvar activeAlerts []*Alert\n1599: \t\t\t\tif !excludeAlerts {\n1600: \t\t\t\t\tactiveAlerts = rulesAlertsToAPIAlerts(rule.ActiveAlerts())\n1601: \t\t\t\t}\n1602: \n1603: \t\t\t\tenrichedRule = AlertingRule{\n1604: \t\t\t\t\tState:          rule.State().String(),\n1605: \t\t\t\t\tName:           rule.Name(),\n1606: \t\t\t\t\tQuery:          rule.Query().String(),\n1607: \t\t\t\t\tDuration:       rule.HoldDuration().Seconds(),\n1608: \t\t\t\t\tKeepFiringFor:  rule.KeepFiringFor().Seconds(),\n1609: \t\t\t\t\tLabels:         rule.Labels(),\n1610: \t\t\t\t\tAnnotations:    rule.Annotations(),\n1611: \t\t\t\t\tAlerts:         activeAlerts,\n1612: \t\t\t\t\tHealth:         rule.Health(),\n1613: \t\t\t\t\tLastError:      lastError,\n1614: \t\t\t\t\tEvaluationTime: rule.GetEvaluationDuration().Seconds(),\n1615: \t\t\t\t\tLastEvaluation: rule.GetEvaluationTimestamp(),\n1616: \t\t\t\t\tType:           \"alerting\",\n1617: \t\t\t\t}\n1618: \n1619: \t\t\tcase *rules.RecordingRule:\n1620: \t\t\t\tif !returnRecording {\n1621: \t\t\t\t\tbreak\n1622: \t\t\t\t}\n1623: \t\t\t\tenrichedRule = RecordingRule{\n1624: \t\t\t\t\tName:           rule.Name(),\n1625: \t\t\t\t\tQuery:          rule.Query().String(),\n1626: \t\t\t\t\tLabels:         rule.Labels(),\n1627: \t\t\t\t\tHealth:         rule.Health(),\n1628: \t\t\t\t\tLastError:      lastError,\n1629: \t\t\t\t\tEvaluationTime: rule.GetEvaluationDuration().Seconds(),\n1630: \t\t\t\t\tLastEvaluation: rule.GetEvaluationTimestamp(),\n1631: \t\t\t\t\tType:           \"recording\",\n1632: \t\t\t\t}\n1633: \t\t\tdefault:\n1634: \t\t\t\terr := fmt.Errorf(\"failed to assert type of rule '%v'\", rule.Name())\n1635: \t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n1636: \t\t\t}\n1637: \n1638: \t\t\tif enrichedRule != nil {\n1639: \t\t\t\tapiRuleGroup.Rules = append(apiRuleGroup.Rules, enrichedRule)\n1640: \t\t\t}\n1641: \t\t}\n1642: \n1643: \t\t// If the rule group response has no rules, skip it - this means we filtered all the rules of this group.\n1644: \t\tif len(apiRuleGroup.Rules) > 0 {\n1645: \t\t\tif maxGroups > 0 && len(rgs) == int(maxGroups) {\n1646: \t\t\t\t// We've reached the capacity of our page plus one. That means that for sure there will be at least one\n1647: \t\t\t\t// rule group in a subsequent request. Therefore a next token is required.\n1648: \t\t\t\tres.GroupNextToken = getRuleGroupNextToken(grp.File(), grp.Name())\n1649: \t\t\t\tbreak\n1650: \t\t\t}\n1651: \t\t\trgs = append(rgs, apiRuleGroup)\n1652: \t\t}\n1653: \t}\n1654: \n1655: \tif maxGroups > 0 && nextToken != \"\" && !foundToken {\n1656: \t\treturn invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"invalid group_next_token '%v'. were rule groups changed?\", nextToken), \"group_next_token\")\n1657: \t}\n1658: \n1659: \tres.RuleGroups = rgs\n1660: \treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n1661: }\n1662: \n1663: func parseExcludeAlerts(r *http.Request) (bool, error) {\n1664: \texcludeAlertsParam := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"exclude_alerts\"))\n1665: \n1666: \tif excludeAlertsParam == \"\" {\n1667: \t\treturn false, nil\n1668: \t}\n1669: \n1670: \texcludeAlerts, err := strconv.ParseBool(excludeAlertsParam)\n1671: \tif err != nil {\n1672: \t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"error converting exclude_alerts: %w\", err)\n1673: \t}\n1674: \treturn excludeAlerts, nil\n1675: }\n1676: \n1677: func parseListRulesPaginationRequest(r *http.Request) (int64, string, *apiFuncResult) {\n1678: \tvar (\n1679: \t\tparsedMaxGroups int64 = -1\n1680: \t\terr             error\n1681: \t)\n1682: \tmaxGroups := r.URL.Query().Get(\"group_limit\")\n1683: \tnextToken := r.URL.Query().Get(\"group_next_token\")\n1684: \n1685: \tif nextToken != \"\" && maxGroups == \"\" {\n1686: \t\terrResult := invalidParamError(errors.New(\"group_limit needs to be present in order to paginate over the groups\"), \"group_next_token\")\n1687: \t\treturn -1, \"\", &errResult\n1688: \t}\n1689: \n1690: \tif maxGroups != \"\" {\n1691: \t\tparsedMaxGroups, err = strconv.ParseInt(maxGroups, 10, 32)\n1692: \t\tif err != nil {\n1693: \t\t\terrResult := invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"group_limit needs to be a valid number: %w\", err), \"group_limit\")\n1694: \t\t\treturn -1, \"\", &errResult\n1695: \t\t}\n1696: \t\tif parsedMaxGroups <= 0 {\n1697: \t\t\terrResult := invalidParamError(errors.New(\"group_limit needs to be greater than 0\"), \"group_limit\")\n1698: \t\t\treturn -1, \"\", &errResult\n1699: \t\t}\n1700: \t}\n1701: \n1702: \tif parsedMaxGroups > 0 {\n1703: \t\treturn parsedMaxGroups, nextToken, nil\n1704: \t}\n1705: \n1706: \treturn -1, \"\", nil\n1707: }\n1708: \n1709: func getRuleGroupNextToken(file, group string) string {\n1710: \th := sha1.New()\n1711: \th.Write([]byte(file + \";\" + group))\n1712: \treturn hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil))\n1713: }\n1714: \n1715: type prometheusConfig struct {\n1716: \tYAML string `json:\"yaml\"`\n1717: }\n1718: \n1719: func (api *API) serveRuntimeInfo(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1720: \tstatus, err := api.runtimeInfo()\n1721: \tif err != nil {\n1722: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{status, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n1723: \t}\n1724: \treturn apiFuncResult{status, nil, nil, nil}\n1725: }\n1726: \n1727: func (api *API) serveBuildInfo(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1728: \treturn apiFuncResult{api.buildInfo, nil, nil, nil}\n1729: }\n1730: \n1731: func (api *API) serveConfig(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1732: \tcfg := &prometheusConfig{\n1733: \t\tYAML: api.config().String(),\n1734: \t}\n1735: \treturn apiFuncResult{cfg, nil, nil, nil}\n1736: }\n1737: \n1738: func (api *API) serveFlags(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1739: \treturn apiFuncResult{api.flagsMap, nil, nil, nil}\n1740: }\n1741: \n1742: // TSDBStat holds the information about individual cardinality.\n1743: type TSDBStat struct {\n1744: \tName  string `json:\"name\"`\n1745: \tValue uint64 `json:\"value\"`\n1746: }\n1747: \n1748: // HeadStats has information about the TSDB head.\n1749: type HeadStats struct {\n1750: \tNumSeries     uint64 `json:\"numSeries\"`\n1751: \tNumLabelPairs int    `json:\"numLabelPairs\"`\n1752: \tChunkCount    int64  `json:\"chunkCount\"`\n1753: \tMinTime       int64  `json:\"minTime\"`\n1754: \tMaxTime       int64  `json:\"maxTime\"`\n1755: }\n1756: \n1757: // TSDBStatus has information of cardinality statistics from postings.\n1758: type TSDBStatus struct {\n1759: \tHeadStats                   HeadStats  `json:\"headStats\"`\n1760: \tSeriesCountByMetricName     []TSDBStat `json:\"seriesCountByMetricName\"`\n1761: \tLabelValueCountByLabelName  []TSDBStat `json:\"labelValueCountByLabelName\"`\n1762: \tMemoryInBytesByLabelName    []TSDBStat `json:\"memoryInBytesByLabelName\"`\n1763: \tSeriesCountByLabelValuePair []TSDBStat `json:\"seriesCountByLabelValuePair\"`\n1764: }\n1765: \n1766: // TSDBStatsFromIndexStats converts a index.Stat slice to a TSDBStat slice.\n1767: func TSDBStatsFromIndexStats(stats []index.Stat) []TSDBStat {\n1768: \tresult := make([]TSDBStat, 0, len(stats))\n1769: \tfor _, item := range stats {\n1770: \t\titem := TSDBStat{Name: item.Name, Value: item.Count}\n1771: \t\tresult = append(result, item)\n1772: \t}\n1773: \treturn result\n1774: }\n1775: \n1776: func (api *API) serveTSDBBlocks(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1777: \tblockMetas, err := api.db.BlockMetas()\n1778: \tif err != nil {\n1779: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, fmt.Errorf(\"error getting block metadata: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n1780: \t}\n1781: \n1782: \treturn apiFuncResult{\n1783: \t\tdata: map[string][]tsdb.BlockMeta{\n1784: \t\t\t\"blocks\": blockMetas,\n1785: \t\t},\n1786: \t}\n1787: }\n1788: \n1789: func (api *API) serveTSDBStatus(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1790: \tlimit := 10\n1791: \tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit\"); s != \"\" {\n1792: \t\tvar err error\n1793: \t\tif limit, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil || limit < 1 {\n1794: \t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit must be a positive number\")}, nil, nil}\n1795: \t\t}\n1796: \t}\n1797: \ts, err := api.db.Stats(labels.MetricName, limit)\n1798: \tif err != nil {\n1799: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n1800: \t}\n1801: \tmetrics, err := api.gatherer.Gather()\n1802: \tif err != nil {\n1803: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, fmt.Errorf(\"error gathering runtime status: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n1804: \t}\n1805: \tchunkCount := int64(math.NaN())\n1806: \tfor _, mF := range metrics {\n1807: \t\tif *mF.Name == \"prometheus_tsdb_head_chunks\" {\n1808: \t\t\tm := mF.Metric[0]\n1809: \t\t\tif m.Gauge != nil {\n1810: \t\t\t\tchunkCount = int64(m.Gauge.GetValue())\n1811: \t\t\t\tbreak\n1812: \t\t\t}\n1813: \t\t}\n1814: \t}\n1815: \treturn apiFuncResult{TSDBStatus{\n1816: \t\tHeadStats: HeadStats{\n1817: \t\t\tNumSeries:     s.NumSeries,\n1818: \t\t\tChunkCount:    chunkCount,\n1819: \t\t\tMinTime:       s.MinTime,\n1820: \t\t\tMaxTime:       s.MaxTime,\n1821: \t\t\tNumLabelPairs: s.IndexPostingStats.NumLabelPairs,\n1822: \t\t},\n1823: \t\tSeriesCountByMetricName:     TSDBStatsFromIndexStats(s.IndexPostingStats.CardinalityMetricsStats),\n1824: \t\tLabelValueCountByLabelName:  TSDBStatsFromIndexStats(s.IndexPostingStats.CardinalityLabelStats),\n1825: \t\tMemoryInBytesByLabelName:    TSDBStatsFromIndexStats(s.IndexPostingStats.LabelValueStats),\n1826: \t\tSeriesCountByLabelValuePair: TSDBStatsFromIndexStats(s.IndexPostingStats.LabelValuePairsStats),\n1827: \t}, nil, nil, nil}\n1828: }\n1829: \n1830: type walReplayStatus struct {\n1831: \tMin     int `json:\"min\"`\n1832: \tMax     int `json:\"max\"`\n1833: \tCurrent int `json:\"current\"`\n1834: }\n1835: \n1836: func (api *API) serveWALReplayStatus(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n1837: \thttputil.SetCORS(w, api.CORSOrigin, r)\n1838: \tstatus, err := api.db.WALReplayStatus()\n1839: \tif err != nil {\n1840: \t\tapi.respondError(w, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil)\n1841: \t}\n1842: \tapi.respond(w, r, walReplayStatus{\n1843: \t\tMin:     status.Min,\n1844: \t\tMax:     status.Max,\n1845: \t\tCurrent: status.Current,\n1846: \t}, nil, \"\")\n1847: }\n1848: \n1849: func (api *API) notifications(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n1850: \thttputil.SetCORS(w, api.CORSOrigin, r)\n1851: \tapi.respond(w, r, api.notificationsGetter(), nil, \"\")\n1852: }\n1853: \n1854: func (api *API) notificationsSSE(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n1855: \thttputil.SetCORS(w, api.CORSOrigin, r)\n1856: \tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream\")\n1857: \tw.Header().Set(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\")\n1858: \tw.Header().Set(\"Connection\", \"keep-alive\")\n1859: \n1860: \t// Subscribe to notifications.\n1861: \tnotifications, unsubscribe, ok := api.notificationsSub()\n1862: \tif !ok {\n1863: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n1864: \t\treturn\n1865: \t}\n1866: \tdefer unsubscribe()\n1867: \n1868: \t// Set up a flusher to push the response to the client.\n1869: \tflusher, ok := w.(http.Flusher)\n1870: \tif !ok {\n1871: \t\thttp.Error(w, \"Streaming unsupported\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1872: \t\treturn\n1873: \t}\n1874: \n1875: \t// Flush the response to ensure the headers are immediately and eventSource\n1876: \t// onopen is triggered client-side.\n1877: \tflusher.Flush()\n1878: \n1879: \tfor {\n1880: \t\tselect {\n1881: \t\tcase notification := <-notifications:\n1882: \t\t\t// Marshal the notification to JSON.\n1883: \t\t\tjsonData, err := json.Marshal(notification)\n1884: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n1885: \t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1886: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1887: \t\t\t}\n1888: \n1889: \t\t\t// Write the event data in SSE format with JSON content.\n1890: \t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(w, \"data: %s\\n\\n\", jsonData)\n1891: \n1892: \t\t\t// Flush the response to ensure the data is sent immediately.\n1893: \t\t\tflusher.Flush()\n1894: \t\tcase <-r.Context().Done():\n1895: \t\t\treturn\n1896: \t\t}\n1897: \t}\n1898: }\n1899: \n1900: func (api *API) remoteRead(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n1901: \t// This is only really for tests - this will never be nil IRL.\n1902: \tif api.remoteReadHandler != nil {\n1903: \t\tapi.remoteReadHandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n1904: \t} else {\n1905: \t\thttp.Error(w, \"not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n1906: \t}\n1907: }\n1908: \n1909: func (api *API) remoteWrite(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n1910: \tif api.remoteWriteHandler != nil {\n1911: \t\tapi.remoteWriteHandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n1912: \t} else {\n1913: \t\thttp.Error(w, \"remote write receiver needs to be enabled with --web.enable-remote-write-receiver\", http.StatusNotFound)\n1914: \t}\n1915: }\n1916: \n1917: func (api *API) otlpWrite(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n1918: \tif api.otlpWriteHandler != nil {\n1919: \t\tapi.otlpWriteHandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n1920: \t} else {\n1921: \t\thttp.Error(w, \"otlp write receiver needs to be enabled with --web.enable-otlp-receiver\", http.StatusNotFound)\n1922: \t}\n1923: }\n1924: \n1925: func (api *API) deleteSeries(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1926: \tif !api.enableAdmin {\n1927: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorUnavailable, errors.New(\"admin APIs disabled\")}, nil, nil}\n1928: \t}\n1929: \tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n1930: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n1931: \t}\n1932: \tif len(r.Form[\"match[]\"]) == 0 {\n1933: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"no match[] parameter provided\")}, nil, nil}\n1934: \t}\n1935: \n1936: \tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n1937: \tif err != nil {\n1938: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n1939: \t}\n1940: \tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n1941: \tif err != nil {\n1942: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n1943: \t}\n1944: \n1945: \tfor _, s := range r.Form[\"match[]\"] {\n1946: \t\tmatchers, err := parser.ParseMetricSelector(s)\n1947: \t\tif err != nil {\n1948: \t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"match[]\")\n1949: \t\t}\n1950: \t\tif err := api.db.Delete(r.Context(), timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end), matchers...); err != nil {\n1951: \t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n1952: \t\t}\n1953: \t}\n1954: \n1955: \treturn apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\n1956: }\n1957: \n1958: func (api *API) snapshot(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1959: \tif !api.enableAdmin {\n1960: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorUnavailable, errors.New(\"admin APIs disabled\")}, nil, nil}\n1961: \t}\n1962: \tvar (\n1963: \t\tskipHead bool\n1964: \t\terr      error\n1965: \t)\n1966: \tif r.FormValue(\"skip_head\") != \"\" {\n1967: \t\tskipHead, err = strconv.ParseBool(r.FormValue(\"skip_head\"))\n1968: \t\tif err != nil {\n1969: \t\t\treturn invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"unable to parse boolean: %w\", err), \"skip_head\")\n1970: \t\t}\n1971: \t}\n1972: \n1973: \tvar (\n1974: \t\tsnapdir = filepath.Join(api.dbDir, \"snapshots\")\n1975: \t\tname    = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s-%016x\",\n1976: \t\t\ttime.Now().UTC().Format(\"20060102T150405Z0700\"),\n1977: \t\t\trand.Int63())\n1978: \t\tdir = filepath.Join(snapdir, name)\n1979: \t)\n1980: \tif err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o777); err != nil {\n1981: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, fmt.Errorf(\"create snapshot directory: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n1982: \t}\n1983: \tif err := api.db.Snapshot(dir, !skipHead); err != nil {\n1984: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, fmt.Errorf(\"create snapshot: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n1985: \t}\n1986: \n1987: \treturn apiFuncResult{struct {\n1988: \t\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n1989: \t}{name}, nil, nil, nil}\n1990: }\n1991: \n1992: func (api *API) cleanTombstones(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1993: \tif !api.enableAdmin {\n1994: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorUnavailable, errors.New(\"admin APIs disabled\")}, nil, nil}\n1995: \t}\n1996: \tif err := api.db.CleanTombstones(); err != nil {\n1997: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n1998: \t}\n1999: \n2000: \treturn apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\n2001: }\n2002: \n2003: // Query string is needed to get the position information for the annotations, and it\n2004: // can be empty if the position information isn't needed.\n2005: func (api *API) respond(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request, data any, warnings annotations.Annotations, query string) {\n2006: \tstatusMessage := statusSuccess\n2007: \twarn, info := warnings.AsStrings(query, 10, 10)\n2008: \n2009: \tresp := &Response{\n2010: \t\tStatus:   statusMessage,\n2011: \t\tData:     data,\n2012: \t\tWarnings: warn,\n2013: \t\tInfos:    info,\n2014: \t}\n2015: \n2016: \tcodec, err := api.negotiateCodec(req, resp)\n2017: \tif err != nil {\n2018: \t\tapi.respondError(w, &apiError{errorNotAcceptable, err}, nil)\n2019: \t\treturn\n2020: \t}\n2021: \n2022: \tb, err := codec.Encode(resp)\n2023: \tif err != nil {\n2024: \t\tapi.logger.Error(\"error marshaling response\", \"url\", req.URL, \"err\", err)\n2025: \t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n2026: \t\treturn\n2027: \t}\n2028: \n2029: \tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", codec.ContentType().String())\n2030: \tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n2031: \tif n, err := w.Write(b); err != nil {\n2032: \t\tapi.logger.Error(\"error writing response\", \"url\", req.URL, \"bytesWritten\", n, \"err\", err)\n2033: \t}\n2034: }\n2035: \n2036: func (api *API) negotiateCodec(req *http.Request, resp *Response) (Codec, error) {\n2037: \tfor _, clause := range goautoneg.ParseAccept(req.Header.Get(\"Accept\")) {\n2038: \t\tfor _, codec := range api.codecs {\n2039: \t\t\tif codec.ContentType().Satisfies(clause) && codec.CanEncode(resp) {\n2040: \t\t\t\treturn codec, nil\n2041: \t\t\t}\n2042: \t\t}\n2043: \t}\n2044: \n2045: \tdefaultCodec := api.codecs[0]\n2046: \tif !defaultCodec.CanEncode(resp) {\n2047: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot encode response as %s\", defaultCodec.ContentType())\n2048: \t}\n2049: \n2050: \treturn defaultCodec, nil\n2051: }\n2052: \n2053: func (api *API) respondError(w http.ResponseWriter, apiErr *apiError, data any) {\n2054: \tjson := jsoniter.ConfigCompatibleWithStandardLibrary\n2055: \tb, err := json.Marshal(&Response{\n2056: \t\tStatus:    statusError,\n2057: \t\tErrorType: apiErr.typ.str,\n2058: \t\tError:     apiErr.err.Error(),\n2059: \t\tData:      data,\n2060: \t})\n2061: \tif err != nil {\n2062: \t\tapi.logger.Error(\"error marshaling json response\", \"err\", err)\n2063: \t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n2064: \t\treturn\n2065: \t}\n2066: \n2067: \tvar code int\n2068: \tif api.overrideErrorCode != nil {\n2069: \t\tif newCode, override := api.overrideErrorCode(apiErr.typ.num, apiErr.err); override {\n2070: \t\t\tcode = newCode\n2071: \t\t} else {\n2072: \t\t\tcode = getDefaultErrorCode(apiErr.typ)\n2073: \t\t}\n2074: \t} else {\n2075: \t\tcode = getDefaultErrorCode(apiErr.typ)\n2076: \t}\n2077: \n2078: \tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n2079: \tw.WriteHeader(code)\n2080: \tif n, err := w.Write(b); err != nil {\n2081: \t\tapi.logger.Error(\"error writing response\", \"bytesWritten\", n, \"err\", err)\n2082: \t}\n2083: }\n2084: \n2085: func getDefaultErrorCode(errType errorType) int {\n2086: \tswitch errType {\n2087: \tcase errorBadData:\n2088: \t\treturn http.StatusBadRequest\n2089: \tcase errorExec:\n2090: \t\treturn http.StatusUnprocessableEntity\n2091: \tcase errorCanceled:\n2092: \t\treturn statusClientClosedConnection\n2093: \tcase errorTimeout:\n2094: \t\treturn http.StatusServiceUnavailable\n2095: \tcase errorInternal:\n2096: \t\treturn http.StatusInternalServerError\n2097: \tcase errorNotFound:\n2098: \t\treturn http.StatusNotFound\n2099: \tcase errorNotAcceptable:\n2100: \t\treturn http.StatusNotAcceptable\n2101: \tdefault:\n2102: \t\treturn http.StatusInternalServerError\n2103: \t}\n2104: }\n2105: \n2106: func parseTimeParam(r *http.Request, paramName string, defaultValue time.Time) (time.Time, error) {\n2107: \tval := r.FormValue(paramName)\n2108: \tif val == \"\" {\n2109: \t\treturn defaultValue, nil\n2110: \t}\n2111: \tresult, err := parseTime(val)\n2112: \tif err != nil {\n2113: \t\treturn time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid time value for '%s': %w\", paramName, err)\n2114: \t}\n2115: \treturn result, nil\n2116: }\n2117: \n2118: func parseTime(s string) (time.Time, error) {\n2119: \tif t, err := strconv.ParseFloat(s, 64); err == nil {\n2120: \t\ts, ns := math.Modf(t)\n2121: \t\tns = math.Round(ns*1000) / 1000\n2122: \t\treturn time.Unix(int64(s), int64(ns*float64(time.Second))).UTC(), nil\n2123: \t}\n2124: \tif t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, s); err == nil {\n2125: \t\treturn t, nil\n2126: \t}\n2127: \n2128: \t// Stdlib's time parser can only handle 4 digit years. As a workaround until\n2129: \t// that is fixed we want to at least support our own boundary times.\n2130: \t// Context: https://github.com/prometheus/client_golang/issues/614\n2131: \t// Upstream issue: https://github.com/golang/go/issues/20555\n2132: \tswitch s {\n2133: \tcase minTimeFormatted:\n2134: \t\treturn MinTime, nil\n2135: \tcase maxTimeFormatted:\n2136: \t\treturn MaxTime, nil\n2137: \t}\n2138: \treturn time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot parse %q to a valid timestamp\", s)\n2139: }\n2140: \n2141: func parseDuration(s string) (time.Duration, error) {\n2142: \tif d, err := strconv.ParseFloat(s, 64); err == nil {\n2143: \t\tts := d * float64(time.Second)\n2144: \t\tif ts > float64(math.MaxInt64) || ts < float64(math.MinInt64) {\n2145: \t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot parse %q to a valid duration. It overflows int64\", s)\n2146: \t\t}\n2147: \t\treturn time.Duration(ts), nil\n2148: \t}\n2149: \tif d, err := model.ParseDuration(s); err == nil {\n2150: \t\treturn time.Duration(d), nil\n2151: \t}\n2152: \treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot parse %q to a valid duration\", s)\n2153: }\n2154: \n2155: func parseMatchersParam(matchers []string) ([][]*labels.Matcher, error) {\n2156: \tmatcherSets, err := parser.ParseMetricSelectors(matchers)\n2157: \tif err != nil {\n2158: \t\treturn nil, err\n2159: \t}\n2160: \n2161: OUTER:\n2162: \tfor _, ms := range matcherSets {\n2163: \t\tfor _, lm := range ms {\n2164: \t\t\tif lm != nil && !lm.Matches(\"\") {\n2165: \t\t\t\tcontinue OUTER\n2166: \t\t\t}\n2167: \t\t}\n2168: \t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"match[] must contain at least one non-empty matcher\")\n2169: \t}\n2170: \treturn matcherSets, nil\n2171: }\n2172: \n2173: // parseLimitParam returning 0 means no limit is to be applied.\n2174: func parseLimitParam(limitStr string) (limit int, err error) {\n2175: \tif limitStr == \"\" {\n2176: \t\treturn limit, nil\n2177: \t}\n2178: \n2179: \tlimit, err = strconv.Atoi(limitStr)\n2180: \tif err != nil {\n2181: \t\treturn limit, err\n2182: \t}\n2183: \tif limit < 0 {\n2184: \t\treturn limit, errors.New(\"limit must be non-negative\")\n2185: \t}\n2186: \n2187: \treturn limit, nil\n2188: }\n2189: \n2190: // toHintLimit increases the API limit, as returned by parseLimitParam, by 1.\n2191: // This allows for emitting warnings when the results are truncated.\n2192: func toHintLimit(limit int) int {\n2193: \t// 0 means no limit and avoid int overflow\n2194: \tif limit > 0 && limit < math.MaxInt {\n2195: \t\treturn limit + 1\n2196: \t}\n2197: \treturn limit\n2198: }\n2199: \n2200: // truncateResults truncates result for queryRange() and query().\n2201: // No truncation for other types(Scalars or Strings).\n2202: func truncateResults(result *promql.Result, limit int) (*promql.Result, bool) {\n2203: \tisTruncated := false\n2204: \n2205: \tswitch v := result.Value.(type) {\n2206: \tcase promql.Matrix:\n2207: \t\tif len(v) > limit {\n2208: \t\t\tresult.Value = v[:limit]\n2209: \t\t\tisTruncated = true\n2210: \t\t}\n2211: \tcase promql.Vector:\n2212: \t\tif len(v) > limit {\n2213: \t\t\tresult.Value = v[:limit]\n2214: \t\t\tisTruncated = true\n2215: \t\t}\n2216: \t}\n2217: \n2218: \t// Return the modified result. Unchanged for other types.\n2219: \treturn result, isTruncated\n2220: }\n2221: ",
