names with matcher.\n3630: \t\t\t{\n3631: \t\t\t\tendpoint: api.labelNames,\n3632: \t\t\t\tquery: url.Values{\n3633: \t\t\t\t\t\"match[]\": []string{`test_metric3`},\n3634: \t\t\t\t},\n3635: \t\t\t\tresponse: []string{\"__name__\", \"dup\", \"foo\"},\n3636: \t\t\t},\n3637: \t\t\t// Label names with matcher using label filter.\n3638: \t\t\t// There is no matching series.\n3639: \t\t\t{\n3640: \t\t\t\tendpoint: api.labelNames,\n3641: \t\t\t\tquery: url.Values{\n3642: \t\t\t\t\t\"match[]\": []string{`test_metric1{foo=\"test\"}`},\n3643: \t\t\t\t},\n3644: \t\t\t\tresponse: []string{},\n3645: \t\t\t},\n3646: \t\t\t// Label names with matcher and time range.\n3647: \t\t\t{\n3648: \t\t\t\tendpoint: api.labelNames,\n3649: \t\t\t\tquery: url.Values{\n3650: \t\t\t\t\t\"match[]\": []string{`test_metric2`},\n3651: \t\t\t\t\t\"start\":   []string{\"1\"},\n3652: \t\t\t\t\t\"end\":     []string{\"100000000\"},\n3653: \t\t\t\t},\n3654: \t\t\t\tresponse: []string{\"__name__\", \"foo\"},\n3655: \t\t\t},\n3656: \t\t\t// Label names with limit.\n3657: \t\t\t{\n3658: \t\t\t\tendpoint: api.labelNames,\n3659: \t\t\t\tquery: url.Values{\n3660: \t\t\t\t\t\"limit\": []string{\"2\"},\n3661: \t\t\t\t},\n3662: \t\t\t\tresponseLen:   2, // API does not specify which particular values will come back.\n3663: \t\t\t\twarningsCount: 1,\n3664: \t\t\t},\n3665: \t\t\t{\n3666: \t\t\t\tendpoint: api.labelNames,\n3667: \t\t\t\tquery: url.Values{\n3668: \t\t\t\t\t\"limit\": []string{\"5\"},\n3669: \t\t\t\t},\n3670: \t\t\t\tresponseLen:   5, // API does not specify which particular values will come back.\n3671: \t\t\t\twarningsCount: 0, // No warnings if limit isn't exceeded.\n3672: \t\t\t},\n3673: \t\t}...)\n3674: \t}\n3675: \n3676: \tmethods := func(f apiFunc) []string {\n3677: \t\tfp := reflect.ValueOf(f).Pointer()\n3678: \t\tif fp == reflect.ValueOf(api.query).Pointer() || fp == reflect.ValueOf(api.queryRange).Pointer() || fp == reflect.ValueOf(api.series).Pointer() {\n3679: \t\t\treturn []string{http.MethodGet, http.MethodPost}\n3680: \t\t}\n3681: \t\treturn []string{http.MethodGet}\n3682: \t}\n3683: \n3684: \trequest := func(m string, q url.Values) (*http.Request, error) {\n3685: \t\tif m == http.MethodPost {\n3686: \t\t\tr, err := http.NewRequest(m, \"http://example.com\", strings.NewReader(q.Encode()))\n3687: \t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/x-www-form-urlencoded\")\n3688: \t\t\tr.RemoteAddr = \"127.0.0.1:20201\"\n3689: \t\t\treturn r, err\n3690: \t\t}\n3691: \t\tr, err := http.NewRequest(m, fmt.Sprintf(\"http://example.com?%s\", q.Encode()), nil)\n3692: \t\tr.RemoteAddr = \"127.0.0.1:20201\"\n3693: \t\treturn r, err\n3694: \t}\n3695: \n3696: \tfor i, test := range tests {\n3697: \t\tt.Run(fmt.Sprintf(\"run %d %s %q\", i, describeAPIFunc(test.endpoint), test.query.Encode()), func(t *testing.T) {\n3698: \t\t\tfor _, method := range methods(test.endpoint) {\n3699: \t\t\t\tt.Run(method, func(t *testing.T) {\n3700: \t\t\t\t\t// Build a context with the correct request params.\n3701: \t\t\t\t\tctx := context.Background()\n3702: \t\t\t\t\tfor p, v := range test.params {\n3703: \t\t\t\t\t\tctx = route.WithParam(ctx, p, v) //nolint:fatcontext // This is intentional to provide the route params.\n3704: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3705: \n3706: \t\t\t\t\treq, err := request(method, test.query)\n3707: \t\t\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n3708: \n3709: \t\t\t\t\ttr.ResetMetadataStore()\n3710: \t\t\t\t\tfor _, tm := range test.metadata {\n3711: \t\t\t\t\t\ttr.SetMetadataStoreForTargets(tm.identifier, &testMetaStore{Metadata: tm.metadata})\n3712: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3713: \n3714: \t\t\t\t\tfor _, te := range test.exemplars {\n3715: \t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, e := range te.Exemplars {\n3716: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t_, err := es.AppendExemplar(0, te.SeriesLabels, e)\n3717: \t\t\t\t\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n3718: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n3719: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3720: \n3721: \t\t\t\t\tres := test.endpoint(req.WithContext(ctx))\n3722: \t\t\t\t\tassertAPIError(t, res.err, test.errType)\n3723: \n3724: \t\t\t\t\tif test.sorter != nil {\n3725: \t\t\t\t\t\ttest.sorter(res.data)\n3726: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3727: \n3728: \t\t\t\t\tif test.responseLen != 0 {\n3729: \t\t\t\t\t\tassertAPIResponseLength(t, res.data, test.responseLen)\n3730: \t\t\t\t\t\tif test.responseMetadataTotal != 0 {\n3731: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tassertAPIResponseMetadataLen(t, res.data, test.responseMetadataTotal)\n3732: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n3733: \t\t\t\t\t} else {\n3734: \t\t\t\t\t\tif test.zeroFunc != nil {\n3735: \t\t\t\t\t\t\ttest.zeroFunc(res.data)\n3736: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n3737: \t\t\t\t\t\tif test.response != nil {\n3738: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tassertAPIResponse(t, res.data, test.response)\n3739: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n3740: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3741: \n3742: \t\t\t\t\tif test.responseAsJSON != \"\" {\n3743: \t\t\t\t\t\tjson := jsoniter.ConfigCompatibleWithStandardLibrary\n3744: \t\t\t\t\t\ts, err := json.Marshal(res.data)\n3745: \t\t\t\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n3746: \t\t\t\t\t\trequire.JSONEq(t, test.responseAsJSON, string(s))\n3747: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3748: \n3749: \t\t\t\t\trequire.Len(t, res.warnings, test.warningsCount)\n3750: \t\t\t\t})\n3751: \t\t\t}\n3752: \t\t})\n3753: \t}\n3754: }\n3755: \n3756: func describeAPIFunc(f apiFunc) string {\n3757: \tname := runtime.FuncForPC(reflect.ValueOf(f).Pointer()).Name()\n3758: \treturn strings.Split(name[strings.LastIndex(name, \".\")+1:], \"-\")[0]\n3759: }\n3760: \n3761: func assertAPIError(t *testing.T, got *apiError, exp errorType) {\n3762: \tt.Helper()\n3763: \n3764: \tif exp.num == ErrorNone {\n3765: \t\trequire.Nil(t, got)\n3766: \t} else {\n3767: \t\trequire.NotNil(t, got)\n3768: \t\trequire.Equal(t, exp, got.typ, \"(%q)\", got)\n3769: \t}\n3770: }\n3771: \n3772: func assertAPIResponse(t *testing.T, got, exp any) {\n3773: \tt.Helper()\n3774: \n3775: \ttestutil.RequireEqual(t, exp, got)\n3776: }\n3777: \n3778: func assertAPIResponseLength(t *testing.T, got any, expLen int) {\n3779: \tt.Helper()\n3780: \n3781: \tgotLen := reflect.ValueOf(got).Len()\n3782: \trequire.Equal(t, expLen, gotLen, \"Response length does not match\")\n3783: }\n3784: \n3785: func assertAPIResponseMetadataLen(t *testing.T, got any, expLen int) {\n3786: \tt.Helper()\n3787: \n3788: \tvar gotLen int\n3789: \tresponse := got.(map[string][]metadata.Metadata)\n3790: \tfor _, m := range response {\n3791: \t\tgotLen += len(m)\n3792: \t}\n3793: \n3794: \trequire.Equal(t, expLen, gotLen, \"Amount of metadata in the response does not match\")\n3795: }\n3796: \n3797: type fakeDB struct {\n3798: \terr        error\n3799: \tblockMetas []tsdb.BlockMeta\n3800: }\n3801: \n3802: func (f *fakeDB) CleanTombstones() error { return f.err }\n3803: \n3804: func (f *fakeDB) BlockMetas() ([]tsdb.BlockMeta, error) {\n3805: \treturn f.blockMetas, nil\n3806: }\n3807: func (f *fakeDB) Delete(context.Context, int64, int64, ...*labels.Matcher) error { return f.err }\n3808: func (f *fakeDB) Snapshot(string, bool) error                                    { return f.err }\n3809: func (*fakeDB) Stats(statsByLabelName string, limit int) (_ *tsdb.Stats, retErr error) {\n3810: \tdbDir, err := os.MkdirTemp(\"\", \"tsdb-api-ready\")\n3811: \tif err != nil {\n3812: \t\treturn nil, err\n3813: \t}\n3814: \tdefer func() {\n3815: \t\terr := os.RemoveAll(dbDir)\n3816: \t\tif retErr != nil {\n3817: \t\t\tretErr = err\n3818: \t\t}\n3819: \t}()\n3820: \topts := tsdb.DefaultHeadOptions()\n3821: \topts.ChunkRange = 1000\n3822: \th, _ := tsdb.NewHead(nil, nil, nil, nil, opts, nil)\n3823: \treturn h.Stats(statsByLabelName, limit), nil\n3824: }\n3825: \n3826: func (*fakeDB) WALReplayStatus() (tsdb.WALReplayStatus, error) {\n3827: \treturn tsdb.WALReplayStatus{}, nil\n3828: }\n3829: \n3830: func TestAdminEndpoints(t *testing.T) {\n3831: \ttsdb, tsdbWithError, tsdbNotReady := &fakeDB{}, &fakeDB{err: errors.New(\"some error\")}, &fakeDB{err: fmt.Errorf(\"wrap: %w\", tsdb.ErrNotReady)}\n3832: \tsnapshotAPI := func(api *API) apiFunc { return api.snapshot }\n3833: \tcleanAPI := func(api *API) apiFunc { return api.cleanTombstones }\n3834: \tdeleteAPI := func(api *API) apiFunc { return api.deleteSeries }\n3835: \n3836: \tfor _, tc := range []struct {\n3837: \t\tdb          *fakeDB\n3838: \t\tenableAdmin bool\n3839: \t\tendpoint    func(api *API) apiFunc\n3840: \t\tmethod      string\n3841: \t\tvalues      url.Values\n3842: \n3843: \t\terrType errorType\n3844: \t}{\n3845: \t\t// Tests for the snapshot endpoint.\n3846: \t\t{\n3847: \t\t\tdb:          tsdb,\n3848: \t\t\tenableAdmin: false,\n3849: \t\t\tendpoint:    snapshotAPI,\n3850: \n3851: \t\t\terrType: errorUnavailable,\n3852: \t\t},\n3853: \t\t{\n3854: \t\t\tdb:          tsdb,\n3855: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3856: \t\t\tendpoint:    snapshotAPI,\n3857: \n3858: \t\t\terrType: errorNone,\n3859: \t\t},\n3860: \t\t{\n3861: \t\t\tdb:          tsdb,\n3862: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3863: \t\t\tendpoint:    snapshotAPI,\n3864: \t\t\tvalues:      map[string][]string{\"skip_head\": {\"true\"}},\n3865: \n3866: \t\t\terrType: errorNone,\n3867: \t\t},\n3868: \t\t{\n3869: \t\t\tdb:          tsdb,\n3870: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3871: \t\t\tendpoint:    snapshotAPI,\n3872: \t\t\tvalues:      map[string][]string{\"skip_head\": {\"xxx\"}},\n3873: \n3874: \t\t\terrType: errorBadData,\n3875: \t\t},\n3876: \t\t{\n3877: \t\t\tdb:          tsdbWithError,\n3878: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3879: \t\t\tendpoint:    snapshotAPI,\n3880: \n3881: \t\t\terrType: errorInternal,\n3882: \t\t},\n3883: \t\t{\n3884: \t\t\tdb:          tsdbNotReady,\n3885: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3886: \t\t\tendpoint:    snapshotAPI,\n3887: \n3888: \t\t\terrType: errorUnavailable,\n3889: \t\t},\n3890: \t\t// Tests for the cleanTombstones endpoint.\n3891: \t\t{\n3892: \t\t\tdb:          tsdb,\n3893: \t\t\tenableAdmin: false,\n3894: \t\t\tendpoint:    cleanAPI,\n3895: \n3896: \t\t\terrType: errorUnavailable,\n3897: \t\t},\n3898: \t\t{\n3899: \t\t\tdb:          tsdb,\n3900: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3901: \t\t\tendpoint:    cleanAPI,\n3902: \n3903: \t\t\terrType: errorNone,\n3904: \t\t},\n3905: \t\t{\n3906: \t\t\tdb:          tsdbWithError,\n3907: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3908: \t\t\tendpoint:    cleanAPI,\n3909: \n3910: \t\t\terrType: errorInternal,\n3911: \t\t},\n3912: \t\t{\n3913: \t\t\tdb:          tsdbNotReady,\n3914: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3915: \t\t\tendpoint:    cleanAPI,\n3916: \n3917: \t\t\terrType: errorUnavailable,\n3918: \t\t},\n3919: \t\t// Tests for the deleteSeries endpoint.\n3920: \t\t{\n3921: \t\t\tdb:          tsdb,\n3922: \t\t\tenableAdmin: false,\n3923: \t\t\tendpoint:    deleteAPI,\n3924: \n3925: \t\t\terrType: errorUnavailable,\n3926: \t\t},\n3927: \t\t{\n3928: \t\t\tdb:          tsdb,\n3929: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3930: \t\t\tendpoint:    deleteAPI,\n3931: \n3932: \t\t\terrType: errorBadData,\n3933: \t\t},\n3934: \t\t{\n3935: \t\t\tdb:          tsdb,\n3936: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3937: \t\t\tendpoint:    deleteAPI,\n3938: \t\t\tvalues:      map[string][]string{\"match[]\": {\"123\"}},\n3939: \n3940: \t\t\terrType: errorBadData,\n3941: \t\t},\n3942: \t\t{\n3943: \t\t\tdb:          tsdb,\n3944: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3945: \t\t\tendpoint:    deleteAPI,\n3946: \t\t\tvalues:      map[string][]string{\"match[]\": {\"up\"}, \"start\": {\"xxx\"}},\n3947: \n3948: \t\t\terrType: errorBadData,\n3949: \t\t},\n3950: \t\t{\n3951: \t\t\tdb:          tsdb,\n3952: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3953: \t\t\tendpoint:    deleteAPI,\n3954: \t\t\tvalues:      map[string][]string{\"match[]\": {\"up\"}, \"end\": {\"xxx\"}},\n3955: \n3956: \t\t\terrType: errorBadData,\n3957: \t\t},\n3958: \t\t{\n3959: \t\t\tdb:          tsdb,\n3960: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3961: \t\t\tendpoint:    deleteAPI,\n3962: \t\t\tvalues:      map[string][]string{\"match[]\": {\"up\"}},\n3963: \n3964: \t\t\terrType: errorNone,\n3965: \t\t},\n3966: \t\t{\n3967: \t\t\tdb:          tsdb,\n3968: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3969: \t\t\tendpoint:    deleteAPI,\n3970: \t\t\tvalues:      map[string][]string{\"match[]\": {\"up{job!=\\\"foo\\\"}\", \"{job=~\\\"bar.+\\\"}\", \"up{instance!~\\\"fred.+\\\"}\"}},\n3971: \n3972: \t\t\terrType: errorNone,\n3973: \t\t},\n3974: \t\t{\n3975: \t\t\tdb:          tsdbWithError,\n3976: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3977: \t\t\tendpoint:    deleteAPI,\n3978: \t\t\tvalues:      map[string][]string{\"match[]\": {\"up\"}},\n3979: \n3980: \t\t\terrType: errorInternal,\n3981: \t\t},\n3982: \t\t{\n3983: \t\t\tdb:          tsdbNotReady,\n3984: \t\t\tenableAdmin: true,\n3985: \t\t\tendpoint:    deleteAPI,\n3986: \t\t\tvalues:      map[string][]string{\"match[]\": {\"up\"}},\n3987: \n3988: \t\t\terrType: errorUnavailable,\n3989: \t\t},\n3990: \t} {\n3991: \t\tt.Run(\"\", func(t *testing.T) {\n3992: \t\t\tdir := t.TempDir()\n3993: \n3994: \t\t\tapi := &API{\n3995: \t\t\t\tdb:          tc.db,\n3996: \t\t\t\tdbDir:       dir,\n3997: \t\t\t\tready:       func(f http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc { return f },\n3998: \t\t\t\tenableAdmin: tc.enableAdmin,\n3999: \t\t\t}\n4000: \n4001: \t\t\tendpoint := tc.endpoint(api)\n4002: \t\t\treq, err := http.NewRequest(tc.method, fmt.Sprintf(\"?%s\", tc.values.Encode()), nil)\n4003: \t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n4004: \n4005: \t\t\tres := setUnavailStatusOnTSDBNotReady(endpoint(req))\n4006: \t\t\tassertAPIError(t, res.err, tc.errType)\n4007: \t\t})\n4008: \t}\n4009: }\n4010: \n4011: func TestRespondSuccess(t *testing.T) {\n4012: \tapi := API{\n4013: \t\tlogger: promslog.NewNopLogger(),\n4014: \t}\n4015: \n4016: \tapi.ClearCodecs()\n4017: \tapi.InstallCodec(JSONCodec{})\n4018: \tapi.InstallCodec(&testCodec{contentType: MIMEType{\"test\", \"cannot-encode\"}, canEncode: false})\n4019: \tapi.InstallCodec(&testCodec{contentType: MIMEType{\"test\", \"can-encode\"}, canEncode: true})\n4020: \tapi.InstallCodec(&testCodec{contentType: MIMEType{\"test\", \"can-encode-2\"}, canEncode: true})\n4021: \n4022: \ts := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n4023: \t\tapi.respond(w, r, \"test\", nil, \"\")\n4024: \t}))\n4025: \tdefer s.Close()\n4026: \n4027: \tfor _, tc := range []struct {\n4028: \t\tname                string\n4029: \t\tacceptHeader        string\n4030: \t\texpectedContentType string\n4031: \t\texpectedBody        string\n4032: \t}{\n4033: \t\t{\n4034: \t\t\tname:                \"no Accept header\",\n4035: \t\t\texpectedContentType: \"application/json\",\n4036: \t\t\texpectedBody:        `{\"status\":\"success\",\"data\":\"test\"}`,\n4037: \t\t},\n4038: \t\t{\n4039: \t\t\tname:                \"Accept header with single content type which is suitable\",\n4040: \t\t\tacceptHeader:        \"test/can-encode\",\n4041: \t\t\texpectedContentType: \"test/can-encode\",\n4042: \t\t\texpectedBody:        `response from test/can-encode codec`,\n4043: \t\t},\n4044: \t\t{\n4045: \t\t\tname:                \"Accept header with single content type which is not available\",\n4046: \t\t\tacceptHeader:        \"test/not-registered\",\n4047: \t\t\texpectedContentType: \"application/json\",\n4048: \t\t\texpectedBody:        `{\"status\":\"success\",\"data\":\"test\"}`,\n4049: \t\t},\n4050: \t\t{\n4051: \t\t\tname:                \"Accept header with single content type which cannot encode the response payload\",\n4052: \t\t\tacceptHeader:        \"test/cannot-encode\",\n4053: \t\t\texpectedContentType: \"application/json\",\n4054: \t\t\texpectedBody:        `{\"status\":\"success\",\"data\":\"test\"}`,\n4055: \t\t},\n4056: \t\t{\n4057: \t\t\tname:                \"Accept header with multiple content types, all of which are suitable\",\n4058: \t\t\tacceptHeader:        \"test/can-encode, test/can-encode-2\",\n4059: \t\t\texpectedContentType: \"test/can-encode\",\n4060: \t\t\texpectedBody:        `response from test/can-encode codec`,\n4061: \t\t},\n4062: \t\t{\n4063: \t\t\tname:                \"Accept header with multiple content types, only one of which is available\",\n4064: \t\t\tacceptHeader:        \"test/not-registered, test/can-encode\",\n4065: \t\t\texpectedContentType: \"test/can-encode\",\n4066: \t\t\texpectedBody:        `response from test/can-encode codec`,\n4067: \t\t},\n4068: \t\t{\n4069: \t\t\tname:                \"Accept header with multiple content types, only one of which can encode the response payload\",\n4070: \t\t\tacceptHeader:        \"test/cannot-encode, test/can-encode\",\n4071: \t\t\texpectedContentType: \"test/can-encode\",\n4072: \t\t\texpectedBody:        `response from test/can-encode codec`,\n4073: \t\t},\n4074: \t\t{\n4075: \t\t\tname:                \"Accept header with multiple content types, none of which are available\",\n4076: \t\t\tacceptHeader:        \"test/not-registered, test/also-not-registered\",\n4077: \t\t\texpectedContentType: \"application/json\",\n4078: \t\t\texpectedBody:        `{\"status\":\"success\",\"data\":\"test\"}`,\n4079: \t\t},\n4080: \t} {\n4081: \t\tt.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n4082: \t\t\treq, err := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, s.URL, nil)\n4083: \t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n4084: \n4085: \t\t\tif tc.acceptHeader != \"\" {\n4086: \t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Accept\", tc.acceptHeader)\n4087: \t\t\t}\n4088: \n4089: \t\t\tresp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)\n4090: \t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n4091: \n4092: \t\t\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n4093: \t\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n4094: \t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n4095: \n4096: \t\t\trequire.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, resp.StatusCode)\n4097: \t\t\trequire.Equal(t, tc.expectedContentType, resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\"))\n4098: \t\t\trequire.Equal(t, tc.expectedBody, string(body))\n4099: \t\t})\n4100: \t}\n4101: }\n4102: \n4103: func TestRespondSuccess_DefaultCodecCannotEncodeResponse(t *testing.T) {\n4104: \tapi := API{\n4105: \t\tlogger: promslog.NewNopLogger(),\n4106: \t}\n4107: \n4108: \tapi.ClearCodecs()\n4109: \tapi.InstallCodec(&testCodec{contentType: MIMEType{\"application\", \"default-format\"}, canEncode: false})\n4110: \n4111: \ts := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n4112: \t\tapi.respond(w, r, \"test\", nil, \"\")\n4113: \t}))\n4114: \tdefer s.Close()\n4115: \n4116: \treq, err := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, s.URL, nil)\n4117: \trequire.NoError(t, err)\n4118: \n4119: \tresp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)\n4120: \trequire.NoError(t, err)\n4121: \n4122: \tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n4123: \tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n4124: \trequire.NoError(t, err)\n4125: \n4126: \trequire.Equal(t, http.StatusNotAcceptable, resp.StatusCode)\n4127: \trequire.Equal(t, \"application/json\", resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\"))\n4128: \trequire.JSONEq(t, `{\"status\":\"error\",\"errorType\":\"not_acceptable\",\"error\":\"cannot encode response as application/default-format\"}`, string(body))\n4129: }\n4130: \n4131: func TestServeTSDBBlocks(t *testing.T) {\n4132: \tblockMeta := tsdb.BlockMeta{\n4133: \t\tULID:    ulid.MustNew(ulid.Now(), nil),\n4134: \t\tMinTime: 0,\n4135: \t\tMaxTime: 1000,\n4136: \t\tStats: tsdb.BlockStats{\n4137: \t\t\tNumSeries: 10,\n4138: \t\t},\n4139: \t}\n4140: \n4141: \tdb := &fakeDB{\n4142: \t\tblockMetas: []tsdb.BlockMeta{blockMeta},\n4143: \t}\n4144: \n4145: \tapi := &API{\n4146: \t\tdb: db,\n4147: \t}\n4148: \n4149: \treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/api/v1/status/tsdb/blocks\", nil)\n4150: \tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n4151: \n4152: \tresult := api.serveTSDBBlocks(req)\n4153: \n4154: \tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(result.data)\n4155: \n4156: \tresp := w.Result()\n4157: \tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n4158: \n4159: \trequire.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, resp.StatusCode)\n4160: \n4161: \tvar resultData struct {\n4162: \t\tBlocks []tsdb.BlockMeta `json:\"blocks\"`\n4163: \t}\n4164: \terr := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&resultData)\n4165: \trequire.NoError(t, err)\n4166: \trequire.Len(t, resultData.Blocks, 1)\n4167: \trequire.Equal(t, blockMeta, resultData.Blocks[0])\n4168: }\n4169: \n4170: func TestRespondError(t *testing.T) {\n4171: \ts := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, _ *http.Request) {\n4172: \t\tapi := API{}\n4173: \t\tapi.respondError(w, &apiError{errorTimeout, errors.New(\"message\")}, \"test\")\n4174: \t}))\n4175: \tdefer s.Close()\n4176: \n4177: \tresp, err := http.Get(s.URL)\n4178: \trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Error on test request\")\n4179: \tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n4180: \tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n4181: \trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Error reading response body\")\n4182: \twant, have := http.StatusServiceUnavailable, resp.StatusCode\n4183: \trequire.Equal(t, want, have, \"Return code %d expected in error response but got %d\", want, have)\n4184: \th := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n4185: \trequire.Equal(t, \"application/json\", h, \"Expected Content-Type %q but got %q\", \"application/json\", h)\n4186: \trequire.JSONEq(t, `{\"status\": \"error\", \"data\": \"test\", \"errorType\": \"timeout\", \"error\": \"message\"}`, string(body))\n4187: }\n4188: \n4189: func TestParseTimeParam(t *testing.T) {\n4190: \ttype resultType struct {\n4191: \t\tasTime  time.Time\n4192: \t\tasError func() error\n4193: \t}\n4194: \n4195: \tts, err := parseTime(\"1582468023986\")\n4196: \trequire.NoError(t, err)\n4197: \n4198: \ttests := []struct {\n4199: \t\tparamName    string\n4200: \t\tparamValue   string\n4201: \t\tdefaultValue time.Time\n4202: \t\tresult       resultType\n4203: \t}{\n4204: \t\t{ // When data is valid.\n4205: \t\t\tparamName:    \"start\",\n4206: \t\t\tparamValue:   \"1582468023986\",\n4207: \t\t\tdefaultValue: MinTime,\n4208: \t\t\tresult: resultType{\n4209: \t\t\t\tasTime:  ts,\n4210: \t\t\t\tasError: nil,\n4211: \t\t\t},\n4212: \t\t},\n4213: \t\t{ // When data is empty string.\n4214: \t\t\tparamName:    \"end\",\n4215: \t\t\tparamValue:   \"\",\n4216: \t\t\tdefaultValue: MaxTime,\n4217: \t\t\tresult: resultType{\n4218: \t\t\t\tasTime:  MaxTime,\n4219: \t\t\t\tasError: nil,\n4220: \t\t\t},\n4221: \t\t},\n4222: \t\t{ // When data is not valid.\n4223: \t\t\tparamName:    \"foo\",\n4224: \t\t\tparamValue:   \"baz\",\n4225: \t\t\tdefaultValue: MaxTime,\n4226: \t\t\tresult: resultType{\n4227: \t\t\t\tasTime: time.Time{},\n4228: \t\t\t\tasError: func() error {\n4229: \t\t\t\t\t_, err := parseTime(\"baz\")\n4230: \t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid time value for '%s': %w\", \"foo\", err)\n4231: \t\t\t\t},\n4232: \t\t\t},\n4233: \t\t},\n4234: \t}\n4235: \n4236: \tfor _, test := range tests {\n4237: \t\treq, err := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"localhost:42/foo?\"+test.paramName+\"=\"+test.paramValue, nil)\n4238: \t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n4239: \n4240: \t\tresult := test.result\n4241: \t\tasTime, err := parseTimeParam(req, test.paramName, test.defaultValue)\n4242: \n4243: \t\tif err != nil {\n4244: \t\t\trequire.EqualError(t, err, result.asError().Error())\n4245: \t\t} else {\n4246: \t\t\trequire.True(t, asTime.Equal(result.asTime), \"time as return value: %s not parsed correctly. Expected %s. Actual %s\", test.paramValue, result.asTime, asTime)\n4247: \t\t}\n4248: \t}\n4249: }\n4250: \n4251: func TestParseTime(t *testing.T) {\n4252: \tts, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, \"2015-06-03T13:21:58.555Z\")\n4253: \tif err != nil {\n4254: \t\tpanic(err)\n4255: \t}\n4256: \n4257: \ttests := []struct {\n4258: \t\tinput  string\n4259: \t\tfail   bool\n4260: \t\tresult time.Time\n4261: \t}{\n4262: \t\t{\n4263: \t\t\tinput: \"\",\n4264: \t\t\tfail:  true,\n4265: \t\t},\n4266: \t\t{\n4267: \t\t\tinput: \"abc\",\n4268: \t\t\tfail:  true,\n4269: \t\t},\n4270: \t\t{\n4271: \t\t\tinput: \"30s\",\n4272: \t\t\tfail:  true,\n4273: \t\t},\n4274: \t\t{\n4275: \t\t\tinput:  \"123\",\n4276: \t\t\tresult: time.Unix(123, 0),\n4277: \t\t},\n4278: \t\t{\n4279: \t\t\tinput:  \"123.123\",\n4280: \t\t\tresult: time.Unix(123, 123000000),\n4281: \t\t},\n4282: \t\t{\n4283: \t\t\tinput:  \"2015-06-03T13:21:58.555Z\",\n4284: \t\t\tresult: ts,\n4285: \t\t},\n4286: \t\t{\n4287: \t\t\tinput:  \"2015-06-03T14:21:58.555+01:00\",\n4288: \t\t\tresult: ts,\n4289: \t\t},\n4290: \t\t{\n4291: \t\t\t// Test float rounding.\n4292: \t\t\tinput:  \"1543578564.705\",\n4293: \t\t\tresult: time.Unix(1543578564, 705*1e6),\n4294: \t\t},\n4295: \t\t{\n4296: \t\t\tinput:  MinTime.Format(time.RFC3339Nano),\n4297: \t\t\tresult: MinTime,\n4298: \t\t},\n4299: \t\t{\n4300: \t\t\tinput:  MaxTime.Format(time.RFC3339Nano),\n4301: \t\t\tresult: MaxTime,\n4302: \t\t},\n4303: \t}\n4304: \n4305: \tfor _, test := range tests {\n4306: \t\tts, err := parseTime(test.input)\n4307: \t\tif !test.fail {\n4308: \t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Unexpected error for %q\", test.input)\n4309: \t\t\trequire.NotNil(t, ts)\n4310: \t\t\trequire.True(t, ts.Equal(test.result), \"Expected time %v for input %q but got %v\", test.result, test.input, ts)\n4311: \t\t\tcontinue\n4312: \t\t}\n4313: \t\trequire.Error(t, err, \"Expected error for %q but got none\", test.input)\n4314: \t}\n4315: }\n4316: \n4317: func TestParseDuration(t *testing.T) {\n4318: \ttests := []struct {\n4319: \t\tinput  string\n4320: \t\tfail   bool\n4321: \t\tresult time.Duration\n4322: \t}{\n4323: \t\t{\n4324: \t\t\tinput: \"\",\n4325: \t\t\tfail:  true,\n4326: \t\t}, {\n4327: \t\t\tinput: \"abc\",\n4328: \t\t\tfail:  true,\n4329: \t\t}, {\n4330: \t\t\tinput: \"2015-06-03T13:21:58.555Z\",\n4331: \t\t\tfail:  true,\n4332: \t\t}, {\n4333: \t\t\t// Internal int64 overflow.\n4334: \t\t\tinput: \"-148966367200.372\",\n4335: \t\t\tfail:  true,\n4336: \t\t}, {\n4337: \t\t\t// Internal int64 overflow.\n4338: \t\t\tinput: \"148966367200.372\",\n4339: \t\t\tfail:  true,\n4340: \t\t}, {\n4341: \t\t\tinput:  \"123\",\n4342: \t\t\tresult: 123 * time.Second,\n4343: \t\t}, {\n4344: \t\t\tinput:  \"123.333\",\n4345: \t\t\tresult: 123*time.Second + 333*time.Millisecond,\n4346: \t\t}, {\n4347: \t\t\tinput:  \"15s\",\n4348: \t\t\tresult: 15 * time.Second,\n4349: \t\t}, {\n4350: \t\t\tinput:  \"5m\",\n4351: \t\t\tresult: 5 * time.Minute,\n4352: \t\t},\n4353: \t}\n4354: \n4355: \tfor _, test := range tests {\n4356: \t\td, err := parseDuration(test.input)\n4357: \t\tif !test.fail {\n4358: \t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Unexpected error for %q\", test.input)\n4359: \t\t\trequire.Equal(t, test.result, d, \"Expected duration %v for input %q but got %v\", test.result, test.input, d)\n4360: \t\t\tcontinue\n4361: \t\t}\n4362: \t\trequire.Error(t, err, \"Expected error for %q but got none\", test.input)\n4363: \t}\n4364: }\n4365: \n4366: func TestOptionsMethod(t *testing.T) {\n4367: \tr := route.New()\n4368: \tapi := &API{ready: func(f http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc { return f }}\n4369: \tapi.Register(r)\n4370: \n4371: \ts := httptest.NewServer(r)\n4372: \tdefer s.Close()\n4373: \n4374: \treq, err := http.NewRequest(http.MethodOptions, s.URL+\"/any_path\", nil)\n4375: \trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Error creating OPTIONS request\")\n4376: \tclient := &http.Client{}\n4377: \tresp, err := client.Do(req)\n4378: \trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Error executing OPTIONS request\")\n4379: \trequire.Equal(t, http.StatusNoContent, resp.StatusCode)\n4380: }\n4381: \n4382: func TestTSDBStatus(t *testing.T) {\n4383: \ttsdb := &fakeDB{}\n4384: \ttsdbStatusAPI := func(api *API) apiFunc { return api.serveTSDBStatus }\n4385: \n4386: \tfor i, tc := range []struct {\n4387: \t\tdb       *fakeDB\n4388: \t\tendpoint func(api *API) apiFunc\n4389: \t\tmethod   string\n4390: \t\tvalues   url.Values\n4391: \n4392: \t\terrType errorType\n4393: \t}{\n4394: \t\t// Tests for the TSDB Status endpoint.\n4395: \t\t{\n4396: \t\t\tdb:       tsdb,\n4397: \t\t\tendpoint: tsdbStatusAPI,\n4398: \t\t\terrType:  errorNone,\n4399: \t\t},\n4400: \t\t{\n4401: \t\t\tdb:       tsdb,\n4402: \t\t\tendpoint: tsdbStatusAPI,\n4403: \t\t\tvalues:   map[string][]string{\"limit\": {\"20\"}},\n4404: \t\t\terrType:  errorNone,\n4405: \t\t},\n4406: \t\t{\n4407: \t\t\tdb:       tsdb,\n4408: \t\t\tendpoint: tsdbStatusAPI,\n4409: \t\t\tvalues:   map[string][]string{\"limit\": {\"0\"}},\n4410: \t\t\terrType:  errorBadData,\n4411: \t\t},\n4412: \t} {\n4413: \t\tt.Run(strconv.Itoa(i), func(t *testing.T) {\n4414: \t\t\tapi := &API{db: tc.db, gatherer: prometheus.DefaultGatherer}\n4415: \t\t\tendpoint := tc.endpoint(api)\n4416: \t\t\treq, err := http.NewRequest(tc.method, fmt.Sprintf(\"?%s\", tc.values.Encode()), nil)\n4417: \t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Error when creating test request\")\n4418: \t\t\tres := endpoint(req)\n4419: \t\t\tassertAPIError(t, res.err, tc.errType)\n4420: \t\t})\n4421: \t}\n4422: }\n4423: \n4424: func TestReturnAPIError(t *testing.T) {\n4425: \tcases := []struct {\n4426: \t\terr      error\n4427: \t\texpected errorType\n4428: \t}{\n4429: \t\t{\n4430: \t\t\terr:      promql.ErrStorage{Err: errors.New(\"storage error\")},\n4431: \t\t\texpected: errorInternal,\n4432: \t\t}, {\n4433: \t\t\terr:      fmt.Errorf(\"wrapped: %w\", promql.ErrStorage{Err: errors.New(\"storage error\")}),\n4434: \t\t\texpected: errorInternal,\n4435: \t\t}, {\n4436: \t\t\terr:      promql.ErrQueryTimeout(\"timeout error\"),\n4437: \t\t\texpected: errorTimeout,\n4438: \t\t}, {\n4439: \t\t\terr:      fmt.Errorf(\"wrapped: %w\", promql.ErrQueryTimeout(\"timeout error\")),\n4440: \t\t\texpected: errorTimeout,\n4441: \t\t}, {\n4442: \t\t\terr:      promql.ErrQueryCanceled(\"canceled error\"),\n4443: \t\t\texpected: errorCanceled,\n4444: \t\t}, {\n4445: \t\t\terr:      fmt.Errorf(\"wrapped: %w\", promql.ErrQueryCanceled(\"canceled error\")),\n4446: \t\t\texpected: errorCanceled,\n4447: \t\t}, {\n4448: \t\t\terr:      errors.New(\"exec error\"),\n4449: \t\t\texpected: errorExec,\n4450: \t\t}, {\n4451: \t\t\terr:      context.Canceled,\n4452: \t\t\texpected: errorCanceled,\n4453: \t\t},\n4454: \t}\n4455: \n4456: \tfor ix, c := range cases {\n4457: \t\tactual := returnAPIError(c.err)\n4458: \t\trequire.Error(t, actual, ix)\n4459: \t\trequire.Equal(t, c.expected, actual.typ, ix)\n4460: \t}\n4461: }\n4462: \n4463: // This is a global to avoid the benchmark being optimized away.\n4464: var testResponseWriter = httptest.ResponseRecorder{}\n4465: \n4466: func BenchmarkRespond(b *testing.B) {\n4467: \tpoints := []promql.FPoint{}\n4468: \tfor i := range 10000 {\n4469: \t\tpoints = append(points, promql.FPoint{F: float64(i * 1000000), T: int64(i)})\n4470: \t}\n4471: \tmatrix := promql.Matrix{}\n4472: \tfor i := range 1000 {\n4473: \t\tmatrix = append(matrix, promql.Series{\n4474: \t\t\tMetric: labels.FromStrings(\"__name__\", fmt.Sprintf(\"series%v\", i),\n4475: \t\t\t\t\"label\", fmt.Sprintf(\"series%v\", i),\n4476: \t\t\t\t\"label2\", fmt.Sprintf(\"series%v\", i)),\n4477: \t\t\tFloats: points[:10],\n4478: \t\t})\n4479: \t}\n4480: \tseries := []labels.Labels{}\n4481: \tfor i := range 1000 {\n4482: \t\tseries = append(series, labels.FromStrings(\"__name__\", fmt.Sprintf(\"series%v\", i),\n4483: \t\t\t\"label\", fmt.Sprintf(\"series%v\", i),\n4484: \t\t\t\"label2\", fmt.Sprintf(\"series%v\", i)))\n4485: \t}\n4486: \n4487: \tcases := []struct {\n4488: \t\tname     string\n4489: \t\tresponse any\n4490: \t}{\n4491: \t\t{name: \"10000 points no labels\", response: &QueryData{\n4492: \t\t\tResultType: parser.ValueTypeMatrix,\n4493: \t\t\tResult: promql.Matrix{\n4494: \t\t\t\tpromql.Series{\n4495: \t\t\t\t\tFloats: points,\n4496: \t\t\t\t\tMetric: labels.EmptyLabels(),\n4497: \t\t\t\t},\n4498: \t\t\t},\n4499: \t\t}},\n4500: \t\t{name: \"1000 labels\", response: series},\n4501: \t\t{name: \"1000 series 10 points\", response: &QueryData{\n4502: \t\t\tResultType: parser.ValueTypeMatrix,\n4503: \t\t\tResult:     matrix,\n4504: \t\t}},\n4505: \t}\n4506: \tfor _, c := range cases {\n4507: \t\tb.Run(c.name, func(b *testing.B) {\n4508: \t\t\tb.ReportAllocs()\n4509: \t\t\trequest, err := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/does-not-matter\", nil)\n4510: \t\t\trequire.NoError(b, err)\n4511: \t\t\tb.ResetTimer()\n4512: \t\t\tapi := API{}\n4513: \t\t\tapi.InstallCodec(JSONCodec{})\n4514: \t\t\tfor n := 0; n < b.N; n++ {\n4515: \t\t\t\tapi.respond(&testResponseWriter, request, c.response, nil, \"\")\n4516: \t\t\t}\n4517: \t\t})\n4518: \t}\n4519: }\n4520: \n4521: func TestGetGlobalURL(t *testing.T) {\n4522: \tmustParseURL := func(t *testing.T, u string) *url.URL {\n4523: \t\tparsed, err := url.Parse(u)\n4524: \t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n4525: \t\treturn parsed\n4526: \t}\n4527: \n4528: \ttestcases := []struct {\n4529: \t\tinput    *url.URL\n4530: \t\topts     GlobalURLOptions\n4531: \t\texpected *url.URL\n4532: \t\terrorful bool\n4533: \t}{\n4534: \t\t{\n4535: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://127.0.0.1:9090\"),\n4536: \t\t\tGlobalURLOptions{\n4537: \t\t\t\tListenAddress: \"127.0.0.1:9090\",\n4538: \t\t\t\tHost:          \"127.0.0.1:9090\",\n4539: \t\t\t\tScheme:        \"http\",\n4540: \t\t\t},\n4541: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://127.0.0.1:9090\"),\n4542: \t\t\tfalse,\n4543: \t\t},\n4544: \t\t{\n4545: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://127.0.0.1:9090\"),\n4546: \t\t\tGlobalURLOptions{\n4547: \t\t\t\tListenAddress: \"127.0.0.1:9090\",\n4548: \t\t\t\tHost:          \"prometheus.io\",\n4549: \t\t\t\tScheme:        \"https\",\n4550: \t\t\t},\n4551: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"https://prometheus.io\"),\n4552: \t\t\tfalse,\n4553: \t\t},\n4554: \t\t{\n4555: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://example.com\"),\n4556: \t\t\tGlobalURLOptions{\n4557: \t\t\t\tListenAddress: \"127.0.0.1:9090\",\n4558: \t\t\t\tHost:          \"prometheus.io\",\n4559: \t\t\t\tScheme:        \"https\",\n4560: \t\t\t},\n4561: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://example.com\"),\n4562: \t\t\tfalse,\n4563: \t\t},\n4564: \t\t{\n4565: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://localhost:8080\"),\n4566: \t\t\tGlobalURLOptions{\n4567: \t\t\t\tListenAddress: \"127.0.0.1:9090\",\n4568: \t\t\t\tHost:          \"prometheus.io\",\n4569: \t\t\t\tScheme:        \"https\",\n4570: \t\t\t},\n4571: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://prometheus.io:8080\"),\n4572: \t\t\tfalse,\n4573: \t\t},\n4574: \t\t{\n4575: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://[::1]:8080\"),\n4576: \t\t\tGlobalURLOptions{\n4577: \t\t\t\tListenAddress: \"127.0.0.1:9090\",\n4578: \t\t\t\tHost:          \"prometheus.io\",\n4579: \t\t\t\tScheme:        \"https\",\n4580: \t\t\t},\n4581: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://prometheus.io:8080\"),\n4582: \t\t\tfalse,\n4583: \t\t},\n4584: \t\t{\n4585: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://localhost\"),\n4586: \t\t\tGlobalURLOptions{\n4587: \t\t\t\tListenAddress: \"127.0.0.1:9090\",\n4588: \t\t\t\tHost:          \"prometheus.io\",\n4589: \t\t\t\tScheme:        \"https\",\n4590: \t\t\t},\n4591: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://prometheus.io\"),\n4592: \t\t\tfalse,\n4593: \t\t},\n4594: \t\t{\n4595: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://localhost:9091\"),\n4596: \t\t\tGlobalURLOptions{\n4597: \t\t\t\tListenAddress: \"[::1]:9090\",\n4598: \t\t\t\tHost:          \"[::1]\",\n4599: \t\t\t\tScheme:        \"https\",\n4600: \t\t\t},\n4601: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://[::1]:9091\"),\n4602: \t\t\tfalse,\n4603: \t\t},\n4604: \t\t{\n4605: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://localhost:9091\"),\n4606: \t\t\tGlobalURLOptions{\n4607: \t\t\t\tListenAddress: \"[::1]:9090\",\n4608: \t\t\t\tHost:          \"[::1]:9090\",\n4609: \t\t\t\tScheme:        \"https\",\n4610: \t\t\t},\n4611: \t\t\tmustParseURL(t, \"http://[::1]:9091\"),\n4612: \t\t\tfalse,\n4613: \t\t},\n4614: \t}\n4615: \n4616: \tfor i, tc := range testcases {\n4617: \t\tt.Run(fmt.Sprintf(\"Test %d\", i), func(t *testing.T) {\n4618: \t\t\toutput, err := getGlobalURL(tc.input, tc.opts)\n4619: \t\t\tif tc.errorful {\n4620: \t\t\t\trequire.Error(t, err)\n4621: \t\t\t\treturn\n4622: \t\t\t}\n4623: \t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n4624: \t\t\trequire.Equal(t, tc.expected, output)\n4625: \t\t})\n4626: \t}\n4627: }\n4628: \n4629: type testCodec struct {\n4630: \tcontentType MIMEType\n4631: \tcanEncode   bool\n4632: }\n4633: \n4634: func (t *testCodec) ContentType() MIMEType {\n4635: \treturn t.contentType\n4636: }\n4637: \n4638: func (t *testCodec) CanEncode(*Response) bool {\n4639: \treturn t.canEncode\n4640: }\n4641: \n4642: func (t *testCodec) Encode(*Response) ([]byte, error) {\n4643: \treturn fmt.Appendf(nil, \"response from %v codec\", t.contentType), nil\n4644: }\n4645: \n4646: func TestExtractQueryOpts(t *testing.T) {\n4647: \ttests := []struct {\n4648: \t\tname   string\n4649: \t\tform   url.Values\n4650: \t\texpect promql.QueryOpts\n4651: \t\terr    error\n4652: \t}{\n4653: \t\t{\n4654: \t\t\tname: \"with stats all\",\n4655: \t\t\tform: url.Values{\n4656: \t\t\t\t\"stats\": []string{\"all\"},\n4657: \t\t\t},\n4658: \t\t\texpect: promql.NewPrometheusQueryOpts(true, 0),\n4659: \n4660: \t\t\terr: nil,\n4661: \t\t},\n4662: \t\t{\n4663: \t\t\tname: \"with stats none\",\n4664: \t\t\tform: url.Values{\n4665: \t\t\t\t\"stats\": []string{\"none\"},\n4666: \t\t\t},\n4667: \t\t\texpect: promql.NewPrometheusQueryOpts(false, 0),\n4668: \t\t\terr:    nil,\n4669: \t\t},\n4670: \t\t{\n4671: \t\t\tname: \"with lookback delta\",\n4672: \t\t\tform: url.Values{\n4673: \t\t\t\t\"stats\":          []string{\"all\"},\n4674: \t\t\t\t\"lookback_delta\": []string{\"30s\"},\n4675: \t\t\t},\n4676: \t\t\texpect: promql.NewPrometheusQueryOpts(true, 30*time.Second),\n4677: \t\t\terr:    nil,\n4678: \t\t},\n4679: \t\t{\n4680: \t\t\tname: \"with invalid lookback delta\",\n4681: \t\t\tform: url.Values{\n4682: \t\t\t\t\"lookback_delta\": []string{\"invalid\"},\n4683: \t\t\t},\n4684: \t\t\texpect: nil,\n4685: \t\t\terr:    errors.New(`error parsing lookback delta duration: cannot parse \"invalid\" to a valid duration`),\n4686: \t\t},\n4687: \t}\n4688: \n4689: \tfor _, test := range tests {\n4690: \t\tt.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n4691: \t\t\treq := &http.Request{Form: test.form}\n4692: \t\t\topts, err := extractQueryOpts(req)\n4693: \t\t\trequire.Equal(t, test.expect, opts)\n4694: \t\t\tif test.err == nil {\n4695: \t\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n4696: \t\t\t} else {\n4697: \t\t\t\trequire.EqualError(t, err, test.err.Error())\n4698: \t\t\t}\n4699: \t\t})\n4700: \t}\n4701: }\n4702: \n4703: // Test query timeout parameter.\n4704: func TestQueryTimeout(t *testing.T) {\n4705: \tstorage := promqltest.LoadedStorage(t, `\n4706: \t\tload 1m\n4707: \t\t\ttest_metric1{foo=\"bar\"} 0+100x100\n4708: \t`)\n4709: \tt.Cleanup(func() {\n4710: \t\t_ = storage.Close()\n4711: \t})\n4712: \n4713: \tnow := time.Now()\n4714: \n4715: \tfor _, tc := range []struct {\n4716: \t\tname   string\n4717: \t\tmethod string\n4718: \t}{\n4719: \t\t{\n4720: \t\t\tname:   \"GET method\",\n4721: \t\t\tmethod: http.MethodGet,\n4722: \t\t},\n4723: \t\t{\n4724: \t\t\tname:   \"POST method\",\n4725: \t\t\tmethod: http.MethodPost,\n4726: \t\t},\n4727: \t} {\n4728: \t\tt.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n4729: \t\t\tengine := &fakeEngine{}\n4730: \t\t\tapi := &API{\n4731: \t\t\t\tQueryable:             storage,\n4732: \t\t\t\tQueryEngine:           engine,\n4733: \t\t\t\tExemplarQueryable:     storage.ExemplarQueryable(),\n4734: \t\t\t\talertmanagerRetriever: testAlertmanagerRetriever{}.toFactory(),\n4735: \t\t\t\tflagsMap:              sampleFlagMap,\n4736: \t\t\t\tnow:                   func() time.Time { return now },\n4737: \t\t\t\tconfig:                func() config.Config { return samplePrometheusCfg },\n4738: \t\t\t\tready:                 func(f http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc { return f },\n4739: \t\t\t}\n4740: \n4741: \t\t\tquery := url.Values{\n4742: \t\t\t\t\"query\":   []string{\"2\"},\n4743: \t\t\t\t\"timeout\": []string{\"1s\"},\n4744: \t\t\t}\n4745: \t\t\tctx := context.Background()\n4746: \t\t\treq, err := http.NewRequest(tc.method, fmt.Sprintf(\"http://example.com?%s\", query.Encode()), nil)\n4747: \t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n4748: \t\t\treq.RemoteAddr = \"127.0.0.1:20201\"\n4749: \n4750: \t\t\tres := api.query(req.WithContext(ctx))\n4751: \t\t\tassertAPIError(t, res.err, errorNone)\n4752: \n4753: \t\t\trequire.Len(t, engine.query.execCalls, 1)\n4754: \t\t\tdeadline, ok := engine.query.execCalls[0].Deadline()\n4755: \t\t\trequire.True(t, ok)\n4756: \t\t\trequire.Equal(t, now.Add(time.Second), deadline)\n4757: \t\t})\n4758: \t}\n4759: }\n4760: \n4761: // fakeEngine is a fake QueryEngine implementation.\n4762: type fakeEngine struct {\n4763: \tquery fakeQuery\n4764: }\n4765: \n4766: func (e *fakeEngine) NewInstantQuery(context.Context, storage.Queryable, promql.QueryOpts, string, time.Time) (promql.Query, error) {\n4767: \treturn &e.query, nil\n4768: }\n4769: \n4770: func (e *fakeEngine) NewRangeQuery(context.Context, storage.Queryable, promql.QueryOpts, string, time.Time, time.Time, time.Duration) (promql.Query, error) {\n4771: \treturn &e.query, nil\n4772: }\n4773: \n4774: // fakeQuery is a fake Query implementation.\n4775: type fakeQuery struct {\n4776: \tquery     string\n4777: \texecCalls []context.Context\n4778: }\n4779: \n4780: func (q *fakeQuery) Exec(ctx context.Context) *promql.Result {\n4781: \tq.execCalls = append(q.execCalls, ctx)\n4782: \treturn &promql.Result{\n4783: \t\tValue: &parser.StringLiteral{\n4784: \t\t\tVal: \"test\",\n4785: \t\t},\n4786: \t}\n4787: }\n4788: \n4789: func (*fakeQuery) Close() {}\n4790: \n4791: func (*fakeQuery) Statement() parser.Statement {\n4792: \treturn nil\n4793: }\n4794: \n4795: func (*fakeQuery) Stats() *stats.Statistics {\n4796: \treturn nil\n4797: }\n4798: \n4799: func (*fakeQuery) Cancel() {}\n4800: \n4801: func (q *fakeQuery) String() string {\n4802: \treturn q.query\n4803: }\n4804: ",

provide the list of all scrape pools.\\n123: type ScrapePoolsRetriever interface {\\n124: \\tScrapePools() []string\\n125: }\\n126: \\n127: // TargetRetriever provides the list of active/dropped targets to scrape or not.\\n128: type TargetRetriever interface {\\n129: \\tTargetsActive() map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n130: \\tTargetsDropped() map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n131: \\tTargetsDroppedCounts() map[string]int\\n132: }\\n133: \\n134: // AlertmanagerRetriever provides a list of all/dropped AlertManager URLs.\\n135: type AlertmanagerRetriever interface {\\n136: \\tAlertmanagers() []*url.URL\\n137: \\tDroppedAlertmanagers() []*url.URL\\n138: }\\n139: \\n140: // RulesRetriever provides a list of active rules and alerts.\\n141: type RulesRetriever interface {\\n142: \\tRuleGroups() []*rules.Group\\n143: \\tAlertingRules() []*rules.AlertingRule\\n144: }\\n145: \\n146: // StatsRenderer converts engine statistics into a format suitable for the API.\\n147: type StatsRenderer func(context.Context, *stats.Statistics, string) stats.QueryStats\\n148: \\n149: // DefaultStatsRenderer is the default stats renderer for the API.\\n150: func DefaultStatsRenderer(_ context.Context, s *stats.Statistics, param string) stats.QueryStats {\\n151: \\tif param != \\\"\\\" {\\n152: \\t\\treturn stats.NewQueryStats(s)\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\treturn nil\\n155: }\\n156: \\n157: // PrometheusVersion contains build information about Prometheus.\\n158: type PrometheusVersion struct {\\n159: \\tVersion   string `json:\\\"version\\\"`\\n160: \\tRevision  string `json:\\\"revision\\\"`\\n161: \\tBranch    string `json:\\\"branch\\\"`\\n162: \\tBuildUser string `json:\\\"buildUser\\\"`\\n163: \\tBuildDate string `json:\\\"buildDate\\\"`\\n164: \\tGoVersion string `json:\\\"goVersion\\\"`\\n165: }\\n166: \\n167: // RuntimeInfo contains runtime information about Prometheus.\\n168: type RuntimeInfo struct {\\n169: \\tStartTime           time.Time `json:\\\"startTime\\\"`\\n170: \\tCWD                 string    `json:\\\"CWD\\\"`\\n171: \\tHostname            string    `json:\\\"hostname\\\"`\\n172: \\tServerTime          time.Time `json:\\\"serverTime\\\"`\\n173: \\tReloadConfigSuccess bool      `json:\\\"reloadConfigSuccess\\\"`\\n174: \\tLastConfigTime      time.Time `json:\\\"lastConfigTime\\\"`\\n175: \\tCorruptionCount     int64     `json:\\\"corruptionCount\\\"`\\n176: \\tGoroutineCount      int       `json:\\\"goroutineCount\\\"`\\n177: \\tGOMAXPROCS struct {\\n186: \\tStatus    status   `json:\\\"status\\\"`\\n187: \\tData      any      `json:\\\"data,omitempty\\\"`\\n188: \\tErrorType string   `json:\\\"errorType,omitempty\\\"`\\n189: \\tError apiFuncResult struct {\\n195: \\tdata      any\\n196: \\terr       *apiError\\n197: \\twarnings  annotations.Annotations\\n198: \\tfinalizer func()\\n199: }\\n200: \\n201: type apiFunc func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult\\n202: \\n203: // TSDBAdminStats defines the tsdb interfaces used by the v1 API for admin operations as well as statistics.\\n204: type TSDBAdminStats interface {\\n205: \\tCleanTombstones() error\\n206: \\tDelete(ctx context.Context, mint, maxt int64, ms ...*labels.Matcher) error\\n207: \\tSnapshot(dir string, withHead bool) error\\n208: \\tStats(statsByLabelName string, limit int) (*tsdb.Stats, error)\\n209: \\tWALReplayStatus() (tsdb.WALReplayStatus, error)\\n210: \\tBlockMetas() ([]tsdb.BlockMeta, error)\\n211: }\\n212: \\n213: type QueryOpts interface {\\n214: \\tEnablePerStepStats() bool\\n215: \\tLookbackDelta() time.Duration\\n216: }\\n217: \\n218: // API can register a set of endpoints in a router and handle\\n219: // them using the provided storage and query engine.\\n220: type API struct {\\n221: \\tQueryable         storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable\\n222: \\tQueryEngine       promql.QueryEngine\\n223: \\tExemplarQueryable storage.ExemplarQueryable\\n224: \\n225: \\tscrapePoolsRetriever  func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever\\n226: \\ttargetRetriever       func(context.Context) TargetRetriever\\n227: \\talertmanagerRetriever func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever\\n228: \\trulesRetriever        func(context.Context) RulesRetriever\\n229: \\tnow                   func() time.Time\\n230: \\tconfig                func() config.Config\\n231: \\tflagsMap              map[string]string\\n232: \\tready                 func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc\\n233: \\tglobalURLOptions \\tCORSOrigin          *regexp.Regexp\\n240: \\tbuildInfo           *PrometheusVersion\\n241: \\truntimeInfo         func() (RuntimeInfo, error)\\n242: \\tgatherer            prometheus.Gatherer\\n243: \\tisAgent             bool\\n244: \\tstatsRenderer       StatsRenderer\\n245: \\tnotificationsGetter func() []notifications.Notification\\n246: \\tnotificationsSub http.Handler\\n251: \\tremoteReadHandler  http.Handler\\n252: \\totlpWriteHandler   http.Handler\\n253: \\n254: \\tcodecs []Codec\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: // NewAPI returns an initialized API type.\\n258: func NewAPI(\\n259: \\tqe promql.QueryEngine,\\n260: \\tq storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable,\\n261: \\tap storage.Appendable,\\n262: \\teq storage.ExemplarQueryable,\\n263: \\tspsr func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever,\\n264: \\ttr func(context.Context) TargetRetriever,\\n265: \\tar func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever,\\n266: \\tconfigFunc func() config.Config,\\n267: \\tflagsMap map[string]string,\\n268: \\tglobalURLOptions GlobalURLOptions,\\n269: \\treadyFunc func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc,\\n270: \\tdb TSDBAdminStats,\\n271: int,\\n277: \\tremoteReadMaxBytesInFrame int,\\n278: \\tisAgent bool,\\n279: \\tcorsOrigin *regexp.Regexp,\\n280: \\truntimeInfo func() (RuntimeInfo, error),\\n281: \\tbuildInfo *PrometheusVersion,\\n282: \\tnotificationsGetter func() []notifications.Notification,\\n283: \\tnotificationsSub func() (<-chan notifications.Notification, func(), bool),\\n284: \\tgatherer prometheus.Gatherer,\\n285: \\tregisterer prometheus.Registerer,\\n286: \\tstatsRenderer StatsRenderer,\\n287: \\trwEnabled bool,\\n288: \\tacceptRemoteWriteProtoMsgs []config.RemoteWriteProtoMsg,\\n289: \\totlpEnabled, otlpDeltaToCumulative, otlpNativeDeltaIngestion bool,\\n290: \\tctZeroIngestionEnabled bool,\\n291: \\tlookbackDelta time.Duration,\\n292: \\tenableTypeAndUnitLabels bool,\\n293: \\toverrideErrorCode OverrideErrorCode,\\n294: ) *API {\\n295: \\ta := &API{\\n296: \\t\\tQueryEngine:       qe,\\n297: \\t\\tQueryable:         q,\\n298: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/web/api/v1/api.go%3FL1-300\"}",

    "content": "1: // Copyright 2016 The Prometheus Authors\n2: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n3: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n4: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\n5: //\n6: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n7: //\n8: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n9: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n10: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n11: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n12: // limitations under the License.\n13: \n14: package v1\n15: \n16: import (\n17: \t\"context\"\n18: \t\"crypto/sha1\"\n19: \t\"encoding/hex\"\n20: \t\"encoding/json\"\n21: \t\"errors\"\n22: \t\"fmt\"\n23: \t\"log/slog\"\n24: \t\"math\"\n25: \t\"math/rand\"\n26: \t\"net\"\n27: \t\"net/http\"\n28: \t\"net/url\"\n29: \t\"os\"\n30: \t\"path/filepath\"\n31: \t\"slices\"\n32: \t\"sort\"\n33: \t\"strconv\"\n34: \t\"strings\"\n35: \t\"time\"\n36: \n37: \t\"github.com/grafana/regexp\"\n38: \tjsoniter \"github.com/json-iterator/go\"\n39: \t\"github.com/munnerz/goautoneg\"\n40: \t\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus\"\n41: \t\"github.com/prometheus/common/model\"\n42: \t\"github.com/prometheus/common/route\"\n43: \n44: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/config\"\n45: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels\"\n46: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/metadata\"\n47: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/timestamp\"\n48: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql\"\n49: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser\"\n50: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/rules\"\n51: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/scrape\"\n52: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage\"\n53: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage/remote\"\n54: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb\"\n55: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/index\"\n56: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/annotations\"\n57: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/httputil\"\n58: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/notifications\"\n59: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/stats\"\n60: )\n61: \n62: type status string\n63: \n64: const (\n65: \tstatusSuccess status = \"success\"\n66: \tstatusError   status = \"error\"\n67: \n68: \t// Non-standard status code (originally introduced by nginx) for the case when a client closes\n69: \t// the connection while the server is still processing the request.\n70: \tstatusClientClosedConnection = 499\n71: \n72: \t// checkContextEveryNIterations is used in some tight loops to check if the context is done.\n73: \tcheckContextEveryNIterations = 128\n74: )\n75: \n76: type errorNum int\n77: \n78: type errorType struct {\n79: \tnum errorNum\n80: \tstr string\n81: }\n82: \n83: const (\n84: \tErrorNone errorNum = iota\n85: \tErrorTimeout\n86: \tErrorCanceled\n87: \tErrorExec\n88: \tErrorBadData\n89: \tErrorInternal\n90: \tErrorUnavailable\n91: \tErrorNotFound\n92: \tErrorNotAcceptable\n93: )\n94: \n95: var (\n96: \terrorNone          = errorType{ErrorNone, \"\"}\n97: \terrorTimeout       = errorType{ErrorTimeout, \"timeout\"}\n98: \terrorCanceled      = errorType{ErrorCanceled, \"canceled\"}\n99: \terrorExec          = errorType{ErrorExec, \"execution\"}\n100: \terrorBadData       = errorType{ErrorBadData, \"bad_data\"}\n101: \terrorInternal      = errorType{ErrorInternal, \"internal\"}\n102: \terrorUnavailable   = errorType{ErrorUnavailable, \"unavailable\"}\n103: \terrorNotFound      = errorType{ErrorNotFound, \"not_found\"}\n104: \terrorNotAcceptable = errorType{ErrorNotAcceptable, \"not_acceptable\"}\n105: )\n106: \n107: // OverrideErrorCode can be used to override status code for different error types.\n108: // Return false to fall back to default status code.\n109: type OverrideErrorCode func(errorNum, error) (code int, override bool)\n110: \n111: var LocalhostRepresentations = []string{\"127.0.0.1\", \"localhost\", \"::1\"}\n112: \n113: type apiError struct {\n114: \ttyp errorType\n115: \terr error\n116: }\n117: \n118: func (e *apiError) Error() string {\n119: \treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", e.typ.str, e.err)\n120: }\n121: \n122: // ScrapePoolsRetriever provide the list of all scrape pools.\n123: type ScrapePoolsRetriever interface {\n124: \tScrapePools() []string\n125: }\n126: \n127: // TargetRetriever provides the list of active/dropped targets to scrape or not.\n128: type TargetRetriever interface {\n129: \tTargetsActive() map[string][]*scrape.Target\n130: \tTargetsDropped() map[string][]*scrape.Target\n131: \tTargetsDroppedCounts() map[string]int\n132: }\n133: \n134: // AlertmanagerRetriever provides a list of all/dropped AlertManager URLs.\n135: type AlertmanagerRetriever interface {\n136: \tAlertmanagers() []*url.URL\n137: \tDroppedAlertmanagers() []*url.URL\n138: }\n139: \n140: // RulesRetriever provides a list of active rules and alerts.\n141: type RulesRetriever interface {\n142: \tRuleGroups() []*rules.Group\n143: \tAlertingRules() []*rules.AlertingRule\n144: }\n145: \n146: // StatsRenderer converts engine statistics into a format suitable for the API.\n147: type StatsRenderer func(context.Context, *stats.Statistics, string) stats.QueryStats\n148: \n149: // DefaultStatsRenderer is the default stats renderer for the API.\n150: func DefaultStatsRenderer(_ context.Context, s *stats.Statistics, param string) stats.QueryStats {\n151: \tif param != \"\" {\n152: \t\treturn stats.NewQueryStats(s)\n153: \t}\n154: \treturn nil\n155: }\n156: \n157: // PrometheusVersion contains build information about Prometheus.\n158: type PrometheusVersion struct {\n159: \tVersion   string `json:\"version\"`\n160: \tRevision  string `json:\"revision\"`\n161: \tBranch    string `json:\"branch\"`\n162: \tBuildUser string `json:\"buildUser\"`\n163: \tBuildDate string `json:\"buildDate\"`\n164: \tGoVersion string `json:\"goVersion\"`\n165: }\n166: \n167: // RuntimeInfo contains runtime information about Prometheus.\n168: type RuntimeInfo struct {\n169: \tStartTime           time.Time `json:\"startTime\"`\n170: \tCWD                 string    `json:\"CWD\"`\n171: \tHostname            string    `json:\"hostname\"`\n172: \tServerTime          time.Time `json:\"serverTime\"`\n173: \tReloadConfigSuccess bool      `json:\"reloadConfigSuccess\"`\n174: \tLastConfigTime      time.Time `json:\"lastConfigTime\"`\n175: \tCorruptionCount     int64     `json:\"corruptionCount\"`\n176: \tGoroutineCount      int       `json:\"goroutineCount\"`\n177: \tGOMAXPROCS          int       `json:\"GOMAXPROCS\"`\n178: \tGOMEMLIMIT          int64     `json:\"GOMEMLIMIT\"`\n179: \tGOGC                string    `json:\"GOGC\"`\n180: \tGODEBUG             string    `json:\"GODEBUG\"`\n181: \tStorageRetention    string    `json:\"storageRetention\"`\n182: }\n183: \n184: // Response contains a response to a HTTP API request.\n185: type Response struct {\n186: \tStatus    status   `json:\"status\"`\n187: \tData      any      `json:\"data,omitempty\"`\n188: \tErrorType string   `json:\"errorType,omitempty\"`\n189: \tError     string   `json:\"error,omitempty\"`\n190: \tWarnings  []string `json:\"warnings,omitempty\"`\n191: \tInfos     []string `json:\"infos,omitempty\"`\n192: }\n193: \n194: type apiFuncResult struct {\n195: \tdata      any\n196: \terr       *apiError\n197: \twarnings  annotations.Annotations\n198: \tfinalizer func()\n199: }\n200: \n201: type apiFunc func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult\n202: \n203: // TSDBAdminStats defines the tsdb interfaces used by the v1 API for admin operations as well as statistics.\n204: type TSDBAdminStats interface {\n205: \tCleanTombstones() error\n206: \tDelete(ctx context.Context, mint, maxt int64, ms ...*labels.Matcher) error\n207: \tSnapshot(dir string, withHead bool) error\n208: \tStats(statsByLabelName string, limit int) (*tsdb.Stats, error)\n209: \tWALReplayStatus() (tsdb.WALReplayStatus, error)\n210: \tBlockMetas() ([]tsdb.BlockMeta, error)\n211: }\n212: \n213: type QueryOpts interface {\n214: \tEnablePerStepStats() bool\n215: \tLookbackDelta() time.Duration\n216: }\n217: \n218: // API can register a set of endpoints in a router and handle\n219: // them using the provided storage and query engine.\n220: type API struct {\n221: \tQueryable         storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable\n222: \tQueryEngine       promql.QueryEngine\n223: \tExemplarQueryable storage.ExemplarQueryable\n224: \n225: \tscrapePoolsRetriever  func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever\n226: \ttargetRetriever       func(context.Context) TargetRetriever\n227: \talertmanagerRetriever func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever\n228: \trulesRetriever        func(context.Context) RulesRetriever\n229: \tnow                   func() time.Time\n230: \tconfig                func() config.Config\n231: \tflagsMap              map[string]string\n232: \tready                 func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc\n233: \tglobalURLOptions      GlobalURLOptions\n234: \n235: \tdb                  TSDBAdminStats\n236: \tdbDir               string\n237: \tenableAdmin         bool\n238: \tlogger              *slog.Logger\n239: \tCORSOrigin          *regexp.Regexp\n240: \tbuildInfo           *PrometheusVersion\n241: \truntimeInfo         func() (RuntimeInfo, error)\n242: \tgatherer            prometheus.Gatherer\n243: \tisAgent             bool\n244: \tstatsRenderer       StatsRenderer\n245: \tnotificationsGetter func() []notifications.Notification\n246: \tnotificationsSub    func() (<-chan notifications.Notification, func(), bool)\n247: \t// Allows customizing the default mapping\n248: \toverrideErrorCode OverrideErrorCode\n249: \n250: \tremoteWriteHandler http.Handler\n251: \tremoteReadHandler  http.Handler\n252: \totlpWriteHandler   http.Handler\n253: \n254: \tcodecs []Codec\n255: }\n256: \n257: // NewAPI returns an initialized API type.\n258: func NewAPI(\n259: \tqe promql.QueryEngine,\n260: \tq storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable,\n261: \tap storage.Appendable,\n262: \teq storage.ExemplarQueryable,\n263: \tspsr func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever,\n264: \ttr func(context.Context) TargetRetriever,\n265: \tar func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever,\n266: \tconfigFunc func() config.Config,\n267: \tflagsMap map[string]string,\n268: \tglobalURLOptions GlobalURLOptions,\n269: \treadyFunc func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc,\n270: \tdb TSDBAdminStats,\n271: \tdbDir string,\n272: \tenableAdmin bool,\n273: \tlogger *slog.Logger,\n274: \trr func(context.Context) RulesRetriever,\n275: \tremoteReadSampleLimit int,\n276: \tremoteReadConcurrencyLimit int,\n277: \tremoteReadMaxBytesInFrame int,\n278: \tisAgent bool,\n279: \tcorsOrigin *regexp.Regexp,\n280: \truntimeInfo func() (RuntimeInfo, error),\n281: \tbuildInfo *PrometheusVersion,\n282: \tnotificationsGetter func() []notifications.Notification,\n283: \tnotificationsSub func() (<-chan notifications.Notification, func(), bool),\n284: \tgatherer prometheus.Gatherer,\n285: \tregisterer prometheus.Registerer,\n286: \tstatsRenderer StatsRenderer,\n287: \trwEnabled bool,\n288: \tacceptRemoteWriteProtoMsgs []config.RemoteWriteProtoMsg,\n289: \totlpEnabled, otlpDeltaToCumulative, otlpNativeDeltaIngestion bool,\n290: \tctZeroIngestionEnabled bool,\n291: \tlookbackDelta time.Duration,\n292: \tenableTypeAndUnitLabels bool,\n293: \toverrideErrorCode OverrideErrorCode,\n294: ) *API {\n295: \ta := &API{\n296: \t\tQueryEngine:       qe,\n297: \t\tQueryable:         q,\n298: \t\tExemplarQueryable: eq,\n299: \n300: \t\tscrapePoolsRetriever:  spsr,\n301: ",

20. Agent @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Find existing silence API handlers and test patterns",
  "prompt": "Search the github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4 repository for:\n1. Any existing silences endpoint handlers in web/api/v1\n2. Test files that might show the expected interface for bulk silences\n3. References to silence creation or Alertmanager integration\n4. Any files mentioning \"silences\" or \"silence\" in the path or content\n\nFocus on finding patterns for how the API handles requests and responses, especially for POST endpoints that accept JSON data.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a423e603c2c17eeb6",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile my findings into a final report.\n\n## Summary of Findings: Prometheus API v1 Web Endpoints\n\nBased on my search of the `github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4` repository, here are the key findings regarding API endpoint patterns, request/response handling, and Alertmanager integration:\n\n### 1. **API Endpoint Structure and Registration**\n\nThe Prometheus API v1 uses a route-based pattern for endpoint registration in `/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go`. Key patterns:\n\n**File**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go` (lines 371-459)\n\n```go\n// Register the API's endpoints in the given router.\nfunc (api *API) Register(r *route.Router) {\n  // Wrapping function for all endpoints\n  wrap := func(f apiFunc) http.HandlerFunc { ... }\n  \n  // Example endpoint registrations:\n  r.Get(\"/query\", wrapAgent(api.query))\n  r.Post(\"/query\", wrapAgent(api.query))\n  r.Get(\"/query_range\", wrapAgent(api.queryRange))\n  r.Post(\"/query_range\", wrapAgent(api.queryRange))\n  \n  // Admin endpoints using POST/PUT\n  r.Post(\"/admin/tsdb/delete_series\", wrapAgent(api.deleteSeries))\n  r.Post(\"/admin/tsdb/clean_tombstones\", wrapAgent(api.cleanTombstones))\n  r.Put(\"/admin/tsdb/snapshot\", wrapAgent(api.snapshot))\n  \n  // Other handlers (remote read/write)\n  r.Post(\"/read\", api.ready(api.remoteRead))\n  r.Post(\"/write\", api.ready(api.remoteWrite))\n}\n```\n\n### 2. **HTTP Method Handling Pattern**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api_test.go` (lines 3676-3694)\n\nThe API supports both GET and POST for certain endpoints. POST is used with form-encoded data:\n\n```go\n// Query endpoints support both methods\nmethods := func(f apiFunc) []string {\n  fp := reflect.ValueOf(f).Pointer()\n  if fp == reflect.ValueOf(api.query).Pointer() || \n     fp == reflect.ValueOf(api.queryRange).Pointer() || \n     fp == reflect.ValueOf(api.series).Pointer() {\n    return []string{http.MethodGet, http.MethodPost}\n  }\n  return []string{http.MethodGet}\n}\n\n// For POST requests, parameters are URL-encoded in the body\nrequest := func(m string, q url.Values) (*http.Request, error) {\n  if m == http.MethodPost {\n    r, err := http.NewRequest(m, \"http://example.com\", \n      strings.NewReader(q.Encode()))\n    r.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/x-www-form-urlencoded\")\n    r.RemoteAddr = \"127.0.0.1:20201\"\n    return r, err\n  }\n  // GET uses query string\n}\n```\n\n### 3. **Response Format Structure**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go` (lines 184-192, 2005-2083)\n\nAll API responses follow a consistent JSON structure with the `Response` type:\n\n```go\n// Response contains a response to a HTTP API request.\ntype Response struct {\n  Status    status   `json:\"status\"`\n  Data      any      `json:\"data,omitempty\"`\n  ErrorType string   `json:\"errorType,omitempty\"`\n  Error     string   `json:\"error,omitempty\"`\n  Warnings  []string `json:\"warnings,omitempty\"`\n  Infos     []string `json:\"infos,omitempty\"`\n}\n```\n\n**Success Response Function**:\n```go\nfunc (api *API) respond(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request, \n  data any, warnings annotations.Annotations, query string) {\n  statusMessage := statusSuccess\n  warn, info := warnings.AsStrings(query, 10, 10)\n  \n  resp := &Response{\n    Status:   statusMessage,\n    Data:     data,\n    Warnings: warn,\n    Infos:    info,\n  }\n  \n  // Encode based on content negotiation (JSON by default)\n  w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", codec.ContentType().String())\n  w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n  w.Write(encodedResponse)\n}\n```\n\n**Error Response Function**:\n```go\nfunc (api *API) respondError(w http.ResponseWriter, apiErr *apiError, data any) {\n  resp := &Response{\n    Status:    statusError,\n    ErrorType: apiErr.typ.str,\n    Error:     apiErr.err.Error(),\n    Data:      data,\n  }\n  \n  // Error codes mapped to HTTP status\n  w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n  w.WriteHeader(getDefaultErrorCode(apiErr.typ))\n  w.Write(encodedResponse)\n}\n```\n\nError type to HTTP status mapping:\n- `errorBadData` \u2192 400 Bad Request\n- `errorExec` \u2192 422 Unprocessable Entity\n- `errorTimeout` \u2192 503 Service Unavailable\n- `errorInternal` \u2192 500 Internal Server Error\n- `errorNotFound` \u2192 404 Not Found\n\n### 4. **apiFunc Pattern for Endpoint Handlers**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go` (lines 194-201)\n\nAll endpoint handlers follow a common function type:\n\n```go\ntype apiFuncResult struct {\n  data      any\n  err       *apiError\n  warnings  annotations.Annotations\n  finalizer func()\n}\n\ntype apiFunc func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult\n```\n\nExample endpoint implementation:\n```go\nfunc (api *API) deleteSeries(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n  if !api.enableAdmin {\n    return apiFuncResult{nil, \n      &apiError{errorUnavailable, errors.New(\"admin APIs disabled\")}, \n      nil, nil}\n  }\n  \n  if err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n    return apiFuncResult{nil, \n      &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, \n      nil, nil}\n  }\n  \n  // Process parameters and return result\n  return apiFuncResult{data, nil, warnings, finalizer}\n}\n```\n\n### 5. **Admin API Examples (POST/PUT Pattern)**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go` (lines 1925-1950)\n\nThe `/admin/tsdb/*` endpoints show the pattern for POST/PUT operations:\n\n```go\nfunc (api *API) deleteSeries(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n  // 1. Enable check\n  if !api.enableAdmin {\n    return apiFuncResult{nil, \n      &apiError{errorUnavailable, errors.New(\"admin APIs disabled\")}, \n      nil, nil}\n  }\n  \n  // 2. Form parsing\n  if err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n    return apiFuncResult{nil, \n      &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, \n      nil, nil}\n  }\n  \n  // 3. Validate parameters\n  if len(r.Form[\"match[]\"]) == 0 {\n    return apiFuncResult{nil, \n      &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"no match[] parameter provided\")}, \n      nil, nil}\n  }\n  \n  // 4. Parse time parameters\n  start, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n  if err != nil {\n    return invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n  }\n  \n  // 5. Execute operation\n  for _, s := range r.Form[\"match[]\"] {\n    matchers, err := parser.ParseMetricSelector(s)\n    if err != nil {\n      return invalidParamError(err, \"match[]\")\n    }\n    if err := api.db.Delete(r.Context(), timestamp.FromTime(start), \n      timestamp.FromTime(end), matchers...); err != nil {\n      // error handling\n    }\n  }\n  \n  // 6. Return success (no content)\n  return apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n```\n\n### 6. **Alertmanager Integration**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go` (lines 431, 2011-2016)\n\nPrometheus provides alertmanager discovery endpoint:\n\n```go\n// Endpoint registration\nr.Get(\"/alertmanagers\", wrapAgent(api.alertmanagers))\n\n// AlertmanagerDiscovery response type for API\ntype AlertmanagerDiscovery struct {\n  ActiveAlertmanagers []*AlertmanagerTarget `json:\"activeAlertmanagers\"`\n  DroppedAlertmanagers []*AlertmanagerTarget `json:\"droppedAlertmanagers\"`\n}\n\ntype AlertmanagerTarget struct {\n  URL string `json:\"url\"`\n}\n```\n\n**Important Finding**: The Prometheus API **does NOT have native silences endpoints**. Silences are managed by Alertmanager, not Prometheus. Prometheus only discovers and reports on Alertmanager instances.\n\n### 7. **NO Silences Endpoint in Prometheus API v1**\n\n**Critical Finding**: After thorough searching of the repository:\n- **No `/silences` endpoint** exists in `/web/api/v1/`\n- **No bulk silences creation** endpoint found\n- **No silence management** functionality in Prometheus\n\nThis is correct by design: **Alertmanager (not Prometheus) manages silences**. The relationship is:\n1. Prometheus generates alerts\n2. Prometheus sends alerts to Alertmanager\n3. Alertmanager manages silence policies (silence creation, updates)\n4. Prometheus only queries Alertmanager discovery\n\n### 8. **Test Pattern for Endpoint Testing**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api_test.go` (lines 1099-1122, 3696-3700)\n\n```go\ntype test struct {\n  endpoint              apiFunc\n  params                map[string]string\n  query                 url.Values\n  response              any\n  responseLen           int\n  responseAsJSON        string\n  warningsCount         int\n  errType               errorType\n  sorter                func(any)\n}\n\n// Test execution\nfor i, test := range tests {\n  t.Run(fmt.Sprintf(\"run %d %s %q\", i, describeAPIFunc(test.endpoint), \n    test.query.Encode()), func(t *testing.T) {\n    for _, method := range methods(test.endpoint) {\n      t.Run(method, func(t *testing.T) {\n        // Build request with correct params\n        req, _ := request(method, test.query)\n        \n        // Execute endpoint\n        result := test.endpoint(req)\n        \n        // Assert response\n        // ...\n      })\n    }\n  })\n}\n```\n\n### 9. **Key Files Referenced**\n\n1. **Main API implementation**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go`\n   - Endpoint registration (lines 371-459)\n   - Response handling (lines 2005-2083)\n   - Admin endpoints (lines 1925-1964)\n   - Error codes (lines 2085-2104)\n\n2. **Tests**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api_test.go`\n   - Test structure (lines 1099-1122)\n   - HTTP method handling (lines 3676-3694)\n   - Request execution (lines 3696-3700)\n\n3. **Configuration**: `/workspace/config/config.go`\n   - AlertmanagerConfig (lines 1197-1200)\n   - AlertmanagerAPIVersion (lines 1164-1167)\n\nThis structure makes it clear that for implementing a bulk silences endpoint, you would need to work with Alertmanager's API, not Prometheus's API.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Search the github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4 repository for:\n1. Any existing silences endpoint handlers in web/api/v1\n2. Test files that might show the expected interface for bulk silences\n3. References to silence creation or Alertmanager integration\n4. Any files mentioning \"silences\" or \"silence\" in the path or content\n\nFocus on finding patterns for how the API handles requests and responses, especially for POST endpoints that accept JSON data.",
  "status": "completed",
  "totalDurationMs": 65113,
  "totalTokens": 98388,
  "totalToolUseCount": 35,
  "usage": {

\\n122: // ScrapePoolsRetriever provide the list of all scrape pools.\\n123: type ScrapePoolsRetriever interface {\\n124: \\tScrapePools() []string\\n125: }\\n126: \\n127: // TargetRetriever provides the list of active/dropped targets to scrape or not.\\n128: type TargetRetriever interface {\\n129: \\tTargetsActive() map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n130: \\tTargetsDropped() map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n131: \\tTargetsDroppedCounts() map[string]int\\n132: }\\n133: \\n134: // AlertmanagerRetriever provides a list of all/dropped AlertManager URLs.\\n135: type alerts.\\n141: type RulesRetriever interface {\\n142: \\tRuleGroups() []*rules.Group\\n143: \\tAlertingRules() []*rules.AlertingRule\\n144: }\\n145: \\n146: // StatsRenderer converts engine statistics into a format suitable for the API.\\n147: type StatsRenderer func(context.Context, *stats.Statistics, string) stats.QueryStats\\n148: \\n149: // DefaultStatsRenderer is the default stats renderer for the API.\\n150: func DefaultStatsRenderer(_ context.Context, s *stats.Statistics, param string) stats.QueryStats {\\n151: \\tif param != \\\"\\\" {\\n152: \\t\\treturn stats.NewQueryStats(s)\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\treturn nil\\n155: }\\n156: \\n157: // PrometheusVersion contains build information about Prometheus.\\n158: type PrometheusVersion struct {\\n159: \\tVersion   string `json:\\\"version\\\"`\\n160: \\tRevision  string `json:\\\"revision\\\"`\\n161: \\tBranch    string `json:\\\"branch\\\"`\\n162: \\tBuildUser string `json:\\\"buildUser\\\"`\\n163: \\tBuildDate string `json:\\\"buildDate\\\"`\\n164: \\tGoVersion string `json:\\\"goVersion\\\"`\\n165: }\\n166: \\n167: // RuntimeInfo contains runtime information about Prometheus.\\n168: type RuntimeInfo struct {\\n169: \\tStartTime           time.Time `json:\\\"startTime\\\"`\\n170: \\tCWD                 string    `json:\\\"CWD\\\"`\\n171: \\tHostname            string    `json:\\\"hostname\\\"`\\n172: \\tServerTime          time.Time `json:\\\"serverTime\\\"`\\n173: \\tReloadConfigSuccess bool      `json:\\\"reloadConfigSuccess\\\"`\\n174: \\tLastConfigTime      time.Time `json:\\\"lastConfigTime\\\"`\\n175: \\tCorruptionCount     int64     `json:\\\"corruptionCount\\\"`\\n176: \\tGoroutineCount      int       `json:\\\"goroutineCount\\\"`\\n177: \\tGOMAXPROCS          int       `json:\\\"GOMAXPROCS\\\"`\\n178: \\tGOMEMLIMIT          int64     `json:\\\"GOMEMLIMIT\\\"`\\n179: \\tGOGC                string    `json:\\\"GOGC\\\"`\\n180: \\tGODEBUG             string struct {\\n186: \\tStatus    status   `json:\\\"status\\\"`\\n187: \\tData      any      `json:\\\"data,omitempty\\\"`\\n188: \\tErrorType string   `json:\\\"errorType,omitempty\\\"`\\n189: \\tError apiFuncResult struct {\\n195: \\tdata      any\\n196: \\terr       *apiError\\n197: \\twarnings  annotations.Annotations\\n198: \\tfinalizer func()\\n199: }\\n200: \\n201: type apiFunc func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult\\n202: \\n203: // TSDBAdminStats defines the tsdb interfaces used by the v1 API for admin operations as well as statistics.\\n204: type TSDBAdminStats interface {\\n205: \\tCleanTombstones() error\\n206: \\tDelete(ctx context.Context, mint, maxt int64, ms ...*labels.Matcher) error\\n207: \\tSnapshot(dir string, withHead bool) error\\n208: \\tStats(statsByLabelName string, limit int) (*tsdb.Stats, error)\\n209: \\tWALReplayStatus() (tsdb.WALReplayStatus, error)\\n210: \\tBlockMetas() ([]tsdb.BlockMeta, error)\\n211: }\\n212: \\n213: type QueryOpts interface {\\n214: \\tEnablePerStepStats() bool\\n215: \\tLookbackDelta() time.Duration\\n216: }\\n217: \\n218: // API can register a set of endpoints in a router and handle\\n219: // them using the provided storage and query engine.\\n220: type API struct {\\n221: \\tQueryable         storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable\\n222: \\tQueryEngine       promql.QueryEngine\\n223: \\tExemplarQueryable storage.ExemplarQueryable\\n224: \\n225: \\tscrapePoolsRetriever  func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever\\n226: \\ttargetRetriever       func(context.Context) TargetRetriever\\n227: \\talertmanagerRetriever func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever\\n228: \\trulesRetriever        func(context.Context) RulesRetriever\\n229: \\tnow                   func() time.Time\\n230: \\tconfig                func() config.Config\\n231: \\tflagsMap              map[string]string\\n232: \\tready                 func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc\\n233: \\tglobalURLOptions http.Handler\\n251: \\tremoteReadHandler  http.Handler\\n252: \\totlpWriteHandler   http.Handler\\n253: \\n254: \\tcodecs []Codec\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: // NewAPI returns an initialized API \\n131: ## Web UI and API\\n132: \\n133: Prometheus serves its web UI and API on port `9090` by default. The web UI is available at `/` and serves a human-usable interface for running expression queries, inspecting active alerts, or getting other insight into the status of the Prometheus server.\\n134: \\n135: The web API is served under `/api/v1` and allows programmatic [querying, metadata, and server status inspection](https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/querying/api/).\\n136: \\n137: \",\"endLine\":137,\"startLine\":130},{\"content\":\"30: \\n31: ### Reload handler\\n32: \\n33: The [configuration reload handler](https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/blob/v2.3.1/cmd/prometheus/main.go#L443-L478) is a goroutine that is implemented directly in `main()` and listens for configuration reload requests from either the web interface or ... [line truncated]\\n34: \\n35: ## Termination handler\\n36: \\n37: The [termination handler](https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/blob/v2.3.1/cmd/prometheus/main.go#L367-L392) is a goroutine that is implemented directly in `main()` and listens for termination requests from either the web interface or a [`TERM` signal] ... [line truncated]\\n38: \\n39: \",\"endLine\":39,\"startLine\":30},{\"content\":\"85: \\n86: Prometheus stores time series samples in a local time series database (TSDB) and optionally also forwards a copy of all samples to a set of configurable remote endpoints. Similarly, Prometheus reads data from the local TSDB and optionally also from remote  ... [line truncated]\\n87: \\n88: \",\"endLine\":88,\"startLine\":85}],\"file\":\"documentation/internal_architecture.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/documentation/internal_architecture.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"805: \\n806: func (h *Handler) runtimeInfo() (api_v1.RuntimeInfo, error) {\\n807: \\tstatus := api_v1.RuntimeInfo{\\n808: \\t\\tStartTime:      h.birth,\\n809: \",\"endLine\":809,\"startLine\":805},{\"content\":\"207: \\n208: // Handler serves various HTTP endpoints of the Prometheus server.\\n209: type Handler struct {\\n210: \\tlogger *slog.Logger\\n211: \",\"endLine\":211,\"startLine\":207},{\"content\":\"694: \\tav1 := route.New().\\n695: \\t\\tWithInstrumentation(h.metrics.instrumentHandlerWithPrefix(\\\"/api/v1\\\")).\\n696: \\t\\tWithInstrumentation(setPathWithPrefix(apiPath + \\\"/v1\\\"))\\n697: \\th.apiV1.Register(av1)\\n698: \\n699: \\tmux.Handle(apiPath+\\\"/v1/\\\", http.StripPrefix(apiPath+\\\"/v1\\\", av1))\\n700: \\n701: \\terrlog := slog.NewLogLogger(h.logger.Handler(), slog.LevelError)\\n702: \\n703: \",\"endLine\":703,\"startLine\":694}],\"file\":\"web/web.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/web/web.go\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"15: \\n16: # Scrape config for API servers.\\n17: #\\n18: # Kubernetes exposes API servers as endpoints to the default/kubernetes\\n19: # service so this uses `endpoints` role and uses relabelling to only keep\\n20: # the endpoints associated with the default/kubernetes service using the\\n21: # default named port `https`. This works for single API server deployments as\\n22: # well as HA API server deployments.\\n23: scrape_configs:\\n24:   - job_name: \\\"kubernetes-apiservers\\\"\\n25: \\n26: \",\"endLine\":26,\"startLine\":15},{\"content\":\"50: \\n51:     # Keep only the default/kubernetes service endpoints for the https port. This\\n52:     # will add targets for each API server which Kubernetes adds an endpoint to\\n53:     # the default/kubernetes service.\\n54: \",\"endLine\":54,\"startLine\":50},{\"content\":\"99:   # (those whose names begin with 'container_') have been removed from the\\n100:   # Kubelet metrics endpoint.
