* [CHANGE] Send target update on empty pod IP in Kubernetes SD.\\n1981: * [FEATURE] Add API endpoint for flags.\\n1982: * [FEATURE] Add API endpoint for dropped targets.\\n1983: * [FEATURE] Display annotations on alerts page.\\n1984: \",\"endLine\":1984,\"startLine\":1980},{\"content\":\"1796: * [FEATURE] Add JSON log format via the `--log.format` flag. #4876\\n1797: * [FEATURE] API: Add /api/v1/labels endpoint to get all label names. #4835\\n1798: * [FEATURE] Web: Allow setting the page's title via the `--web.ui-title` flag. #4841\\n1799: \",\"endLine\":1799,\"startLine\":1796},{\"content\":\"1559: \\n1560: * [SECURITY/BUGFIX] UI: Ensure warnings from the API are escaped. #6279\\n1561: * [FEATURE] API: `/api/v1/status/runtimeinfo` and `/api/v1/status/buildinfo` endpoints added for use by the React UI. #6243\\n1562: * [FEATURE] React UI: implement the new experimental React based UI. #5694 and many more\\n1563: \",\"endLine\":1563,\"startLine\":1559}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/CHANGELOG.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"155: a\\n350: }\\n351: \\n352: // InstallCodec adds codec to this API's available codecs.\\n353: // Codecs installed first take precedence over codecs installed later when evaluating wildcards in Accept headers.\\n354: // The first installed codec is used as a fallback when the Accept header cannot be satisfied or if there is no Accept header.\\n355: func (api *API) InstallCodec(codec Codec) {\\n356: ClearCodecs() {\\n361: \\tapi.codecs = nil\\n362: }\\n363: \\n364: func setUnavailStatusOnTSDBNotReady(r apiFuncResult) apiFuncResult {\\n365: \\tif r.err != nil && errors.Is(r.err.err, tsdb.ErrNotReady) {\\n366: \\t\\tr.err.typ = errorUnavailable\\n367: \\t}\\n368: \\treturn r\\n369: }\\n370: \\n371: // Register the API's endpoints in the given router.\\n372: func (api *API) \\tr.Put(\\\"/admin/tsdb/snapshot\\\", wrapAgent(api.snapshot))\\n459: }\\n460: \\n461: type QueryData struct {\\n462: \\tResultType parser.ValueType `json:\\\"resultType\\\"`\\n463: \\tResult     parser.Value     `json:\\\"result\\\"`\\n464: \\tStats      stats.QueryStats `json:\\\"stats,omitempty\\\"`\\n465: }\\n466: \\n467: func invalidParamError(err error, parameter string) apiFuncResult {\\n468: \\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{\\n469: \\t\\terrorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid parameter %q: %w\\\", parameter, err),\\n470: \\t}, nil, nil}\\n471: }\\n472: \\n473: func (*API) options(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\\n474: \\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\\n475: }\\n476: \\n477: func (api *API) query(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\\n478: \\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\\\"limit\\\"))\\n479: \\tif err != nil {\\n480: \\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"limit\\\")\\n481: \\t}\\n482: \\tts, err := parseTimeParam(r, \\\"time\\\", api.now())\\n483: \\tif err != nil {\\n484: \\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"time\\\")\\n485: \\t}\\n486: \\tctx := r.Context()\\n487: \\tif to := r.FormValue(\\\"timeout\\\"); to != \\\"\\\" {\\n488: \\t\\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\\n489: \\t\\ttimeout, err := parseDuration(to)\\n490: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n491: \\t\\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"timeout\\\")\\n492: \\t\\t}\\n493: \\n494: \\t\\tctx, cancel = context.WithDeadline(ctx, api.now().Add(timeout))\\n495: \\t\\tdefer cancel()\\n496: \\t}\\n497: \\n498: \\topts, err := extractQueryOpts(r)\\n499: \\tif err != nil {\\n500: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\\n501: Input
{
  "query": "repo:^github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4$ Alertmanager integration silence creation"

"{\"blocks\":[{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"116: Prometheus can be [configured](configuration.md) to automatically discover available\\n117: Alertmanager instances through its service discovery integrations.\\n118: \",\"endLine\":118,\"startLine\":116},{\"content\":\"109: they are not a fully-fledged notification solution. Another layer is needed to\\n110: add summarization, notification rate limiting, silencing and alert dependencies\\n111: on top of the simple alert definitions. In Prometheus's ecosystem, the\\n112: [Alertmanager](https://prometheus.io/docs/alerting/alertmanager/) takes on this\\n113: role. Thus, Prometheus may be configured to periodically send information about\\n114: alert states to an Alertmanager instance, which then takes care of dispatching\\n115: the right notifications.\\n116: \",\"endLine\":116,\"startLine\":109}],\"file\":\"docs/configuration/alerting_rules.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/docs/configuration/alerting_rules.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"187: \\n188: ### Existing integrations\\n189: \\n190: To learn more about existing integrations with remote storage systems, see the\\n191: [Integrations documentation](https://prometheus.io/docs/operating/integrations/#remote-endpoints-and-storage).\\n192: \\n193: \",\"endLine\":193,\"startLine\":187},{\"content\":\"146: \\n147: ## Remote storage integrations\\n148: \\n149: \",\"endLine\":149,\"startLine\":146},{\"content\":\"253: When a new recording rule is created, there is no historical data for it.\\n254: Recording rule data only exists from the creation time on.\\n255: `promtool` makes it possible to create historical recording rule data.\\n256: \",\"endLine\":256,\"startLine\":253}],\"file\":\"docs/storage.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/docs/storage.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"2480: \\n2481: * [CHANGE] Integrate with Alertmanager 0.1.0 and higher\\n2482: * [CHANGE] Degraded storage mode renamed to rushed mode\\n2483: \",\"endLine\":2483,\"startLine\":2480},{\"content\":\"2126: \\n2127: * [BUGFIX] Fix disappearing Alertmanager targets in Alertmanager discovery.\\n2128: * [BUGFIX] Fix panic with remote_write on ARMv7.\\n2129: \",\"endLine\":2129,\"startLine\":2126},{\"content\":\"1227: \\n1228: In the next minor release (2.26), Prometheus will use the Alertmanager API v2.\\n1229: It will be done by defaulting `alertmanager_config.api_version` to `v2`.\\n1230: Alertmanager API v2 was released in Alertmanager v0.16.0 (released in January\\n1231: 2019).\\n1232: \",\"endLine\":1232,\"startLine\":1227}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/CHANGELOG.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"263: - List the service discovery in both `<scrape_config>` and\\n264:   `<alertmanager_config>` in `docs/configuration/configuration.md`.\\n265: \\n266: \",\"endLine\":266,\"startLine\":263},{\"content\":\"246: It's used to tag the provided `Logger` and as the part of the YAML key for your SD\\n247: mechanism's list of configs in `scrape_config` and `alertmanager_config`\\n248: (e.g. `${NAME}_sd_configs`).\\n249: \",\"endLine\":249,\"startLine\":246},{\"content\":\"36: single generic mechanism for things with infinite variations, rather than\\n37: trying to support everything natively (see also, alertmanager webhook, remote\\n38: read, remote write, node exporter textfile collector). \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/testutil\\\"\\n60: )\\n61: \\n62: func testEngine(t *testing.T) *promql.Engine {\\n63: \\tt.Helper()\\n64: \\treturn promqltest.NewTestEngineWithOpts(t, promql.EngineOpts{\\n65: \\t\\tLogger:                   nil,\\n66: \\t\\tReg:                      nil,\\n67: \\t\\tMaxSamples:               10000,\\n68: \\t\\tTimeout:                  100 * time.Second,\\n69: \\t\\tNoStepSubqueryIntervalFn: func(int64) int64 { return 60 * 1000 },\\n70: \\t\\tEnableAtModifier:         true,\\n71: \\t\\tEnableNegativeOffset:     true,\\n72: \\t\\tEnablePerStepStats:       true,\\n73: \\t})\\n74: }\\n75: \\n76: // testMetaStore satisfies the scrape.MetricMetadataStore interface.\\n77: // It is used to inject specific metadata as part of a test case.\\n78: type testMetaStore struct {\\n79: \\tMetadata []scrape.MetricMetadata\\n80: }\\n81: \\n82: func (s *testMetaStore) ListMetadata() []scrape.MetricMetadata {\\n83: \\treturn {\\n89: \\t\\t\\treturn m, true\\n90: \\t\\t}\\n91: \\t}\\n92: \\n93: \\treturn scrape.MetricMetadata{}, false\\n94: }\\n95: \\n96: func (*testMetaStore) SizeMetadata() int   { return 0 }\\n97: func (*testMetaStore) LengthMetadata() int { return 0 }\\n98: \\n99: // testTargetRetriever represents a list of targets to scrape.\\n100: // It is used to represent targets as part of test cases.\\n101: type testTargetRetriever struct {\\n102: \\tactiveTargets  map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n103: \\tdroppedTargets map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n104: }\\n105: \\n106: type testTargetParams struct {\\n107: \\tIdentifier   string\\n108: \\tLabels       labels.Labels\\n109: \\ttargetLabels model.LabelSet\\n110: \\tParams       url.Values\\n111: \\tReports      []*testReport\\n112: \\tActive       bool\\n113: }\\n114: \\n115: type testReport struct {\\n116: \\tStart    time.Time\\n117: \\tDuration time.Duration\\n118: \\tError    error\\n119: }\\n120: \\n121: func newTestTargetRetriever(targetsInfo []*testTargetParams) *testTargetRetriever {\\n122: \\tvar activeTargets map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n123: \\tvar droppedTargets map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n124: \\tactiveTargets = make(map[string][]*scrape.Target)\\n125: \\tdroppedTargets = make(map[string][]*scrape.Target)\\n126: \\n127: \\tfor _, t := range targetsInfo {\\n128: \\t\\tnt := scrape.NewTarget(t.Labels, &config.ScrapeConfig{Params: t.Params}, t.targetLabels, nil)\\n129: \\n130: \\t\\tfor _, r := range t.Reports {\\n131: \\t\\t\\tnt.Report(r.Start, r.Duration, r.Error)\\n132: \\t\\t}\\n133: \\n134: \\t\\tif t.Active {\\n135: \\t\\t\\tactiveTargets[t.Identifier] = []*scrape.Target{nt}\\n136: \\t\\t} else {\\n137: \\t\\t\\tdroppedTargets[t.Identifier] = []*scrape.Target{nt}\\n138: \\t\\t}\\n139: \\t}\\n140: \\n141: \\treturn &testTargetRetriever{\\n142: \\t\\tactiveTargets:  activeTargets,\\n143: \\t\\tdroppedTargets: droppedTargets,\\n144: \\t}\\n145: }\\n146: \\n147: var scrapeStart = time.Now().Add(-11 * time.Second)\\n148: \\n149: func (t testTargetRetriever) TargetsActive() map[string][]*scrape.Target {\\n150: \\treturn t.activeTargets\\n151: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/web/api/v1/api_test.go%3FL1-150\"}"

nonzero, check only the length; `response` is ignored.\\n1113: \\t\\tresponseMetadataTotal int\\n1114: \\t\\tresponseAsJSON        string\\n1115: \\t\\twarningsCount         int\\n1116: \\t\\terrType \\t\\tzeroFunc              func(any)\\n1121: \\t}\\n1122: \\n1123: \\trulesZeroFunc := func(i any) {\\n1124: \\t\\tif i != nil {\\n1125: \\t\\t\\tv := i.(*RuleDiscovery)\\n1126: \\t\\t\\tfor _, \\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Connection\\\", \\\"keep-alive\\\")\\n1859: \\n1860: \\t// Subscribe to notifications.\\n1861: \\tnotifications, unsubscribe, ok := api.notificationsSub()\\n1862: \\tif !ok \\tflusher, ok := w.(http.Flusher)\\n1870: \\tif !ok {\\n1871: \\t\\thttp.Error(w, \\\"Streaming unsupported\\\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\\n1872: \\t\\treturn\\n1873: \\t}\\n1874: \\n1875: "{\"content\":\"2050: \\treturn defaultCodec, nil\\n2051: }\\n2052: \\n2053: func (api *API) respondError(w http.ResponseWriter, apiErr *apiError, data any) {\\n2054: \\tjson := jsoniter.ConfigCompatibleWithStandardLibrary\\n2055: \\tb, err := json.Marshal(&Response{\\n2056: \\t\\tStatus:    statusError,\\n2057: \\t\\tErrorType: apiErr.typ.str,\\n2058: \\t\\tError:     apiErr.err.Error(),\\n2059: \\t\\tData:      data,\\n2060: \\t})\\n2061: \\tif err != nil {\\n2062: \\t\\tapi.logger.Error(\\\"error marshaling json response\\\", \\\"err\\\", err)\\n2063: \\t\\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\\n2064: \\t\\treturn\\n2065: \\t}\\n2066: \\n2067: \\tvar code int\\n2068: \\tif api.overrideErrorCode != nil {\\n2069: \\t\\tif newCode, override := api.overrideErrorCode(apiErr.typ.num, apiErr.err); override {\\n2070: \\t\\t\\tcode = newCode\\n2071: \\t\\t} else {\\n2072: \\t\\t\\tcode = getDefaultErrorCode(apiErr.typ)\\n2073: \\t\\t}\\n2074: \\t} else {\\n2075: \\t\\tcode = getDefaultErrorCode(apiErr.typ)\\n2076: \\t}\\n2077: \\n2078: \\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/json\\\")\\n2079: \\tw.WriteHeader(code)\\n2080: \\tif n, err := w.Write(b); err != nil {\\n2081: \\t\\tapi.logger.Error(\\\"error writing response\\\", \\\"bytesWritten\\\", n, \\\"err\\\", err)\\n2082: \\t}\\n2083: }\\n2084: \\n2085: func getDefaultErrorCode(errType errorType) int {\\n2086: \\tswitch errType {\\n2087: \\tcase errorBadData:\\n2088: \\t\\treturn http.StatusBadRequest\\n2089: \\tcase errorExec:\\n2090: \\t\\treturn http.StatusUnprocessableEntity\\n2091: \\tcase errorCanceled:\\n2092: \\t\\treturn statusClientClosedConnection\\n2093: \\tcase errorTimeout:\\n2094: \\t\\treturn http.StatusServiceUnavailable\\n2095: \\tcase errorInternal:\\n2096: \\t\\treturn http.StatusInternalServerError\\n2097: \\tcase errorNotFound:\\n2098: \\t\\treturn http.StatusNotFound\\n2099: \\tcase errorNotAcceptable:\\n2100: \\t\\treturn http.StatusNotAcceptable\\n2101: \\tdefault:\\n2102: \\t\\treturn http.StatusInternalServerError\\n2103: \\t}\\n2104: }\\n2105: \\n2106: func parseTimeParam(r *http.Request, paramName string, defaultValue time.Time) (time.Time, error) {\\n2107: \\tval := r.FormValue(paramName)\\n2108: \\tif val == \\\"\\\" {\\n2109: \\t\\treturn defaultValue, nil\\n2110: \\t}\\n2111: \\tresult, err := parseTime(val)\\n2112: \\tif err != nil {\\n2113: \\t\\treturn time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid time value for '%s': %w\\\", paramName, err)\\n2114: \\t}\\n2115: \\treturn result, nil\\n2116: }\\n2117: \\n2118: func parseTime(s string) (time.Time, error) {\\n2119: \\tif t, err := strconv.ParseFloat(s, 64); err == nil {\\n2120: \\t\\ts, ns := math.Modf(t)\\n2121: \\t\\tns = math.Round(ns*1000) / 1000\\n2122: \\t\\treturn time.Unix(int64(s), int64(ns*float64(time.Second))).UTC(), nil\\n2123: \\t}\\n2124: \\tif t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, s); err == nil {\\n2125: \\t\\treturn t, nil\\n2126: \\t}\\n2127: \\n2128: \\t// Stdlib's time parser can only handle 4 digit years. As a workaround until\\n2129: \\t// that is fixed we want to at least support our own boundary times.\\n2130: \\t// Context: https://github.com/prometheus/client_golang/issues/614\\n2131: \\t// Upstream issue: https://github.com/golang/go/issues/20555\\n2132: \\tswitch s {\\n2133: \\tcase minTimeFormatted:\\n2134: \\t\\treturn MinTime, nil\\n2135: \\tcase maxTimeFormatted:\\n2136: \\t\\treturn MaxTime, nil\\n2137: \\t}\\n2138: \\treturn time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf(\\\"cannot parse %q to a valid timestamp\\\", s)\\n2139: }\\n2140: \\n2141: func parseDuration(s string) (time.Duration, error) {\\n2142: \\tif d, err := strconv.ParseFloat(s, 64); err == nil {\\n2143: \\t\\tts := d * float64(time.Second)\\n2144: \\t\\tif ts > float64(math.MaxInt64) || ts < float64(math.MinInt64) {\\n2145: \\t\\t\\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\\\"cannot parse %q to a valid duration. It overflows int64\\\", s)\\n2146: \\t\\t}\\n2147: \\t\\treturn time.Duration(ts), nil\\n2148: \\t}\\n2149: \\tif d, err := model.ParseDuration(s); err == nil {\\n2150: \\t\\treturn time.Duration(d), nil\\n2151: \\t}\\n2152: \\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\\\"cannot parse %q to a valid duration\\\", s)\\n2153: }\\n2154: \\n2155: func parseMatchersParam(matchers []string) ([][]*labels.Matcher, error) {\\n2156: \\tmatcherSets, err := parser.ParseMetricSelectors(matchers)\\n2157: \\tif err != nil {\\n2158: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n2159: \\t}\\n2160: \\n2161: OUTER:\\n2162: \\tfor _, ms := range matcherSets {\\n2163: \\t\\tfor _, lm := range ms {\\n2164: \\t\\t\\tif lm != nil && !lm.Matches(\\\"\\\") {\\n2165: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue OUTER\\n2166: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2167: \\t\\t}\\n2168: \\t\\treturn nil, errors.New(\\\"match[] must contain at least one non-empty matcher\\\")\\n2169: \\t}\\n2170: \\treturn matcherSets, nil\\n2171: }\\n2172: \\n2173: // parseLimitParam returning 0 means no limit is to be applied.\\n2174: func parseLimitParam(limitStr string) (limit int, err error) {\\n2175: \\tif limitStr == \\\"\\\" {\\n2176: \\t\\treturn limit, nil\\n2177: \\t}\\n2178: \\n2179: \\tlimit, err = strconv.Atoi(limitStr)\\n2180: \\tif err != nil {\\n2181: \\t\\treturn limit, err\\n2182: \\t}\\n2183: \\tif limit < 0 {\\n2184: \\t\\treturn limit, errors.New(\\\"limit must be non-negative\\\")\\n2185: \\t}\\n2186: \\n2187: \\treturn limit, nil\\n2188: }\\n2189: \\n2190: // toHintLimit increases the API limit, as returned by parseLimitParam, by 1.\\n2191: // This allows for emitting warnings when the results are truncated.\\n2192: func toHintLimit(limit int) int {\\n2193: \\t// 0 means no limit and avoid int overflow\\n2194: \\tif limit > 0 && limit < math.MaxInt {\\n2195: \\t\\treturn limit + 1\\n2196: \\t}\\n2197: \\treturn limit\\n2198: }\\n2199: \\n2200: // truncateResults truncates result for queryRange() and query().\\n2201: err != nil {\\n2032: \\t\\tapi.logger.Error(\\\"error writing response\\\", \\\"url\\\", req.URL, \\\"bytesWritten\\\", n, \\\"err\\\", err)\\n2033: \\t}\\n2034: }\\n2035: \\n2036: func (api *API) negotiateCodec(req *http.Request, resp *Response) (Codec, error) {\\n2037: \\tfor _, clause := range goautoneg.ParseAccept(req.Header.Get(\\\"Accept\\\")) {\\n2038: \\t\\tfor _, codec := range api.codecs {\\n2039: \\t\\t\\tif codec.ContentType().Satisfies(clause) && codec.CanEncode(resp) {\\n2040: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn codec, nil\\n2041: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2042: \\t\\t}\\n2043: \\t}\\n2044: \\n2045: \\tdefaultCodec := api.codecs[0]\\n2046: \\tif !defaultCodec.CanEncode(resp) {\\n2047: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"cannot encode response as %s\\\", defaultCodec.ContentType())\\n2048: \\t}\\n2049: \\n2050: \\treturn defaultCodec, nil\\n2051: }\\n2052: \\n2053: func (api *API) respondError(w http.ResponseWriter, apiErr *apiError, data any) {\\n2054: \\tjson := jsoniter.ConfigCompatibleWithStandardLibrary\\n2055: \\tb, err := json.Marshal(&Response{\\n2056: \\t\\tStatus:    statusError,\\n2057: \\t\\tErrorType: apiErr.typ.str,\\n2058: \\t\\tError:     apiErr.err.Error(),\\n2059: \\t\\tData:      data,\\n2060: \\t})\\n2061: \\tif err != nil {\\n2062: \\t\\tapi.logger.Error(\\\"error marshaling json response\\\", \\\"err\\\", err)\\n2063: \\t\\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\\n2064: \\t\\treturn\\n2065: \\t}\\n2066: \\n2067: \\tvar code int\\n2068: \\tif api.overrideErrorCode != nil {\\n2069: \\t\\tif newCode, override := api.overrideErrorCode(apiErr.typ.num, apiErr.err); override {\\n2070: \\t\\t\\tcode = newCode\\n2071: \\t\\t} else {\\n2072: \\t\\t\\tcode = getDefaultErrorCode(apiErr.typ)\\n2073: \\t\\t}\\n2074: \\t} else {\\n2075: \\t\\tcode = getDefaultErrorCode(apiErr.typ)\\n2076: \\t}\\n2077: \\n2078: \\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/json\\\")\\n2079: \\tw.WriteHeader(code)\\n2080: \\tif n, err := w.Write(b); err != nil {\\n2081: \\t\\tapi.logger.Error(\\\"error writing response\\\", \\\"bytesWritten\\\", n, \\\"err\\\", err)\\n2082: \\t}\\n2083: }\\n2084: \\n2085: func getDefaultErrorCode(errType errorType) int {\\n2086: \\tswitch errType {\\n2087: \\tcase errorBadData:\\n2088: \\t\\treturn http.StatusBadRequest\\n2089: \\tcase errorExec:\\n2090: \\t\\treturn http.StatusUnprocessableEntity\\n2091: \\tcase errorCanceled:\\n2092: \\t\\treturn statusClientClosedConnection\\n2093: \\tcase errorTimeout:\\n2094: \\t\\treturn http.StatusServiceUnavailable\\n2095: \\tcase errorInternal:\\n2096: \\t\\treturn http.StatusInternalServerError\\n2097: \\tcase errorNotFound:\\n2098: \\t\\treturn http.StatusNotFound\\n2099: \\tcase errorNotAcceptable:\\n2100: \\t\\treturn http.StatusNotAcceptable\\n2101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/web/api/v1/api.go%3FL2000-2100\"}"

\tMetadata []scrape.MetricMetadata\n80: }\n81: \n82: func (s *testMetaStore) ListMetadata() []scrape.MetricMetadata {\n83: \treturn s.Metadata\n84: }\n85: \n86: func (s *testMetaStore) Error handling tests (~288 lines)\n6. **web/api/v1/json_codec.go** - JSON codec implementation (~265 lines)\n7. **web/api/v1/translate_ast.go** - AST translation utilities (~158 lines)\n8. **go.mod** - Visit the repository directly at: https://sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4\n   - Navigate to each file and download via the UI\n\n3. **Use Claude with File Access**:\n   - The sg_read_file tool successfully reads from Sourcegraph and can be piped to local writes for smaller files\n   - performs encoding of API responses.\\n19: type Codec interface {\\n20: \\t// ContentType returns the MIME time that this Codec emits.\\n21: \\tContentType() MIMEType\\n22: \\n23: \\t// CanEncode determines if this Codec can encode resp.\\n24: \\tCanEncode(resp *Response) bool\\n25: \\n26: \\t// Encode encodes resp, ready for transmission to an API consumer.\\n27: \\tEncode(resp *Response) ([]byte, error)\\n28: }\\n29: \\n30: type MIMEType struct {\\n31: \\tType    string\\n32: \\tSubType string\\n33: }\\n34: \\n35: func (m MIMEType) String() string {\\n36: \\treturn m.Type + \\\"/\\\" + m.SubType\\n37: }\\n38: \\n39: func (m MIMEType) Satisfies(accept goautoneg.Accept) bool {\\n40: \\tif accept.Type == \\\"*\\\" && accept.SubType == \\\"*\\\" {\\n41: \\t\\treturn true\\n42: \\t}\\n43: \\n44: \\tif accept.Type == m.Type && accept.SubType == \\\"*\\\" {\\n45: \\t\\treturn true\\n46: \\t}\\n47: \\n48: \\tif accept.Type == m.Type && accept.SubType == m.SubType {\\n49: \\t\\treturn true\\n50: \\t}\\n51: \\n52: \\treturn false\\n53: }\\n54: \\t\\\"github.com/munnerz/goautoneg\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\\\"\\n21: )\\n22: \\n23: func TestMIMEType_String(t *testing.T) {\\n24: \\tm := MIMEType{Type: goautoneg.Accept{Type: \\\"json\\\", SubType: \\\"application\\\"},\\n50: \\t\\t\\texpected: false,\\n51: \\t\\t},\\n52: \\t\\t\\\"inverted sub-type wildcard\\\": {\\n53: \\t\\t\\taccept:   goautoneg.Accept{Type: \\\"json\\\", SubType: \\\"*\\\"},\\n54: \\t\\t\\texpected: false,\\n55: \\t\\t},\\n56: \\t\\t\\\"complete mismatch\\\": {\\n57: \\t\\t\\taccept:   goautoneg.Accept{Type: \\\"text\\\", SubType: \\\"plain\\\"},\\n58: \\t\\t\\texpected: false,\\n59: \\t\\t},\\n60: \\t}\\n61: \\n62: \\tfor name, scenario := range scenarios {\\n63: \\t\\tt.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {\\n64: \\t\\t\\tactual := m.Satisfies(scenario.accept)\\n65: \\t\\t\\trequire.Equal(t, scenario.expected, actual)\\n66: \\t\\t})\\n67: \\t}\\n68: }\\n69: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/web/api/v1/codec_test.go\"}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright 2022 The Prometheus Authors\\n2: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n3: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n4: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n5: //\\n6: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n7: //\\n8: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n9: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n10: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n11: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n12: // limitations under the License.\\n13: \\n14: package v1\\n15: \\n16: import (\\n17: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n18: \\t\\\"errors\\\"\\n19: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"net/http/httptest\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"net/url\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"testing\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n26: \\n27: \\t\\\"github.com/grafana/regexp\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/common/promslog\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/common/route\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\\\"\\n32: \\n33: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/config\\\"\\n34: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql\\\"\\n36: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/promqltest\\\"\\n37: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/rules\\\"\\n38: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/scrape\\\"\\n39: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage\\\"\\n40: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/annotations\\\"\\n41: )\\n42: \\n43: func TestApiStatusCodes(t *testing.T) {\\n44: \\tfor name, tc := range map[string]struct {\\n45: \\t\\terr               error\\n46: \\t\\texpectedString    string\\n47: \\t\\texpectedCode      int\\n48: \\t\\toverrideErrorCode OverrideErrorCode\\n49: \\t}{\\n50: \\t\\t\\\"random error\\\": {\\n51: \\t\\t\\terr:            errors.New(\\\"some random error\\\"),\\n52: \\t\\t\\texpectedString: \\\"some random error\\\",\\n53: \\t\\t\\texpectedCode:   http.StatusUnprocessableEntity,\\n54: \\t\\t},\\n55: \\n56: \\t\\t\\\"promql.ErrTooManySamples\\\": {\\n57: \\t\\t\\terr:            promql.ErrTooManySamples(\\\"some error\\\"),\\n58: \\t\\t\\texpectedString: \\\"too many samples\\\",\\n59: \\t\\t\\texpectedCode:   http.StatusUnprocessableEntity,\\n60: \\t\\t},\\n61: \\n62: \\t\\t\\\"overridden error code for engine error\\\": {\\n63: \\t\\t\\terr:            promql.ErrTooManySamples(\\\"some error\\\"),\\n64: \\t\\t\\texpectedString: \\\"too many samples\\\",\\n65: \\t\\t\\toverrideErrorCode: func(errNum errorNum, err error) (code int, override bool) {\\n66: \\t\\t\\t\\tif errNum == ErrorExec {\\n67: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif strings.Contains(err.Error(), \\\"some error\\\") {\\n68: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn 999, true\\n69: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n70: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn 998, true\\n71: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n72: \\n73: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, false\\n74: \\t\\t\\t},\\n75: \\t\\t\\texpectedCode: 999,\\n76: \\t\\t},\\n77: \\n78: \\t\\t\\\"promql.ErrQueryCanceled\\\": {\\n79: \\t\\t\\terr:            promql.ErrQueryCanceled(\\\"some error\\\"),\\n80: \\t\\t\\texpectedString: \\\"query was canceled\\\",\\n81: \\t\\t\\texpectedCode:   statusClientClosedConnection,\\n82: \\t\\t},\\n83: \\n84: \\t\\t\\\"promql.ErrQueryTimeout\\\": {\\n85: \\t\\t\\terr:            promql.ErrQueryTimeout(\\\"some error\\\"),\\n86: \\t\\t\\texpectedString: \\\"query timed out\\\",\\n87: \\t\\t\\texpectedCode:   http.StatusServiceUnavailable,\\n88: \\t\\t},\\n89: \\n90: \\t\\t\\\"context.DeadlineExceeded\\\": {\\n91: \\t\\t\\terr:            context.DeadlineExceeded,\\n92: \\t\\t\\texpectedString: \\\"context deadline exceeded\\\",\\n93: \\t\\t\\texpectedCode:   http.StatusUnprocessableEntity,\\n94: \\t\\t},\\n95: \\n96: \\t\\t\\\"context.Canceled\\\": {\\n97: \\t\\t\\terr:            context.Canceled,\\n98: \\t\\t\\texpectedString: \\\"context canceled\\\",\\n99: \\t\\t\\texpectedCode:   statusClientClosedConnection,\\n100: \\t\\t},\\n101: \\t} {\\n102: \\t\\tfor k, q := range map[string]storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable{\\n103: \\t\\t\\t\\\"error from queryable\\\": errorTestQueryable{err: tc.err},\\n104: \\t\\t\\t\\\"error from querier\\\":   errorTestQueryable{q: errorTestQuerier{err: tc.err}},\\n105: \\t\\t\\t\\\"error from seriesset\\\": errorTestQueryable{q: errorTestQuerier{s: errorTestSeriesSet{err: tc.err}}},\\n106: \\t\\t} {\\n107: \\t\\t\\tt.Run(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s/%s\\\", name, k), func(t *testing.T) {\\n108: \\t\\t\\t\\tr := createPrometheusAPI(t, q, tc.overrideErrorCode)\\n109: \\t\\t\\t\\trec := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n110: \\n111: \\t\\t\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/api/v1/query?query=up\\\", nil)\\n112: \\n113: \\t\\t\\t\\tr.ServeHTTP(rec, req)\\n114: \\n115: \\t\\t\\t\\trequire.Equal(t, tc.expectedCode, rec.Code)\\n116: \\t\\t\\t\\trequire.Contains(t, rec.Body.String(), tc.expectedString)\\n117: \\t\\t\\t})\\n118: \\t\\t}\\n119: \\t}\\n120: }\\n121: \\n122: func createPrometheusAPI(t *testing.T, q storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable, overrideErrorCode OverrideErrorCode) *route.Router {\\n123: \\tt.Helper()\\n124: \\n125: \\tengine := promqltest.NewTestEngineWithOpts(t, promql.EngineOpts{\\n126: \\t\\tLogger:             promslog.NewNopLogger(),\\n127: \\t\\tReg:                nil,\\n128: \\t\\tActiveQueryTracker: nil,\\n129: \\t\\tMaxSamples:         100,\\n130: \\t\\tTimeout:            5 * time.Second,\\n131: \\t})\\n132: \\n133: \\tapi := NewAPI(\\n134: \\t\\tengine,\\n135: \\t\\tq,\\n136: \\t\\tnil,\\n137: \\t\\tnil,\\n138: \\t\\tfunc(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever { return &DummyScrapePoolsRetriever{} },\\n139: \\t\\tfunc(context.Context) TargetRetriever { return &DummyTargetRetriever{} },\\n140: \\t\\tfunc(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever { return &DummyAlertmanagerRetriever{} },\\n141: \\t\\tfunc() config.Config { return config.Config{} },\\n142: \\t\\tmap[string]string{}, // TODO: include configuration flags\\n143: \\t\\tGlobalURLOptions{},\\n144: \\t\\tfunc(f http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc { return f },\\n145: \\t\\tnil,   // Only needed for admin APIs.\\n146: \\t\\t\\\"\\\",    // This is for snapshots, which is disabled when admin APIs are disabled. Hence empty.\\n147: \\t\\tfalse, // Disable admin APIs.\\n148: \\t\\tpromslog.NewNopLogger(),\\n149: \\t\\tfunc(context.Context) RulesRetriever { return &DummyRulesRetriever{} },\\n150: \\t\\t0, 0, 0, // Remote read samples and concurrency limit.\\n151: \\t\\tfalse, // Not an agent.\\n152: \\t\\tregexp.MustCompile(\\\".*\\\"),\\n153: \\t\\tfunc() (RuntimeInfo, error) { return RuntimeInfo{}, errors.New(\\\"not implemented\\\") },\\n154: \\t\\t&PrometheusVersion{},\\n155: \\t\\tnil,\\n156: \\t\\tnil,\\n157: \\t\\tprometheus.DefaultGatherer,\\n158: \\t\\tnil,\\n159: \\t\\tnil,\\n160: \\t\\tfalse,\\n161: \\t\\tconfig.RemoteWriteProtoMsgs{config.RemoteWriteProtoMsgV1, config.RemoteWriteProtoMsgV2},\\n162: \\t\\tfalse,\\n163: \\t\\tfalse,\\n164: \\t\\tfalse,\\n165: \\t\\tfalse,\\n166: \\t\\t5*time.Minute,\\n167: \\t\\tfalse,\\n168: \\t\\toverrideErrorCode,\\n169: \\t)\\n170: \\n171: \\tpromRouter := route.New().WithPrefix(\\\"/api/v1\\\")\\n172: \\tapi.Register(promRouter)\\n173: \\n174: \\treturn promRouter\\n175: }\\n176: \\n177: type errorTestQueryable struct {\\n178: \\tstorage.ExemplarQueryable\\n179: \\tq   storage.Querier\\n180: \\terr error\\n181: }\\n182: \\n183: func (t errorTestQueryable) ExemplarQuerier(context.Context) (storage.ExemplarQuerier, error) {\\n184: \\treturn nil, t.err\\n185: }\\n186: \\n187: func (t errorTestQueryable) ChunkQuerier(_, _ int64) (storage.ChunkQuerier, error) {\\n188: \\treturn nil, t.err\\n189: }\\n190: \\n191: func (t errorTestQueryable) Querier(_, _ int64) (storage.Querier, error) {\\n192: \\tif t.q != nil {\\n193: \\t\\treturn t.q, nil\\n194: \\t}\\n195: \\treturn nil, t.err\\n196: }\\n197: \\n198: type errorTestQuerier struct {\\n199: \\ts   storage.SeriesSet\\n200: \\terr error\\n201: }\\n202: \\n203: func (t errorTestQuerier) LabelValues(context.Context, string, *storage.LabelHints, ...*labels.Matcher) ([]string, annotations.Annotations, error) {\\n204: \\treturn nil, nil, t.err\\n205: }\\n206: \\n207: func (t errorTestQuerier) LabelNames(context.Context, *storage.LabelHints, ...*labels.Matcher) ([]string, annotations.Annotations, error) {\\n208: \\treturn nil, nil, t.err\\n209: }\\n210: \\n211: func (errorTestQuerier) Close() error {\\n212: \\treturn nil\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: func (t errorTestQuerier) Select(context.Context, bool, *storage.SelectHints, ...*labels.Matcher) storage.SeriesSet {\\n216: \\tif t.s != nil {\\n217: \\t\\treturn t.s\\n218: \\t}\\n219: \\treturn storage.ErrSeriesSet(t.err)\\n220: }\\n221: \\n222: type errorTestSeriesSet struct {\\n223: \\terr error\\n224: }\\n225: \\n226: func (errorTestSeriesSet) Next() bool {\\n227: \\treturn false\\n228: }\\n229: \\n230: func (errorTestSeriesSet) At() storage.Series {\\n231: \\treturn nil\\n232: }\\n233: \\n234: func (t errorTestSeriesSet) Err() error {\\n235: \\treturn t.err\\n236: }\\n237: \\n238: func (errorTestSeriesSet) Warnings() annotations.Annotations {\\n239: \\treturn nil\\n240: }\\n241: \\n242: // DummyScrapePoolsRetriever implements github.com/prometheus/prometheus/web/api/v1.ScrapePoolsRetriever.\\n243: type DummyScrapePoolsRetriever struct{}\\n244: \\n245: func (DummyScrapePoolsRetriever) ScrapePools() []string {\\n246: \\treturn []string{}\\n247: }\\n248: \\n249: // DummyTargetRetriever implements github.com/prometheus/prometheus/web/api/v1.targetRetriever.\\n250: type DummyTargetRetriever struct{}\\n251: \\n252: // TargetsActive implements targetRetriever.\\n253: func (DummyTargetRetriever) TargetsActive() map[string][]*scrape.Target {\\n254: \\treturn map[string][]*scrape.Target{}\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: // TargetsDropped implements targetRetriever.\\n258: func (DummyTargetRetriever) TargetsDropped() map[string][]*scrape.Target {\\n259: \\treturn map[string][]*scrape.Target{}\\n260: }\\n261: \\n262: // TargetsDroppedCounts implements targetRetriever.\\n263: func (DummyTargetRetriever) TargetsDroppedCounts() map[string]int {\\n264: \\treturn nil\\n265: }\\n266: \\n267: // DummyAlertmanagerRetriever implements AlertmanagerRetriever.\\n268: type DummyAlertmanagerRetriever struct{}\\n269: \\n270: // Alertmanagers implements AlertmanagerRetriever.\\n271: func (DummyAlertmanagerRetriever) Alertmanagers() []*url.URL { return nil }\\n272: \\n273: // DroppedAlertmanagers implements AlertmanagerRetriever.\\n274: func (DummyAlertmanagerRetriever) DroppedAlertmanagers() []*url.URL { return nil }\\n275: \\n276: // DummyRulesRetriever implements RulesRetriever.\\n277: type DummyRulesRetriever struct{}\\n278: \\n279: // RuleGroups implements RulesRetriever.\\n280: func (DummyRulesRetriever) RuleGroups() []*rules.Group {\\n281: \\treturn nil\\n282: }\\n283: \\n284: // AlertingRules implements RulesRetriever.\\n285: func (DummyRulesRetriever) AlertingRules() []*rules.AlertingRule {\\n286: \\treturn nil\\n287: }\\n288: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/web/api/v1/errors_test.go\"}"
76. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "web/api/v1/json_codec.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright 2016 The Prometheus Authors\\n2: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n3: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n4: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n5: //\\n6: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n7: //\\n8: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n9: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n10: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n11: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n12: // limitations under the License.\\n13: \\n14: package v1\\n15: \\n16: import (\\n17: \\t\\\"unsafe\\\"\\n18: \\n19: \\tjsoniter \\\"github.com/json-iterator/go\\\"\\n20: \\n21: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/exemplar\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/jsonutil\\\"\\n25: )\\n26: \\n27: func init() {\\n28: \\tjsoniter.RegisterTypeEncoderFunc(\\\"promql.Vector\\\", unsafeMarshalVectorJSON, neverEmpty)\\n29: \\tjsoniter.RegisterTypeEncoderFunc(\\\"promql.Matrix\\\", unsafeMarshalMatrixJSON, neverEmpty)\\n30: \\tjsoniter.RegisterTypeEncoderFunc(\\\"promql.Series\\\", unsafeMarshalSeriesJSON, neverEmpty)\\n31: \\tjsoniter.RegisterTypeEncoderFunc(\\\"promql.Sample\\\", unsafeMarshalSampleJSON, neverEmpty)\\n32: \\tjsoniter.RegisterTypeEncoderFunc(\\\"promql.FPoint\\\", unsafeMarshalFPointJSON, neverEmpty)\\n33: \\tjsoniter.RegisterTypeEncoderFunc(\\\"promql.HPoint\\\", unsafeMarshalHPointJSON, neverEmpty)\\n34: \\tjsoniter.RegisterTypeEncoderFunc(\\\"exemplar.Exemplar\\\", marshalExemplarJSON, neverEmpty)\\n35: \\tjsoniter.RegisterTypeEncoderFunc(\\\"labels.Labels\\\", unsafeMarshalLabelsJSON, labelsIsEmpty)\\n36: }\\n37: \\n38: // JSONCodec is a Codec that encodes API responses as JSON.\\n39: type JSONCodec struct{}\\n40: \\n41: func (JSONCodec) ContentType() MIMEType {\\n42: \\treturn MIMEType{Type: \\\"application\\\", SubType: \\\"json\\\"}\\n43: }\\n44: \\n45: func (JSONCodec) CanEncode(*Response) bool {\\n46: \\treturn true\\n47: }\\n48: \\n49: func (JSONCodec) Encode(resp *Response) ([]byte, error) {\\n50: \\tjson := jsoniter.ConfigCompatibleWithStandardLibrary\\n51: \\treturn json.Marshal(resp)\\n52: }\\n53: \\n54: // marshalSeriesJSON writes something like the following:\\n55: //\\n56: //\\t{\\n57: //\\t   \\\"metric\\\" : {\\n58: //\\t      \\\"__name__\\\" : \\\"up\\\",\\n59: //\\t      \\\"job\\\" : \\\"prometheus\\\",\\n60: //\\t      \\\"instance\\\" : \\\"localhost:9090\\\"\\n61: //\\t   },\\n62: //\\t   \\\"values\\\": [\\n63: //\\t      [ 1435781451.781, \\\"1\\\" ],\\n64: //\\t      < more values>\\n65: //\\t   ],\\n66: //\\t   \\\"histograms\\\": [\\n67: //\\t      [ 1435781451.781, { < histogram, see jsonutil.MarshalHistogram > } ],\\n68: //\\t      < more histograms >\\n69: //\\t   ],\\n70: //\\t},\\n71: func unsafeMarshalSeriesJSON(ptr unsafe.Pointer, stream *jsoniter.Stream) {\\n72: \\ts := *((*promql.Series)(ptr))\\n73: \\tmarshalSeriesJSON(s, stream)\\n74: }\\n75: \\n76: func marshalSeriesJSON(s promql.Series, stream *jsoniter.Stream) {\\n77: \\tstream.WriteObjectStart()\\n78: \\tstream.WriteObjectField(`metric`)\\n79: \\tmarshalLabelsJSON(s.Metric, stream)\\n80: \\n81: \\tfor i, p := range s.Floats {\\n82: \\t\\tstream.WriteMore()\\n83: \\t\\tif i == 0 {\\n84: \\t\\t\\tstream.WriteObjectField(`values`)\\n85: \\t\\t\\tstream.WriteArrayStart()\\n86: \\t\\t}\\n87: \\t\\tmarshalFPointJSON(p, stream)\\n88: \\t}\\n89: \\tif len(s.Floats) > 0 {\\n90: \\t\\tstream.WriteArrayEnd()\\n91: \\t}\\n92: \\tfor i, p := range s.Histograms {\\n93: \\t\\tstream.WriteMore()\\n94: \\t\\tif i == 0 {\\n95: \\t\\t\\tstream.WriteObjectField(`histograms`)\\n96: \\t\\t\\tstream.WriteArrayStart()\\n97: \\t\\t}\\n98: \\t\\tmarshalHPointJSON(p, stream)\\n99: \\t}\\n100: \\tif len(s.Histograms) > 0 {\\n101: \\t\\tstream.WriteArrayEnd()\\n102: \\t}\\n103: \\tstream.WriteObjectEnd()\\n104: }\\n105: \\n106: // In the Prometheus API we render an empty object as `[]` or similar.\\n107: func neverEmpty(unsafe.Pointer) bool {\\n108: \\treturn false\\n109: }\\n110: \\n111: // marshalSampleJSON writes something like the following for normal value samples:\\n112: //\\n113: //\\t{\\n114: //\\t   \\\"metric\\\" : {\\n115: //\\t      \\\"__name__\\\" : \\\"up\\\",\\n116: //\\t      \\\"job\\\" : \\\"prometheus\\\",\\n117: //\\t      \\\"instance\\\" : \\\"localhost:9090\\\"\\n118: //\\t   },\\n119: //\\t   \\\"value\\\": [ 1435781451.781, \\\"1.234\\\" ]\\n120: //\\t},\\n121: //\\n122: // For histogram samples, it writes something like this:\\n123: //\\n124: //\\t{\\n125: //\\t   \\\"metric\\\" : {\\n126: //\\t      \\\"__name__\\\" : \\\"up\\\",\\n127: //\\t      \\\"job\\\" : \\\"prometheus\\\",\\n128: //\\t      \\\"instance\\\" : \\\"localhost:9090\\\"\\n129: //\\t   },\\n130: //\\t   \\\"histogram\\\": [ 1435781451.781, { < histogram, see jsonutil.MarshalHistogram > } ]\\n131: //\\t},\\n132: func unsafeMarshalSampleJSON(ptr unsafe.Pointer, stream *jsoniter.Stream) {\\n133: \\ts := *((*promql.Sample)(ptr))\\n134: \\tmarshalSampleJSON(s, stream)\\n135: }\\n136: \\n137: func marshalSampleJSON(s promql.Sample, stream *jsoniter.Stream) {\\n138: \\tstream.WriteObjectStart()\\n139: \\tstream.WriteObjectField(`metric`)\\n140: \\tmarshalLabelsJSON(s.Metric, stream)\\n141: \\tstream.WriteMore()\\n142: \\tif s.H == nil {\\n143: \\t\\tstream.WriteObjectField(`value`)\\n144: \\t} else {\\n145: \\t\\tstream.WriteObjectField(`histogram`)\\n146: \\t}\\n147: \\tstream.WriteArrayStart()\\n148: \\tjsonutil.MarshalTimestamp(s.T, stream)\\n149: \\tstream.WriteMore()\\n150: \\tif s.H == nil {\\n151: \\t\\tjsonutil.MarshalFloat(s.F, stream)\\n152: \\t} else {\\n153: \\t\\tjsonutil.MarshalHistogram(s.H, stream)\\n154: \\t}\\n155: \\tstream.WriteArrayEnd()\\n156: \\tstream.WriteObjectEnd()\\n157: }\\n158: \\n159: // unsafeMarshalFPointJSON writes `[ts, \\\"1.234\\\"]`.\\n160: func unsafeMarshalFPointJSON(ptr unsafe.Pointer, stream *jsoniter.Stream) {\\n161: \\tp := *((*promql.FPoint)(ptr))\\n162: \\tmarshalFPointJSON(p, stream)\\n163: }\\n164: \\n165: func marshalFPointJSON(p promql.FPoint, stream *jsoniter.Stream) {\\n166: \\tstream.WriteArrayStart()\\n167: \\tjsonutil.MarshalTimestamp(p.T, stream)\\n168: \\tstream.WriteMore()\\n169: \\tjsonutil.MarshalFloat(p.F, stream)\\n170: \\tstream.WriteArrayEnd()\\n171: }\\n172: \\n173: // unsafeMarshalHPointJSON writes `[ts, { < histogram, see jsonutil.MarshalHistogram > } ]`.\\n174: func unsafeMarshalHPointJSON(ptr unsafe.Pointer, stream *jsoniter.Stream) {\\n175: \\tp := *((*promql.HPoint)(ptr))\\n176: \\tmarshalHPointJSON(p, stream)\\n177: }\\n178: \\n179: func marshalHPointJSON(p promql.HPoint, stream *jsoniter.Stream) {\\n180: \\tstream.WriteArrayStart()\\n181: \\tjsonutil.MarshalTimestamp(p.T, stream)\\n182: \\tstream.WriteMore()\\n183: \\tjsonutil.MarshalHistogram(p.H, stream)\\n184: \\tstream.WriteArrayEnd()\\n185: }\\n186: \\n187: // marshalExemplarJSON writes.\\n188: //\\n189: //\\t{\\n190: //\\t   labels: <labels>,\\n191: //\\t   value: \\\"<string>\\\",\\n192: //\\t   timestamp: <float>\\n193: //\\t}\\n194: func marshalExemplarJSON(ptr unsafe.Pointer, stream *jsoniter.Stream) {\\n195: \\tp := *((*exemplar.Exemplar)(ptr))\\n196: \\tstream.WriteObjectStart()\\n197: \\n198: \\t// \\\"labels\\\" key.\\n199: \\tstream.WriteObjectField(`labels`)\\n200: \\tmarshalLabelsJSON(p.Labels, stream)\\n201: \\n202: \\t// \\\"value\\\" key.\\n203: \\tstream.WriteMore()\\n204: \\tstream.WriteObjectField(`value`)\\n205: \\tjsonutil.MarshalFloat(p.Value, stream)\\n206: \\n207: \\t// \\\"timestamp\\\" key.\\n208: \\tstream.WriteMore()\\n209: \\tstream.WriteObjectField(`timestamp`)\\n210: \\tjsonutil.MarshalTimestamp(p.Ts, stream)\\n211: \\n212: \\tstream.WriteObjectEnd()\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: func unsafeMarshalLabelsJSON(ptr unsafe.Pointer, stream *jsoniter.Stream) {\\n216: \\tlabelsPtr := (*labels.Labels)(ptr)\\n217: \\tmarshalLabelsJSON(*labelsPtr, stream)\\n218: }\\n219: \\n220: func marshalLabelsJSON(lbls labels.Labels, stream *jsoniter.Stream) {\\n221: \\tstream.WriteObjectStart()\\n222: \\ti := 0\\n223: \\tlbls.Range(func(v labels.Label) {\\n224: \\t\\tif i != 0 {\\n225: \\t\\t\\tstream.WriteMore()\\n226: \\t\\t}\\n227: \\t\\ti++\\n228: \\t\\tstream.WriteString(v.Name)\\n229: \\t\\tstream.WriteRaw(`:`)\\n230: \\t\\tstream.WriteString(v.Value)\\n231: \\t})\\n232: \\tstream.WriteObjectEnd()\\n233: }\\n234: \\n235: func labelsIsEmpty(ptr unsafe.Pointer) bool {\\n236: \\tlabelsPtr := (*labels.Labels)(ptr)\\n237: \\treturn labelsPtr.IsEmpty()\\n238: }\\n239: \\n240: // Marshal a Vector as `[sample,sample,...]` - empty Vector is `[]`.\\n241: func unsafeMarshalVectorJSON(ptr unsafe.Pointer, stream *jsoniter.Stream) {\\n242: \\tv := *((*promql.Vector)(ptr))\\n243: \\tstream.WriteArrayStart()\\n244: \\tfor i, s := range v {\\n245: \\t\\tmarshalSampleJSON(s, stream)\\n246: \\t\\tif i != len(v)-1 {\\n247: \\t\\t\\tstream.WriteMore()\\n248: \\t\\t}\\n249: \\t}\\n250: \\tstream.WriteArrayEnd()\\n251: }\\n252: \\n253: // Marshal a Matrix as `[series,series,...]` - empty Matrix is `[]`.\\n254: func unsafeMarshalMatrixJSON(ptr unsafe.Pointer, stream *jsoniter.Stream) {\\n255: \\tm := *((*promql.Matrix)(ptr))\\n256: \\tstream.WriteArrayStart()\\n257: \\tfor i, s := range m {\\n258: \\t\\tmarshalSeriesJSON(s, stream)\\n259: \\t\\tif i != len(m)-1 {\\n260: \\t\\t\\tstream.WriteMore()\\n261: \\t\\t}\\n262: \\t}\\n263: \\tstream.WriteArrayEnd()\\n264: }\\n265: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/web/api/v1/json_codec.go\"}"
77. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "web/api/v1/translate_ast.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright 2024 The Prometheus Authors\\n2: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n3: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n4: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n5: //\\n6: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n7: //\\n8: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n9: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n10: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n11: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n12: // limitations under the License.\\n13: \\n14: package v1\\n15: \\n16: import (\\n17: \\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n18: \\n19: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser\\\"\\n21: )\\n22: \\n23: // Take a Go PromQL AST and translate it to an object that's nicely JSON-serializable\\n24: // for the tree view in the UI.\\n25: // TODO: Could it make sense to do this via the normal JSON marshalling methods? Maybe\\n26: // too UI-specific though.\\n27: func translateAST(node parser.Expr) any {\\n28: \\tif node == nil {\\n29: \\t\\treturn nil\\n30: \\t}\\n31: \\n32: \\tswitch n := node.(type) {\\n33: \\tcase *parser.AggregateExpr:\\n34: \\t\\treturn map[string]any{\\n35: \\t\\t\\t\\\"type\\\":     \\\"aggregation\\\",\\n36: \\t\\t\\t\\\"op\\\":       n.Op.String(),\\n37: \\t\\t\\t\\\"expr\\\":     translateAST(n.Expr),\\n38: \\t\\t\\t\\\"param\\\":    translateAST(n.Param),\\n39: \\t\\t\\t\\\"grouping\\\": sanitizeList(n.Grouping),\\n40: \\t\\t\\t\\\"without\\\":  n.Without,\\n41: \\t\\t}\\n42: \\tcase *parser.BinaryExpr:\\n43: \\t\\tvar matching any\\n44: \\t\\tif m := n.VectorMatching; m != nil {\\n45: \\t\\t\\tmatching = map[string]any{\\n46: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"card\\\":    m.Card.String(),\\n47: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"labels\\\":  sanitizeList(m.MatchingLabels),\\n48: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"on\\\":      m.On,\\n49: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"include\\\": sanitizeList(m.Include),\\n50: \\t\\t\\t}\\n51: \\t\\t}\\n52: \\n53: \\t\\treturn map[string]any{\\n54: \\t\\t\\t\\\"type\\\":     \\\"binaryExpr\\\",\\n55: \\t\\t\\t\\\"op\\\":       n.Op.String(),\\n56: \\t\\t\\t\\\"lhs\\\":      translateAST(n.LHS),\\n57: \\t\\t\\t\\\"rhs\\\":      translateAST(n.RHS),\\n58: \\t\\t\\t\\\"matching\\\": matching,\\n59: \\t\\t\\t\\\"bool\\\":     n.ReturnBool,\\n60: \\t\\t}\\n61: \\tcase *parser.Call:\\n62: \\t\\targs := []any{}\\n63: \\t\\tfor _, arg := range n.Args {\\n64: \\t\\t\\targs = append(args, translateAST(arg))\\n65: \\t\\t}\\n66: \\n67: \\t\\treturn map[string]any{\\n68: \\t\\t\\t\\\"type\\\": \\\"call\\\",\\n69: \\t\\t\\t\\\"func\\\": map[string]any{\\n70: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"name\\\":       n.Func.Name,\\n71: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"argTypes\\\":   n.Func.ArgTypes,\\n72: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"variadic\\\":   n.Func.Variadic,\\n73: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"returnType\\\": n.Func.ReturnType,\\n74: \\t\\t\\t},\\n75: \\t\\t\\t\\\"args\\\": args,\\n76: \\t\\t}\\n77: \\tcase *parser.MatrixSelector:\\n78: \\t\\tvs := n.VectorSelector.(*parser.VectorSelector)\\n79: \\t\\treturn map[string]any{\\n80: \\t\\t\\t\\\"type\\\":       \\\"matrixSelector\\\",\\n81: \\t\\t\\t\\\"name\\\":       vs.Name,\\n82: \\t\\t\\t\\\"range\\\":      n.Range.Milliseconds(),\\n83: \\t\\t\\t\\\"offset\\\":     vs.OriginalOffset.Milliseconds(),\\n84: \\t\\t\\t\\\"matchers\\\":   translateMatchers(vs.LabelMatchers),\\n85: \\t\\t\\t\\\"timestamp\\\":  vs.Timestamp,\\n86: \\t\\t\\t\\\"startOrEnd\\\": getStartOrEnd(vs.StartOrEnd),\\n87: \\t\\t}\\n88: \\tcase *parser.SubqueryExpr:\\n89: \\t\\treturn map[string]any{\\n90: \\t\\t\\t\\\"type\\\":       \\\"subquery\\\",\\n91: \\t\\t\\t\\\"expr\\\":       translateAST(n.Expr),\\n92: \\t\\t\\t\\\"range\\\":      n.Range.Milliseconds(),\\n93: \\t\\t\\t\\\"offset\\\":     n.OriginalOffset.Milliseconds(),\\n94: \\t\\t\\t\\\"step\\\":       n.Step.Milliseconds(),\\n95: \\t\\t\\t\\\"timestamp\\\":  n.Timestamp,\\n96: \\t\\t\\t\\\"startOrEnd\\\": getStartOrEnd(n.StartOrEnd),\\n97: \\t\\t}\\n98: \\tcase *parser.NumberLiteral:\\n99: \\t\\treturn map[string]string{\\n100: \\t\\t\\t\\\"type\\\": \\\"numberLiteral\\\",\\n101: \\t\\t\\t\\\"val\\\":  strconv.FormatFloat(n.Val, 'f', -1, 64),\\n102: \\t\\t}\\n103: \\tcase *parser.ParenExpr:\\n104: \\t\\treturn map[string]any{\\n105: \\t\\t\\t\\\"type\\\": \\\"parenExpr\\\",\\n106: \\t\\t\\t\\\"expr\\\": translateAST(n.Expr),\\n107: \\t\\t}\\n108: \\tcase *parser.StringLiteral:\\n109: \\t\\treturn map[string]any{\\n110: \\t\\t\\t\\\"type\\\": \\\"stringLiteral\\\",\\n111: \\t\\t\\t\\\"val\\\":  n.Val,\\n112: \\t\\t}\\n113: \\tcase *parser.UnaryExpr:\\n114: \\t\\treturn map[string]any{\\n115: \\t\\t\\t\\\"type\\\": \\\"unaryExpr\\\",\\n116: \\t\\t\\t\\\"op\\\":   n.Op.String(),\\n117: \\t\\t\\t\\\"expr\\\": translateAST(n.Expr),\\n118: \\t\\t}\\n119: \\tcase *parser.VectorSelector:\\n120: \\t\\treturn map[string]any{\\n121: \\t\\t\\t\\\"type\\\":       \\\"vectorSelector\\\",\\n122: \\t\\t\\t\\\"name\\\":       n.Name,\\n123: \\t\\t\\t\\\"offset\\\":     n.OriginalOffset.Milliseconds(),\\n124: \\t\\t\\t\\\"matchers\\\":   translateMatchers(n.LabelMatchers),\\n125: \\t\\t\\t\\\"timestamp\\\":  n.Timestamp,\\n126: \\t\\t\\t\\\"startOrEnd\\\": getStartOrEnd(n.StartOrEnd),\\n127: \\t\\t}\\n128: \\t}\\n129: \\tpanic(\\\"unsupported node type\\\")\\n130: }\\n131: \\n132: func sanitizeList(l []string) []string {\\n133: \\tif l == nil {\\n134: \\t\\treturn []string{}\\n135: \\t}\\n136: \\treturn l\\n137: }\\n138: \\n139: func translateMatchers(in []*labels.Matcher) any {\\n140: \\tout := []map[string]any{}\\n141: \\tfor _, m := range in {\\n142: \\t\\tout = append(out, map[string]any{\\n143: \\t\\t\\t\\\"name\\\":  m.Name,\\n144: \\t\\t\\t\\\"value\\\": m.Value,\\n145: \\t\\t\\t\\\"type\\\":  m.Type.String(),\\n146: \\t\\t})\\n147: \\t}\\n148: \\treturn out\\n149: }\\n150: \\n151: func getStartOrEnd(startOrEnd parser.ItemType) any {\\n152: \\tif startOrEnd == 0 {\\n153: \\t\\treturn nil\\n154: \\t}\\n155: \\n156: \\treturn startOrEnd.String()\\n157: }\\n158: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/web/api/v1/translate_ast.go\"}"

LocalhostRepresentations = []string{\\\"127.0.0.1\\\", \\\"localhost\\\", \\\"::1\\\"}\\n112: \\n113: type apiError struct {\\n114: \\ttyp errorType\\n115: \\terr error\\n116: }\\n117: \\n118: func (e *apiError) Error() string {\\n119: \\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s: %s\\\", e.typ.str, e.err)\\n120: }\\n121: \\n122: // ScrapePoolsRetriever provide the list of all scrape pools.\\n123: type ScrapePoolsRetriever interface {\\n124: \\tScrapePools() []string\\n125: }\\n126: \\n127: // TargetRetriever provides the list of active/dropped targets to scrape or not.\\n128: type TargetRetriever interface {\\n129: \\tTargetsActive() map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n130: \\tTargetsDropped() map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n131: \\tTargetsDroppedCounts() map[string]int\\n132: }\\n133: \\n134: // AlertmanagerRetriever provides a list of all/dropped AlertManager URLs.\\n135: type AlertmanagerRetriever interface {\\n136: \\tAlertmanagers() []*url.URL\\n137: \\tDroppedAlertmanagers() []*url.URL\\n138: }\\n139: \\n140: // RulesRetriever provides a list of active rules and alerts.\\n141: type RulesRetriever interface {\\n142: \\tRuleGroups() []*rules.Group\\n143: \\tAlertingRules() []*rules.AlertingRule\\n144: }\\n145: \\n146: // StatsRenderer converts engine statistics into a format suitable for the API.\\n147: type StatsRenderer func(context.Context, *stats.Statistics, string) stats.QueryStats\\n148: \\n149: // DefaultStatsRenderer is the default stats renderer for the API.\\n150: func DefaultStatsRenderer(_ context.Context, s *stats.Statistics, param string) stats.QueryStats {\\n151: \\tif param != \\\"\\\" {\\n152: \\t\\treturn stats.NewQueryStats(s)\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\treturn nil\\n155: }\\n156: \\n157: // PrometheusVersion contains build information about Prometheus.\\n158: type PrometheusVersion struct {\\n159: \\tVersion   string `json:\\\"version\\\"`\\n160: \\tRevision  string `json:\\\"revision\\\"`\\n161: \\tBranch    string \\n167: // RuntimeInfo contains runtime information about Prometheus.\\n168: type RuntimeInfo struct {\\n169: \\tStartTime           time.Time `json:\\\"startTime\\\"`\\n170: \\tCWD time.Second,\\n69: \\t\\tNoStepSubqueryIntervalFn: func(int64) int64 { return 60 * 1000 },\\n70: \\t\\tEnableAtModifier:         true,\\n71: \\t\\tEnableNegativeOffset:     true,\\n72: Output
"{\"content\":\"201: type apiFunc func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult\\n202: \\n203: // TSDBAdminStats defines the tsdb interfaces used by the v1 API for admin operations as well as statistics.\\n204: type TSDBAdminStats interface {\\n205: \\tCleanTombstones() error\\n206: \\tDelete(ctx context.Context, mint, maxt int64, ms ...*labels.Matcher) error\\n207: \\tSnapshot(dir string, withHead bool) error\\n208: \\tStats(statsByLabelName string, limit int) (*tsdb.Stats, error)\\n209: \\tWALReplayStatus() (tsdb.WALReplayStatus, error)\\n210: \\tBlockMetas() ([]tsdb.BlockMeta, error)\\n211: }\\n212: \\n213: type QueryOpts interface {\\n214: \\tEnablePerStepStats() bool\\n215: \\tLookbackDelta() time.Duration\\n216: }\\n217: \\n218: // API can register a set of endpoints in a router and handle\\n219: // them using the provided storage and query engine.\\n220: type API struct {\\n221: \\tQueryable         storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable\\n222: \\tQueryEngine       promql.QueryEngine\\n223: \\tExemplarQueryable storage.ExemplarQueryable\\n224: \\n225: \\tscrapePoolsRetriever  func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever\\n226: \\ttargetRetriever       func(context.Context) TargetRetriever\\n227: \\talertmanagerRetriever func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever\\n228: \\trulesRetriever        func(context.Context) RulesRetriever\\n229: \\tnow                   func() time.Time\\n230: \\tconfig                func() config.Config\\n231: \\tflagsMap              map[string]string\\n232: \\tready                 func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc\\n233: \\tglobalURLOptions      GlobalURLOptions\\n234: \\n235: \\tdb                  TSDBAdminStats\\n236: \\tdbDir               string\\n237: \\tenableAdmin         bool\\n238: \\tlogger              *slog.Logger\\n239: \\tCORSOrigin          *regexp.Regexp\\n240: \\tbuildInfo           *PrometheusVersion\\n241: \\truntimeInfo         func() (RuntimeInfo, error)\\n242: \\tgatherer            prometheus.Gatherer\\n243: \\tisAgent             bool\\n244: \\tstatsRenderer       StatsRenderer\\n245: \\tnotificationsGetter func() []notifications.Notification\\n246: \\tnotificationsSub    func() (<-chan notifications.Notification, func(), bool)\\n247: \\t// Allows customizing the default mapping\\n248: \\toverrideErrorCode OverrideErrorCode\\n249: \\n250: \\tremoteWriteHandler http.Handler\\n251: \\tremoteReadHandler  http.Handler\\n252: \\totlpWriteHandler   http.Handler\\n253: \\n254: \\tcodecs []Codec\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: // NewAPI returns an initialized API type.\\n258: func NewAPI(\\n259: \\tqe promql.QueryEngine,\\n260: \\tq storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable,\\n261: \\tap storage.Appendable,\\n262: \\teq storage.ExemplarQueryable,\\n263: \\tspsr func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever,\\n264: \\ttr func(context.Context) TargetRetriever,\\n265: \\tar func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever,\\n266: \\tconfigFunc func() config.Config,\\n267: \\tflagsMap map[string]string,\\n268: \\tglobalURLOptions GlobalURLOptions,\\n269: \\treadyFunc func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc,\\n270: \\tdb TSDBAdminStats,\\n271: \\tdbDir string,\\n272: \\tenableAdmin bool,\\n273: \\tlogger *slog.Logger,\\n274: \\trr func(context.Context) RulesRetriever,\\n275: \\tremoteReadSampleLimit int,\\n276: \\tremoteReadConcurrencyLimit int,\\n277: \\tremoteReadMaxBytesInFrame int,\\n278: \\tisAgent bool,\\n279: \\tcorsOrigin *regexp.Regexp,\\n280: \\truntimeInfo func() (RuntimeInfo, error),\\n281: \\tbuildInfo prometheus.Gatherer,\\n285: \\tregisterer prometheus.Registerer,\\n286: \\tstatsRenderer StatsRenderer,\\n287: \\trwEnabled bool,\\n288: \\tacceptRemoteWriteProtoMsgs []config.RemoteWriteProtoMsg,\\n289: \\totlpEnabled, otlpDeltaToCumulative, otlpNativeDeltaIngestion bool,\\n290: \\tctZeroIngestionEnabled bool,\\n291: \\tlookbackDelta time.Duration,\\n292: \\tenableTypeAndUnitLabels bool,\\n293: \\toverrideErrorCode OverrideErrorCode,\\n294: ) *API {\\n295: \\ta := &API{\\n296: \\t\\tQueryEngine:       qe,\\n297: \\t\\tQueryable:         q,\\n298: \\ta.InstallCodec(JSONCodec{})\\n328: \\n329: \\tif statsRenderer != nil {\\n330: \\t\\ta.statsRenderer = statsRenderer\\n331: \\t}\\n332: \\n333: \\tif ap == nil && (rwEnabled || otlpEnabled) {\\n334: \\t\\tpanic(\\\"remote write or otlp write enabled, but no appender passed in.\\\")\\n335: \\t}\\n336: \\n337: \\tif rwEnabled {\\n338: \\t\\ta.remoteWriteHandler = remote.NewWriteHandler(logger, registerer, ap, acceptRemoteWriteProtoMsgs, ctZeroIngestionEnabled)\\n339: \\t}\\n340: \\tif otlpEnabled {\\n341: \\t\\ta.otlpWriteHandler = remote.NewOTLPWriteHandler(logger, registerer, ap, configFunc, remote.OTLPOptions{\\n342: \\t\\t\\tConvertDelta:            otlpDeltaToCumulative,\\n343: \\t\\t\\tNativeDelta:             otlpNativeDeltaIngestion,\\n344: \\t\\t\\tLookbackDelta:           lookbackDelta,\\n345: \\t\\t\\tEnableTypeAndUnitLabels: enableTypeAndUnitLabels,\\n346: \\t\\t})\\n347: \\t}\\n348: \\n349: \\treturn a\\n350: }\\n351: \\n352: // InstallCodec adds codec to this API's available codecs.\\n353: // Codecs installed first take precedence over codecs installed later when evaluating wildcards in Accept headers.\\n354: // The first installed codec is used as a fallback when the Accept header cannot be satisfied or if there is no Accept header.\\n355: func (api *API) InstallCodec(codec Codec) {\\n356: \\tapi.codecs = append(api.codecs, codec)\\n357: }\\n358: \\n359: // ClearCodecs removes all available codecs from this API, including the default codec installed by NewAPI.\\n360: func (api *API) ClearCodecs() {\\n361: \\tapi.codecs = nil\\n362: }\\n363: \\n364: func setUnavailStatusOnTSDBNotReady(r apiFuncResult) apiFuncResult {\\n365: \\tif r.err != nil && errors.Is(r.err.err, tsdb.ErrNotReady) {\\n366: \\t\\tr.err.typ = errorUnavailable\\n367: \\t}\\n368: \\treturn r\\n369: }\\n370: \\n371: // Register the API's endpoints in the given router.\\n372: func (api *API) Register(r *route.Router) {\\n373: \\twrap := func(f apiFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\\n374: \\t\\thf := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n375: \\t\\t\\thttputil.SetCORS(w, api.CORSOrigin, r)\\n376: \\t\\t\\tresult := setUnavailStatusOnTSDBNotReady(f(r))\\n377: \\t\\t\\tif result.finalizer != nil {\\n378: \\t\\t\\t\\tdefer result.finalizer()\\n379: \\t\\t\\t}\\n380: \\t\\t\\tif result.err != nil {\\n381: \\t\\t\\t\\tapi.respondError(w, result.err, result.data)\\n382: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n383: \\t\\t\\t}\\n384: \\n385: \\t\\t\\tif result.data != nil {\\n386: \\t\\t\\t\\tapi.respond(w, r, result.data, result.warnings, r.FormValue(\\\"query\\\"))\\n387: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n388: \\t\\t\\t}\\n389: \\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\\n390: \\t\\t})\\n391: \\t\\treturn api.ready(httputil.CompressionHandler{\\n392: \\t\\t\\tHandler: hf,\\n393: \\t\\t}.ServeHTTP)\\n394: \\t}\\n395: \\n396: \\twrapAgent := func(f apiFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\\n397: \\t\\treturn wrap(func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\\n398: \\t\\t\\tif api.isAgent {\\n399: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, errors.New(\\\"unavailable with Prometheus Agent\\\")}, nil, nil}\\n400: \\t\\t\\t}\\n401: "{\"content\":\"101: type testTargetRetriever struct {\\n102: \\tactiveTargets  map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n103: \\tdroppedTargets map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n104: }\\n105: \\n106: type testTargetParams struct {\\n107: \\tIdentifier   string\\n108: \\tLabels       labels.Labels\\n109: \\ttargetLabels model.LabelSet\\n110: \\tParams       url.Values\\n111: \\tReports      []*testReport\\n112: \\tActive       bool\\n113: }\\n114: \\n115: type testReport struct {\\n116: \\tStart    time.Time\\n117: \\tDuration time.Duration\\n118: \\tError    error\\n119: }\\n120: \\n121: func newTestTargetRetriever(targetsInfo []*testTargetParams) *testTargetRetriever {\\n122: \\tvar activeTargets map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n123: \\tvar droppedTargets map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n124: \\tactiveTargets = make(map[string][]*scrape.Target)\\n125: \\tdroppedTargets = make(map[string][]*scrape.Target)\\n126: \\n127: \\tfor _, t := range targetsInfo {\\n128: \\t\\tnt := scrape.NewTarget(t.Labels, &config.ScrapeConfig{Params: t.Params}, t.targetLabels, nil)\\n129: \\n130: \\t\\tfor _, r := range t.Reports {\\n131: \\t\\t\\tnt.Report(r.Start, r.Duration, r.Error)\\n132: \\t\\t}\\n133: \\n134: \\t\\tif t.Active {\\n135: \\t\\t\\tactiveTargets[t.Identifier] = []*scrape.Target{nt}\\n136: \\t\\t} else {\\n137: \\t\\t\\tdroppedTargets[t.Identifier] = []*scrape.Target{nt}\\n138: \\t\\t}\\n139: \\t}\\n140: \\n141: \\treturn &testTargetRetriever{\\n142: \\t\\tactiveTargets:  activeTargets,\\n143: \\t\\tdroppedTargets: droppedTargets,\\n144: \\t}\\n145: }\\n146: \\n147: var scrapeStart = time.Now().Add(-11 * time.Second)\\n148: \\n149: func (t testTargetRetriever) TargetsActive() map[string][]*scrape.Target {\\n150: \\treturn t.activeTargets\\n151: }\\n152: \\n153: func (t testTargetRetriever) TargetsDropped() map[string][]*scrape.Target {\\n154: \\treturn t.droppedTargets\\n155: }\\n156: \\n157: func (t testTargetRetriever) TargetsDroppedCounts() map[string]int {\\n158: \\tr := make(map[string]int)\\n159: \\tfor k, v := range t.droppedTargets {\\n160: \\t\\tr[k] = len(v)\\n161: \\t}\\n162: \\treturn r\\n163: }\\n164: \\n165: func (t *testTargetRetriever) SetMetadataStoreForTargets(identifier string, metadata scrape.MetricMetadataStore) error {\\n166: \\ttargets, ok := t.activeTargets[identifier]\\n167: \\n168: \\tif !ok {\\n169: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"targets not found\\\")\\n170: \\t}\\n171: \\n172: \\tfor _, at := range targets {\\n173: \\t\\tat.SetMetadataStore(metadata)\\n174: \\t}\\n175: \\n176: \\treturn nil\\n177: }\\n178: \\n179: func (t *testTargetRetriever) ResetMetadataStore() {\\n180: \\tfor _, at := range t.activeTargets {\\n181: \\t\\tfor _, tt := range at {\\n182: \\t\\t\\ttt.SetMetadataStore(&testMetaStore{})\\n183: \\t\\t}\\n184: \\t}\\n185: }\\n186: \\n187: func (t *testTargetRetriever) toFactory() func(context.Context) TargetRetriever {\\n188: \\treturn func(context.Context) TargetRetriever { return t }\\n189: }\\n190: \\n191: type testAlertmanagerRetriever struct{}\\n192: \\n193: "{\"content\":\"600: \\tif step <= 0 {\\n601: \\t\\treturn invalidParamError(errors.New(\\\"zero or negative query resolution step widths are not accepted. Try a positive integer\\\"), \\\"step\\\")\\n602: \\t}\\n603: \\n604: \\t// For safety, limit the number of returned points per timeseries.\\n605: \\t// This is sufficient for 60s resolution for a week or 1h resolution for a year.\\n606: \\tif end.Sub(start)/step > 11000 {\\n607: \\t\\terr := errors.New(\\\"exceeded maximum resolution of 11,000 points per timeseries. Try decreasing the query resolution (?step=XX)\\\")\\n608: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\\n609: \\t}\\n610: \\n611: \\tctx := r.Context()\\n612: \\tif to := r.FormValue(\\\"timeout\\\"); to != \\\"\\\" {\\n613: \\t\\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\\n614: \\t\\ttimeout, err := parseDuration(to)\\n615: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n616: \\t\\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"timeout\\\")\\n617: \\t\\t}\\n618: \\n619: \\t\\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)\\n620: \\t\\tdefer cancel()\\n621: \\t}\\n622: \\n623: \\topts, err := extractQueryOpts(r)\\n624: \\tif err != nil {\\n625: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\\n626: \\t}\\n627: \\tqry, err := api.QueryEngine.NewRangeQuery(ctx, api.Queryable, opts, r.FormValue(\\\"query\\\"), start, end, step)\\n628: \\tif err != nil {\\n629: \\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"query\\\")\\n630: \\t}\\n631: \\t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\\n632: \\t// be called by the caller) or call qry.Close ourselves (which is\\n633: \\t// required in the case of a panic).\\n634: \\tdefer func() {\\n635: \\t\\tif result.finalizer == nil {\\n636: \\t\\t\\tqry.Close()\\n637: \\t\\t}\\n638: \\t}()\\n639: \\n640: \\tctx = httputil.ContextFromRequest(ctx, r)\\n641: \\n642: \\tres := qry.Exec(ctx)\\n643: \\tif res.Err != nil {\\n644: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(res.Err), res.Warnings, qry.Close}\\n645: \\t}\\n646: \\n647: \\twarnings := res.Warnings\\n648: \\tif limit > 0 {\\n649: \\t\\tvar isTruncated bool\\n650: \\n651: \\t\\tres, isTruncated = truncateResults(res, limit)\\n652: \\t\\tif isTruncated {\\n653: \\t\\t\\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\\\"results truncated due to limit\\\"))\\n654: \\t\\t}\\n655: \\t}\\n656: \\n657: \\t// Optional stats field in response if parameter \\\"stats\\\" is not empty.\\n658: \\tsr := api.statsRenderer\\n659: \\tif sr == nil {\\n660: \\t\\tsr = DefaultStatsRenderer\\n661: \\t}\\n662: \\tqs := sr(ctx, qry.Stats(), r.FormValue(\\\"stats\\\"))\\n663: \\n664: \\treturn apiFuncResult{&QueryData{\\n665: \\t\\tResultType: res.Value.Type(),\\n666: \\t\\tResult:     res.Value,\\n667: \\t\\tStats:      qs,\\n668: \\t}, nil, warnings, qry.Close}\\n669: }\\n670: \\n671: func (api *API) queryExemplars(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\\n672: \\tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \\\"start\\\", MinTime)\\n673: \\tif err != nil {\\n674: \\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"start\\\")\\n675: \\t}\\n676: \\tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \\\"end\\\", MaxTime)\\n677: \\tif err != nil {\\n678: \\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"end\\\")\\n679: \\t}\\n680: \\tif end.Before(start) {\\n681: \\t\\terr := errors.New(\\\"end timestamp must not be before start timestamp\\\")\\n682: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\\n683: \\t}\\n684: \\n685: \\texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\\\"query\\\"))\\n686: \\tif err != nil {\\n687: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\\n688: \\t}\\n689: \\n690: \\tselectors := parser.ExtractSelectors(expr)\\n691: \\tif len(selectors) < 1 {\\n692: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\\n693: \\t}\\n694: \\n695: \\tctx := r.Context()\\n696: \\teq, err := api.ExemplarQueryable.ExemplarQuerier(ctx)\\n697: \\tif err != nil {\\n698: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\\n699: \\t}\\n700: \\n701: \\tres, err := eq.Select(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end), selectors...)\\n702: \\tif err != nil {\\n703: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\\n704: \\t}\\n705: \\n706: \\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\\n707: }\\n708: \\n709: func returnAPIError(err error) *apiError {\\n710: \\tif err == nil {\\n711: \\t\\treturn nil\\n712: \\t}\\n713: \\n714: \\tvar eqc promql.ErrQueryCanceled\\n715: \\tvar eqt promql.ErrQueryTimeout\\n716: \\tvar es promql.ErrStorage\\n717: \\tswitch {\\n718: \\tcase errors.As(err, &eqc):\\n719: \\t\\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\\n720: \\tcase errors.As(err, &eqt):\\n721: \\t\\treturn &apiError{errorTimeout, err}\\n722: \\tcase errors.As(err, &es):\\n723: \\t\\treturn &apiError{errorInternal, err}\\n724: \\t}\\n725: \\n726: \\tif errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {\\n727: \\t\\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\\n728: \\t}\\n729: \\n730: \\treturn &apiError{errorExec, err}\\n731: }\\n732: \\n733: func (api *API) labelNames(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\\n734: \\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\\\"limit\\\"))\\n735: \\tif err != nil {\\n736: \\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"limit\\\")\\n737: \\t}\\n738: \\n739: \\tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \\\"start\\\", MinTime)\\n740: \\tif err != nil {\\n741: \\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"start\\\")\\n742: \\t}\\n743: \\tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \\\"end\\\", MaxTime)\\n744: \\tif err != nil {\\n745: \\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"end\\\")\\n746: \\t}\\n747: \\n748: \\tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\\\"match[]\\\"])\\n749: \\tif err != nil {\\n750: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\\n751: \\t}\\n752: \\n753: annotations.Annotations\\n766: \\t)\\n767: \\tif len(matcherSets) > 1 {\\n768: \\t\\tlabelNamesSet := make(map[string]struct{})\\n769: \\n770: \\t\\tfor _, matchers := range matcherSets {\\n771: \\t\\t}\\n781: \\n782: \\t\\t// Convert the map to an array.\\n783: \\t\\tnames = make([]string, 0, len(labelNamesSet))\\n784: \\t\\tfor key := range labelNamesSet {\\n785: \\t\\t\\tnames = nil}\\n808: }\\n809: \\n810: func (api *API) labelValues(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\\n811: \\tctx := r.Context()\\n812: \\tname := route.Param(ctx, \\\"name\\\")\\n813: \\n814: \\tif \\t}\\n849: \\t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\\n850: \\t// be called by the caller) or call q.Close ourselves (which is required\\n851: \\t// in the case of a panic).\\n852: \\tdefer func() {\\n853: \\t\\tif result.finalizer == nil {\\n854: \\t\\t\\tq.Close()\\n855: \\t\\t}\\n856: \\t}()\\n857: \\tcloser := func() {\\n858: \\t\\tq.Close()\\n859: \\t}\\n860: annotations.Annotations\\n867: \\t\\tlabelValuesSet := make(map[string]struct{})\\n868: \\t\\tfor _, matchers := range matcherSets {\\n869: \\t\\t\\tvals, callWarnings, err = q.LabelValues(ctx, name, make([]string, 0, len(labelValuesSet))\\n880: \\t\\tfor val := range labelValuesSet {\\n881: \\t\\t\\tvals = append(vals, val)\\n882: \\t\\t}\\n883: \\t} else {\\n884: \\t\\tvar matchers warnings.Add(errors.New(\\\"results truncated due to limit\\\"))\\n903: \\t}\\n904: \\n905: \\treturn apiFuncResult{vals, nil, warnings, closer}\\n906: }\\n907: \\n908: var (\\n909: \\t// MinTime is the default timestamp used for the start of optional time ranges.\\n910: \\t// Exposed to let downstream projects reference it.\\n911: \\t//\\n912: \\t// Historical note: This should just be time.Unix(math.MinInt64/1000, 0).UTC(),\\n913: \\t// but it was set to a higher value in the past due to a misunderstanding.\\n914: \\t// The value is still low enough for practical purposes, so we don't want\\n915: \\t// to change it now, avoiding confusion for importers of this variable.\\n916: \\tMinTime = time.Unix(math.MinInt64/1000+62135596801, 0).UTC()\\n917: \\n918: \\t// MaxTime is the default timestamp used for the end of optional time ranges.\\n919: \\t// Exposed to let downstream projects to reference it.\\n920: \\t//\\n921: \\t// Historical note: This should just be time.Unix(math.MaxInt64/1000, 0).UTC(),\\n922: \\t// but it was set to a lower value in the past due to a misunderstanding.\\n923: \\t// The value is still high enough for practical purposes, so we don't want\\n924: \\t// to change it now, avoiding confusion for importers of this variable.\\n925: \\tMaxTime = time.Unix(math.MaxInt64/1000-62135596801, 999999999).UTC()\\n926: \\n927: \\tminTimeFormatted = MinTime.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\\n928: \\tmaxTimeFormatted = MaxTime.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\\n929: )\\n930: \\n931: func (api *API) series(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\\n932: \\tctx := r.Context()\\n933: \\n934: \\tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\\n935: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\\\"error parsing form []storage.SeriesSet\\n986: \\t\\tfor _, mset := range matcherSets {\\n987: \\t\\t\\t// We need to sort this select results to merge (deduplicate) the series sets later.\\n988: \\t\\t\\ts := q.Select(ctx, true, hints, mset...)\\n989: \\t\\t\\tsets = append(sets, s)\\n990: \\t\\t}\\n991: \\t\\tset = storage.NewMergeSeriesSet(sets, 0, storage.ChainedSeriesMerge)\\n992: \\t} else {\\n993: }\\n1066: \\n1067: // GlobalURLOptions contains fields used for deriving the global URL for local targets.\\n1068: type GlobalURLOptions struct {\\n1069: \\tListenAddress string\\n1070: \\tHost          string\\n1071: \\tScheme        string\\n1072: }\\n1073: \\n1074: // sanitizeSplitHostPort acts like net.SplitHostPort.\\n1075: // Additionally, if there is no port in the host passed as input, we return the\\n1076: // original host, making sure that IPv6 addresses are not surrounded by square\\n1077: // brackets.\\n1078: func sanitizeSplitHostPort(input string) (string, string, error) {\\n1079: \\thost, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(input)\\n1080: \\tif err != nil && strings.HasSuffix(err.Error(), \\\"missing port in address\\\") {\\n1081: \\t\\tvar errWithPort error\\n1082: \\t\\thost, _, errWithPort = net.SplitHostPort(input + \\\":80\\\")\\n1083: \\t\\tif errWithPort == nil {\\n1084: \\t\\t\\terr = nil\\n1085: \\t\\t}\\n1086: \\t}\\n1087: \\treturn host, port, err\\n1088: }\\n1089: \\n1090: func getGlobalURL(u *url.URL, opts GlobalURLOptions) (*url.URL, error) {\\n1091: \\thost, port, err := sanitizeSplitHostPort(u.Host)\\n1092: \\tif err != nil {\\n1093: \\t\\treturn u, err\\n1094: \\t}\\n1095: \\n1096: \\tfor _, lhr := range LocalhostRepresentations {\\n1097: \\t\\tif host == lhr {\\n1098: \\t\\t\\t_, ownPort, err := net.SplitHostPort(opts.ListenAddress)\\n1099: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1100: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn u, err\\n1101: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1102: \\n1103: \\t\\t\\tif port == ownPort {\\n1104: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Only in the case where the target is on localhost and its port is\\n1105: \\t\\t\\t\\t// the same as the one we're listening on, we know for sure that\\n1106: \\t\\t\\t\\t// we're monitoring our own process and that we need to change the\\n1107: \\t\\t\\t\\t// scheme, hostname, and port to the externally reachable ones as\\n1108: \\t\\t\\t\\t// well. We shouldn't need to touch the path at all, since if a\\n1109: \\t\\t\\t\\t// path prefix is defined, the path under which we scrape ourselves\\n1110: \\t\\t\\t\\t// should already contain the prefix.\\n1111: \\t\\t\\t\\tu.Scheme = opts.Scheme\\n1112: \\t\\t\\t\\tu.Host = opts.Host\\n1113: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n1114: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Otherwise, we only know that localhost is not reachable\\n1115: \\t\\t\\t\\t// externally, so we replace only the hostname by the one in the\\n1116: \\t\\t\\t\\t// external URL. It could be the wrong hostname for the service on\\n1117: \\t\\t\\t\\t// this port, but it's still the best possible guess.\\n1118: \\t\\t\\t\\thost, _, err := sanitizeSplitHostPort(opts.Host)\\n1119: \\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1120: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn u, err\\n1121: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1122: \\t\\t\\t\\tu.Host = host\\n1123: \\t\\t\\t\\tif port != \\\"\\\" {\\n1124: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tu.Host = net.JoinHostPort(u.Host, port)\\n1125: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1126: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1127: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n1128: \\t\\t}\\n1129: \\t}\\n1130: \\n1131: \\treturn u, nil\\n1132: }\\n1133: \\n1134: func (api *API)
